Spike: CLAUDE.md inversion — thin bootstrap that assembles context from live system (services.json, board, chorus index, state files). MEMORY.md replaced by /chorus search. Goal: static instruction manual → dynamic self-describing system.

Spine portability — design principle: all spine communication via files (JSON, markdown), HTTP APIs, and CLI. No in-process coupling. Contracts not implementations. Enables language/stack changes without spine rewrite.
Incremental Fuseki sync — track sync state, only PUT deltas on restart. Eliminates 5,800+ file reload on every container start. Root cause of health timeout during fullSyncAll.
sessions.db isolation — separate SQLite per environment or move sessions to non-file store. Root cause of recurring corruption: E2E and running app share same SQLite with no locking.
Background sync worker — decouple Fuseki sync from app startup. Health gate passes immediately, sync runs in background. Restart becomes near-instant.

tasks.description:
## What
Document and operationalize Borg's two-phase delivery pattern as a repeatable methodology.

## Phase 1: Iterate with client (spike/discovery)
- Hand drawings → HTML prototypes, rapidly iterated
- Refine UI + OWL + value stream + domains as we go
- 2-3 cycles
- Identify integrations, security, and harvest considerations along the way
- Output: validated prototype that captures intent

## Phase 2: Build from 1 (commitment)
- The iteration output becomes the spec — estimable, buildable
- Code + tests + UX + tickets + logs + monitoring + security + documentation
- Full blast radius coverage (DEC-072)

## Why
This is Jeff's natural working pattern — validated by the Athena experience at Staples. Phase 1 is discovery through making. Phase 2 is commitment informed by understanding. Maps to spike/discovery/commitment taxonomy.

## Athena lesson
Process weight kills adoption. Phase 1 must stay lightweight — sketches, conversation, rapid cycles. Phase 2 inherits rigor from the system, not from human paperwork.

## AC
- [ ] Pattern documented in About library
- [ ] Card lifecycle integrates the two phases (spike → discovery → commitment maps to Phase 1 → Phase 2)
- [ ] Borg can execute Phase 1 autonomously given hand drawings as input

## Tags
[medium] [discovery] chorus methodology

tasks.description:
## What
Two issues with Kade's demo pattern:
1. **No invitation** — Kade demos directly to Jeff without notifying Wren or Silas. Breaks DEC-048 proving gate (Wren accepts, not the builder). Wren only learns about acceptance when Jeff relays it.
2. **Style drift** — demos are verbose/procedural instead of visual-first (show it, wait for Jeff, Wren accepts).

## AC
- Kade's /demo skill sends a nudge to Wren (and Silas if infra-touching) BEFORE presenting to Jeff
- Demo output matches team pattern: frame → /look evidence → wait for Jeff → Wren accepts
- No verbose narration — visual evidence first, words second
- Verify nudge delivery so Wren can observe the demo live

## Context
Jeff observed both gaps during #1197 acceptance. Compare to Silas #537 demo (nudged Wren, followed tuned pattern). The demo skill fragment in Kade's CLAUDE.md needs to enforce the routing, not just the format.

2,279 contacts with shallow labels. Chandra Bommas is a Colleague but also Jeffs favorite manager. The category doesnt carry that. As Apple Contacts (#1269) and other sources fill in, the relationship layer needs to express meaning, not just type.

## AC

- [ ] Design doc: what relationship depth means for Gathering (not just more fields)

## Design
Three dimensions: howWeMet, sharedContext, relationshipState — plus story links. See design doc in product-manager/.

tasks.description:
## What

Live dashboard page showing the taxonomy map assessed against the current codebase. For each domain × value stream stage, Borg classifies:

- **Build** — domain defined in taxonomy but no page/handler exists yet
- **Refactor** — page exists but in the wrong domain, wrong nav position, or missing boundaries
- **Rewrite** — page exists but was built ahead of the map and can't be salvaged by moving

Color-coded grid: green (conforms), yellow (refactor), red (rewrite), grey (build needed).

## Why

DEC-086 (domain-first development) establishes the taxonomy as the governing artifact. This dashboard is the enforcement surface — makes model-vs-reality visible at a glance.

## AC

- [ ] Page at a Product Vision nav location (exact placement TBD with v5 nav)
- [ ] Domain × value stream grid with color-coded status
- [ ] Each cell links to the relevant page or card
- [ ] Assessment logic reads from taxonomy + live routes/handlers
- [ ] Three modes labeled: build, refactor, rewrite

Files: views/borg-assessment.ejs, src/handlers/borg.handler.ts, src/app.ts (route), data/about/PRODUCT_TAXONOMY.md (read-only)
Domains: self

tasks.description:
## What

The If-Touched doc scan in session close-out isn't working. Docs in data/about/ are stale — decisions made, domains renamed, value streams redefined, but the docs still reflect old state. The close-out checklist says "scan for staleness" but roles skip it or do it superficially.

## Problem

- PRODUCT_VISION.md, GLOSSARY.md, SITE_MAP.md, API.md — likely stale after weeks of taxonomy/nav/domain changes
- Close-out If-Touched section lists docs to check but there's no enforcement or freshness signal
- No way to see at a glance which docs are current vs stale
- Roles have no incentive to update docs they didn't create

## AC

- [ ] Audit all docs in data/about/ — mark each as current, stale, or obsolete with evidence
- [ ] Add last-verified date to doc frontmatter or a freshness manifest
- [ ] Borg churn lens (#1286) should flag docs whose domain has changed but the doc hasn't
- [ ] Propose enforcement mechanism — gate, nudge, or dashboard visibility

## Context

Jeff noticed docs are "somewhat to very stale." This is the doc-drift gate (#515) that was never enforced. DEC-086 (domain-first) makes this worse — the map changed but the docs didn't follow. Third Borg lens: doc freshness.

tasks.title: Invert search hierarchy — hook queries Chorus transparently before filesystem search, injects results
tasks.description:
## What

IoC pattern: don't ask roles to query Chorus first, have the hook do it. On Grep/Glob intercept, hook runs chorus-query.sh with the search term, prepends relevant hits to the result. Role gets enriched context without changing behavior. The principle is satisfied by infrastructure, not discipline. Ref: DEC-074, fitness function data shows 260 flags with flat trend — nudging isn't working.

## AC

- [ ] PreToolUse hook on Grep and/or Glob intercepts search terms
- [ ] Hook runs chorus-query.sh (or equivalent) with the intercepted term
- [ ] Relevant Chorus hits are prepended to or injected alongside tool results
- [ ] Roles receive enriched context without changing their search behavior
- [ ] DEC-074 search hierarchy is enforced by infrastructure, not role discipline
- [ ] Hook is transparent — no user-visible prompt or approval step for the Chorus query
- [ ] Performance: hook adds <2s latency to search operations
- [ ] Fallback: if Chorus is down or returns empty, original search proceeds unmodified

tasks.title: Add personal gate scorecard to werk-init.sh session boot — show role their own fitness data

## What

Each role sees their own gate compliance data — how often they hit Chorus before grep, how often they asked permission when they didn't need to, autonomy score, etc. Makes the boot sequence a mirror, not just a status dump.

## AC

- [ ] werk-init.sh boot output includes a per-role fitness section
- [ ] Scorecard shows at minimum: search hierarchy compliance (DEC-074), autonomy gate compliance (DEC-058), permission-seeking rate
- [ ] Data sourced from spine events and/or chorus-log (not manual tracking)
- [ ] Scores cover recent window (last 3-5 sessions, not all-time)
- [ ] Format is compact — 3-5 lines max, no walls of stats
- [ ] Role sees only their own data (not other roles' scores)
- [ ] Scorecard is informational — no gate, no block, just visibility

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Research 3+ garden mapping tools/approaches (analog and digital)

tasks.title: Extend doc-drift.conf to cover data/about/ docs — map nav-tree, ontology, routes to their docs
tasks.description:
## What

doc-drift.conf only maps team repo docs (team-architecture.md, system-architecture.md). The 65 docs in data/about/ — including SITE_MAP, GLOSSARY, API, INFRASTRUCTURE — have no drift detection. This is why SITE_MAP drifted for weeks after nav-tree changed without being flagged.

## Two Gaps

1. **Missing mappings**: data/about/ docs not in conf. Need at minimum:
   - config/nav-tree.json → data/about/SITE_MAP.md
   - src/ontology/ → data/about/GLOSSARY.md, data/about/ONTOLOGY_STATUS.md
   - src/routes/ → data/about/API.md
   - docker-compose.yml → data/about/INFRASTRUCTURE.md
   - src/handlers/ → data/about/USER_CAPABILITIES.md

2. **Time-based freshness**: current gate only checks git diff within a session. Docs that drift across multiple sessions (the real problem) aren't caught. Consider a staleness check against FRESHNESS_MANIFEST.md or file mtime vs domain activity.

## AC

- [ ] doc-drift.conf includes mappings for key data/about/ docs (SITE_MAP, API, GLOSSARY, INFRASTRUCTURE, ONTOLOGY_STATUS at minimum)
- [ ] werk-init.sh --close flags data/about/ docs when their source files changed
- [ ] Propose or spike a cross-session freshness check (mtime or manifest-based)
- [ ] FRESHNESS_MANIFEST.md referenced as canonical staleness source

tasks.title: Integrate Google Photos harvest — import photo metadata into Fuseki knowledge graph
tasks.description:
## What

Harvest Google Photos library metadata into the knowledge graph. Follow the established harvest pattern: adapter → ontology mapper → writer → pod + Fuseki index. Photos stay in Google — only metadata (title, date, location, album, faces) enters the graph.

## Context

- Photos ontology exists (v0.9.0): Photo, PhotoAlbum, PhotoLocation, FaceDetection
- Google Photos Picker API already used for Property photo import (GooglePhotosService exists)
- Apple Photos harvest in progress (#1261, #1304) — this is the second source
- Scale: potentially 200K+ photos. Must estimate disk impact per C7.

## AC

- [ ] Google Photos API adapter fetches album and photo metadata (title, date, location, dimensions, album membership)
- [ ] Metadata mapped to existing Photos ontology (Photo, PhotoAlbum, PhotoLocation)
- [ ] Turtle files written to pod under photos/ container
- [ ] Fuseki indexed via standard sync pipeline

tasks.title: Harvest Google Docs and Sheets — import document metadata into Fuseki knowledge graph
tasks.description:
## What

Harvest Google Docs and Google Sheets metadata into the knowledge graph. Documents stay in Google — metadata (title, owner, created/modified dates, sharing status, folder structure) enters the graph. Follows the established harvest pattern.

## Context

- No existing Google Docs/Sheets ontology — needs domain design (shared with #1331 iCloud docs)
- Google Drive API provides metadata access (OAuth2, same auth pattern as Google Photos)
- Scale: likely hundreds to low thousands of documents, not the 200K scale of photos
- These are Jeff's working documents — business plans, spreadsheets, notes. High personal relevance.
- Part of a unified document harvest vision: Google + iCloud + local → single searchable index across both Macs

## AC

- [ ] Google Drive API adapter fetches Docs and Sheets metadata (title, type, created, modified, owner, folder path, sharing status)
- [ ] New document ontology classes designed (Document, Spreadsheet, Folder or similar) — shared with #1331
- [ ] Metadata mapped to ontology and written to pod
- [ ] Fuseki indexed via standard sync pipeline
- [ ] Harvest manifest entry created for Google Docs/Sheets source
- [ ] Folder hierarchy preserved as relationships
- [ ] Documents searchable via /search and viewable in a Documents collection page
- [ ] Incremental sync — only fetch documents modified since last harvest

tasks.title: Harvest iCloud documents — import document metadata into Fuseki knowledge graph
tasks.description:
## What

Harvest iCloud Drive / iCloud Documents metadata into the knowledge graph. Files stay in iCloud — metadata (filename, type, folder, dates, size) enters the graph. Follows the established harvest pattern.

## Context

- No existing iCloud harvester — needs research on access method
- iCloud Drive files are synced to ~/Library/Mobile Documents/ on macOS
- Unlike Google APIs, there's no official iCloud API — harvester will likely use filesystem scan of the local sync folder
- Scale: depends on what Jeff stores in iCloud. Likely moderate.
- Part of a unified document harvest vision: Google + iCloud + local → single searchable index across both Macs
- Both Macs have iCloud sync — Library and Bedroom. Harvest once, search everywhere.

## AC

- [ ] Research access method — filesystem scan of ~/Library/Mobile Documents/ vs CloudKit API vs other
- [ ] Adapter extracts document metadata (filename, type, path, dates, size, iCloud container/app)
- [ ] Metadata mapped to document ontology (shared with #1330 Google Docs)
- [ ] Turtle files written to pod
- [ ] Fuseki indexed via standard sync pipeline
- [ ] Harvest manifest entry created for iCloud source
- [ ] Documents searchable via /search and viewable in a Documents collection page
- [ ] Cross-machine: index covers documents synced to both Library and Bedroom Macs
- [ ] Incremental sync — only re-scan changed files

tasks.title: Automate doc-drift sweeps — continuous garbage collection for constraint violations and stale docs
Fix 5 broken back-links — stale parents from nav-tree restructure (#1294)
tasks.description:
Back-link audit from site walkthrough (#1265). ~24 fixes across views. All bind-mounted — no deploy.

## Rules
- L1 pages → `← Home` (mind map)
- L2 pages → `← [L1 Parent Name]`
- All use `.back-link` class, `←` prefix, top of page
- No inline styles, no `.back-btn`, no arrow-only links

## Fixes by category

### Chorus pages — add missing back-links (← Chorus)
cost.ejs, decisions.ejs, flow.ejs, werk.ejs, gathering-chorus-system-graph.ejs, flow-map.html, session-replay.ejs, dashboard.ejs

### Chorus pages — fix wrong target
- hooks.ejs: `/` → `/chorus`
- fitness-functions.ejs: `/` → `/chorus`
- admin-access.ejs: `/dashboard` → `/chorus`
- admin-users.ejs: `/dashboard` → `/chorus`

### Broken route
- admin-harvest-photos.ejs: `/admin` → `/photos`

### Ontology views — fix parent (← Self, not ← Model + Data)
books.ejs, chorus.ejs, model-data.ejs, music.ejs, notes.ejs, photos.ejs, property.ejs, self.ejs
Also: sexuality.ejs — add missing back-link to `/self`

### UX consistency
- collection-network.ejs: inline styles → `.back-link` class
- collection-people.ejs: inline styles → `.back-link` class
- book-upload.ejs: `.back-btn` arrow-only → `.back-link` `← Books`
- self.ejs: `← Mind Map` → `← Home` (consistent naming)

## AC
- Every page with a back-link uses `.back-link` class with `←` prefix
- L1 → Home, L2 → L1 parent, no exceptions
- All hrefs resolve to live routes
- No inline-styled navigation elements
- No deploy needed (views are bind-mounted)

Three personal analytics streams siloed across pages nobody visits independently. Correlation is the value — e.g., evening posture degradation alongside voice energy and attention patterns.

## AC

- /voice-analytics and /attention-analytics redirect to /jeff

tasks.title: Fix tool-telemetry false positives — skip all board-ts commands, not just list/view/audit
tasks.description:
tool-telemetry.sh PostToolUse:Bash hook scans output for error keywords. Skip list (line 103) filters board-ts list/view/audit but misses add/update/done. When card titles or descriptions contain "error" (e.g., auto-error cards), the hook logs false positives.
    
    ## Fix
    Change line 105 from:
      *board-ts*list*|*board-ts*view*|*board-ts*audit*)
    To:
      *board-ts*)
    
    All board-ts output is safe — it's card metadata, not command errors.

    - board-ts add/update/done no longer trigger PostToolUse hook errors
    - Existing skip filters for list/view/audit still work (subsumed by broader match)
  Domains:  chunk:ops, sequence:hardening

## Files
- messages/scripts/tool-telemetry.sh

tasks.title: Add nightly Fuseki perf baseline — cron job with daily SPARQL timing tests for morning review
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

1. Shell script at `shared-observability/scripts/fuseki-perf-baseline.sh` runs a set of representative SPARQL queries against Fuseki and logs timing results
2. Queries cover: simple lookup, type aggregation, cross-graph join, full-text search, and count queries
3. Results written to `shared-observability/perf/fuseki-baseline-<date>.json` with query name, duration_ms, result count
4. LaunchAgent or cron job runs the script nightly (2am Boston)
5. Morning review: script output is readable in under 30 seconds — table format, trend vs prior day
6. No test execution during build — cron only, first run is overnight

tasks.title: Fix TTL sync to detect new files outside manifest — canSkipSync bypasses harvest-written files
tasks.description:
Fix TTL sync to detect new files outside manifest — canSkipSync bypasses harvest-written files

## Files
- src/services/document-pod.service.ts
- src/services/pod-write.service.ts

## Problem
canSkipSync() checks the manifest timestamp to decide if TTL files need re-reading. But harvest writes TTL files directly — they don't go through the manifest. So new harvest data sits on disk unindexed until the next page load or manual cache invalidation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] canSkipSync detects TTL files newer than the manifest timestamp
- [ ] After a harvest completes, the next page load picks up new data without restart
- [ ] Existing manifest-based skip still works for non-harvest TTL changes
- [ ] No performance regression on startup (index warm stays under 60s)

Three roles boot fresh every session. Structural knowledge about what exists is scattered across code, Chorus, and Jeff's head. Jeff becomes the carrier — that's failure demand. This eliminates it.

tasks.title: Improve /documents UX — hide platform noise, group by name/year, add top-level summary
tasks.description:
/documents dumps 43K folders in a flat grid with no meaningful organization. Platform detritus (.svn, Cache, plugins, hex-shard folders) drowns the signal.

## What
1. **Hide noise by default** — filter .svn, Cache, plugins, .Picasa3Temp, single-hex-char folders, img, https. Add "Show all" toggle.
2. **Group by type** — Named folders (human-meaningful) and By Year (2006-2019 cluster). Document counts per group.
3. **Top-level summary** — before the grid: top 10 biggest folders, most recent by harvest date, breakdown of personal content vs platform detritus vs app artifacts.
4. **Split "other" content type** — expand MIME_TYPE_MAP to classify image/* → photo, video/* → video, audio/* → audio, application/zip → archive. "Other" at 448K (91%) is unusable as a single bucket. Data already in Fuseki via jb:mimeType.
5. **Pagination** — paginate document and folder lists. 570K docs cannot render in one page load.

## AC
- Default view shows only human-meaningful folders
- Year folders cluster together as a timeline section
- Summary section above the grid with top folders and content breakdown

- Content type filter chips include photo, video, audio, archive (not just Google Workspace types)

tasks.title: Root-cause SMS placeholder seed generation — photos create empty text seeds despite 15+ prior fixes
tasks.description:
Every SMS photo Jeff sends generates two placeholder text seeds ("Team discussion topic", "Product idea for Wren") with no real content. These get silently deleted by /cs, but the pattern has been "fixed" 15-20 times in the past month and keeps recurring.

## Root Cause Investigation
The fix keeps not sticking because it is likely applied at the wrong layer — triage logic gets patched, but the SMS capture pipeline itself generates placeholder text before triage sees it. Need to trace the full path: SMS arrival → attachment detection → seed file creation → triage routing.

## AC
- Identify where in the pipeline placeholder text seeds are generated for image-only SMS
- Fix at the source, not at triage
- Photos arrive as image seeds (or no text seed at all), not as "Product idea for Wren"
- Verify fix holds across 5+ photo sends without regression

tasks.title: Analyze Drive media overlap with Photos/Music domains — read-only census before routing decisions
tasks.description:
## Goal
Read-only census: show Jeff the overlap between Drive's 316K media files and existing Photos/Music domains. No mutations, no routing — just analysis.

## What gets built
- `src/services/drive-analysis.service.ts` — analysis service
- `src/handlers/drive-analysis.handler.ts` — mounts at `/admin/drive-analysis` (HTML) + `/api/drive-analysis` (JSON)
- `views/admin-drive-analysis.ejs` — report page

## How it works
1. Call `DocumentPodService.getDocumentIndex()` — already builds in-memory index with contentCategory
2. Group by contentCategory (photo/video/music/other/doc/etc)
3. Pull photo filenames from Photos graph via SPARQL, music titles from Music graph
4. In-memory Set intersection to count filename overlaps
5. Cache results (5 min TTL, same pattern as DocumentPodService)

Memory: ~280K filenames at ~50 bytes = ~14MB. Photos similar. ~30MB total. Fine on 16GB M1.

## Output
Summary page showing:
- Drive media by type (photo: 280K, video: 29K, music: 6K)
- Filename overlap with Photos domain: X matches / Y unique to Drive
- Filename overlap with Music domain: X matches / Y unique to Drive
- "Truly new" count per category

## Reuse (key files)
- `DocumentPodService.getDocumentIndex()` — `document-pod.service.ts`
- `contentCategoryFromMime()` — `document-pod.service.ts:43`
- Handler pattern — `document.handler.ts`
- SPARQL graph URIs — `sparql-constants.ts`

## Acceptance criteria
1. `/admin/drive-analysis` loads and shows media breakdown by type
2. Overlap counts shown for Photos and Music domains
3. "Truly new" count per category visible
4. Counts cross-check against content type chips on `/documents`
5. No mutations to any graph — read-only

## Sequencing
Phase 1 of 4. Pick up after harvest freeze lifts. Phase 2 (confidence-scored matching) carded separately after Jeff reviews these numbers.

tasks.title: Suppress test-triggered harvest notifications — add test flag to /harvest payload

tasks.title: Serve document content via API — roles can read routed docs without Jeff as relay
tasks.description:
## Problem
When Jeff routes a document to a role via /documents, the role gets metadata (title, link, type) but can't read the content. Jeff has to download, copy-paste, or describe it. He's man-in-the-middle for his own data.

## What this enables
Jeff nudges a PDF/doc/text to Wren → Wren reads it directly. No relay steps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] `/api/documents/<slug>/content` returns extracted text for supported types
- [x] Google Docs/Sheets/Slides → export as text via Drive API
- [x] Plain text/markdown/JSON → returned raw
- [x] PDF → text extraction via pdf-parse
- [x] Routed brief includes a local content URL the role can fetch
- [x] Works from inside Docker (bind mount paths for briefs)
- [x] Local content cache — Drive export once, serve from disk after
- [x] 20s timeout on Drive export — prevents server hang

## Out of scope (carded separately)
- #1369: Drive API binary download hangs in Docker (token refresh issue)
- #1370: PDF image-to-text extraction (needs sidecar or vision model)

## Scope
Content extraction for text-based documents. The nudge brief gets a fetchable URL. PDF text extraction works; image-only PDF pages flagged but not OCR'd (see #1370).

tasks.title: Multi-select document routing — pick 1..N docs, send as batch to a role
tasks.description:
Multi-select document routing — pick 1..N docs, send as batch to a role

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can select multiple documents via checkboxes in the document table view
- [ ] "Send to..." button shows selected count and target role picker
- [ ] Batch route creates one brief per batch (not per doc) with all titles and links

tasks.title: Fix Drive API binary download hanging in Docker — token refresh or DNS
tasks.description:
## Problem
Drive API binary download (files.get?alt=media) hangs inside Docker container. OAuth token refresh via google-auth-library calls Google's OAuth server with no timeout — under I/O contention, this blocks indefinitely and kills the content API.

## AC
- [ ] getAccessToken() has a timeout (10s) so token refresh can't hang forever
- [ ] exportFile() returns 502 with clear error on timeout instead of hanging
- [ ] Content API stays responsive even when Drive is unreachable
- [ ] Health endpoint never blocked by Drive operations

tasks.title: PDF image-to-text extraction — sidecar or vision model approach

## AC
- [ ] Image-only PDF pages return extracted text via OCR or image description
- [ ] Works inside node:22-alpine Docker container
- [ ] No server hang or OOM — extraction must be bounded in time and memory
- [ ] Falls back gracefully if extraction fails (returns image metadata, not blank)

tasks.title: Investigate deploy time regression — 24s target vs 76-94s actual, blocking flow
tasks.description:
## Problem
Deploy times regressed from ~24s target to 76-94s actual. Blocking Kade's demo flow — 4+ minute deploys during I/O contention.

## Investigation scope
- Profile Docker build layers (which step is slow?)
- Check if new deps (tesseract.js ~60MB, unpdf) invalidate layer cache
- Measure deploy time with and without background I/O (98GB file copy)
- Identify whether restart vs deploy distinction is clear in app-state.sh

## AC
- [ ] Root cause identified with timing data per build layer
- [ ] Recommendation: fix, workaround, or 'environmental — wait for copy to finish'
- [ ] If fixable: deploy time back under 60s on clean system
- [ ] Document findings in card comment

tasks.title: Pre-flight card quality at /pull and board-ts add — AC, chunk, sequence required before WIP, not at the gate error
tasks.description:
Two parts:

1. **board-ts add** — require AC draft + chunk at creation time. No skeleton cards.
2. **/pull skill** — pre-flight checklist before attempting move. If AC missing, draft from title. If chunk missing, infer from domain. If sequence missing, flag. Fix what you can, ask for what you can't. Never hit the gate error blind.

Also investigate: board-ts move has been reported to strip AC from descriptions. If true, that's the root cause of 'missing AC' false alarms — the system writes AC, move strips it, gate rejects. Jeff sees roles blaming him for AC he already wrote.

## Files
- src/app.ts (placeholder — actual files are team repo: messages/scripts/board-ts, .claude/skills/pull/SKILL.md)

## AC
- board-ts add rejects cards without AC and chunk (error message tells you what's missing)
- /pull pre-flight checks AC, chunk, sequence before attempting board-ts move
- /pull auto-drafts AC from title if missing, auto-infers chunk from domain labels
- Verify board-ts move preserves description (AC) through status transitions
- Zero gate errors visible to Jeff for fields that should have been caught earlier

tasks.title: Card quality check
tasks.description:

- /pull on a card with no AC drafts AC automatically and reports what it wrote

tasks.title: Migrate Navidrome + Vikunja + node-exporter — Phase 1 Docker→Native
tasks.description:
## AC
- Each service evaluated individually: container vs LaunchAgent vs bare process. Decision documented with rationale.
- Navidrome: already on Bedroom as Docker — reassess whether LaunchAgent (brew install) is better fit
- WordPress + MySQL: evaluate Docker vs native install on Bedroom (32GB, no other containers)
- Library docker-compose updated (migrated services removed or profiled)
- Docker VM on Library drops by ~1.4GB (verify with docker stats)
- Prometheus scrape targets and blackbox probes updated
- All services accessible from Library after migration

## Decision Gate (mandatory per service)
Ask: What problem does Docker solve for this service on this machine?
- Complex dependencies (Fuseki, MySQL) → container justified
- Simple runtime (Node app, Go binary) → LaunchAgent likely better
- Document the choice. The Parnas principle: hide deployment method behind the service interface.

## Approach
1. Navidrome — already on Bedroom Docker. Evaluate if LaunchAgent via brew is simpler.
2. WordPress + MySQL — evaluate both approaches. MySQL has real dependency complexity. WordPress less so.
3. Update monitoring for each move.

Files: jeff-bridwell-personal-site/docker-compose.yml, wordpress-blog/docker-compose.yml, shared-observability/config/prometheus/prometheus.yml

tasks.title: Standardize disk and memory reporting — use tools that match what Jeff sees in Finder and Activity Monitor
tasks.description:
## AC
- Disk: use diskutil info / or osascript to get APFS available+purgeable, not df (which excludes purgeable)
- Memory: use memory_pressure or Activity Monitor-equivalent, not vm_stat raw pages
- du results on iCloud-managed dirs must be flagged as potentially misleading (stubs report full size)
- Write a small script (e.g. system-health.sh) that reports disk, memory, swap in one call using the correct tools
- All roles use this script instead of ad-hoc df/du/vm_stat

## Why
df and du misreport on APFS with purgeable space and iCloud stubs. Yesterday: Finder showed 364GB free, df showed 72GB. Today: Finder showed 585GB, df showed 15GB. Both times caused false alarm and wasted Jeff's attention.

tasks.title: Migrate Express app from Docker to LaunchAgent — eliminate container overhead and deploy cycling

## Context
Docker IS the root cause of #1380. Every deploy cycles the container (30-90s downtime). Docker VM adds 680MB overhead. Health checks route through Docker networking. Watchdog (#1380) is the bandaid; this is the structural fix. Kade confirmed 3 files need cleanup, native builds are simpler (no Alpine cross-compile). Heavy lift is app-state.sh rewrite — Silas vertical.

- Data migrated: Loki WAL, Prometheus TSDB, Grafana SQLite
- shared-observability/docker-compose.yml reduced to 0 services
- service-registry.conf updated for all 7
- 24h parallel run before each decommission
- All Prometheus targets green, Grafana dashboards flowing, Loki ingesting

## Context
Phase 2 of Docker → Native migration plan. Phase 1 done (#1375): node-exporter removed, Navidrome + Vikunja migrated. Library Docker VM drops from ~7GB to ~4GB after this phase. Free RAM goes from ~3GB to ~5-7GB.

## Blast radius

- shared-observability/config/promtail/promtail-config.yaml (update log sources)

- 7 new LaunchAgent plist files
- service-registry.conf

tasks.title: Add critical-path flow checks to smoke-check.sh — beyond page loads to click-and-verify on 5-10 key user flows
tasks.description:
## AC
- Walk every nav link deterministically — extract all hrefs from nav tree, hit each one, verify non-error response
- External links (Grafana dashboards, etc.) verified separately with expected status
- Report: total links, pass count, fail count, each failure with URL + status
- Runs as part of smoke-check.sh, not a separate script
- Demo brief smoke status includes nav coverage (e.g. '47/47 nav links resolve')

## Context
The nav tree IS the product surface. If a link in the nav doesn't resolve, the product is broken — regardless of how many Prometheus targets are green. After the Docker→LaunchAgent migration, hardcoded URLs (Grafana dashboard links, external service URLs) broke silently. A deterministic nav walk catches this class of issue automatically.

Current smoke check answers 'does it crash?' not 'does it work?' This closes that gap by treating the navbar as the enumerable, testable contract with the user.

tasks.title: Memory Architecture — conceptual model for capture pipeline + retrieval orchestration across personal and team memory
tasks.description:
## AC
- Conceptual architecture document rendered as HTML and committed to About docs
- Covers: Jeff's 6 personal memory layers, team's 5 memory layers, the intersection
- Capture pipeline: signal → capture → persist → integrate (with durability tiers)
- Retrieval orchestration: trigger → search → synthesize → deliver (active, not passive)
- Gaps identified: missing edges, no durable event stream, sensory has no home, capture depends on conversation
- Pressure tested: what we have vs what we need to build
- Deployed to data/about/MEMORY_ARCHITECTURE.html

## Context
Emerged from today's session — Ginsburg story seed failure revealed memory fragility, Docker migration revealed log rotation destroying team memory, Jeff asked for deep work on the conceptual layers. This is the product vision for how memory should work in Gathering/Chorus.

tasks.title: Startup seed reconciliation — scan for seeds that arrived during app downtime, emit missing spine events

- No duplicate events if seed was already announced

## Context
2026-03-14: lost 2 seeds during Docker migration restarts. Twilio retries saved files to disk but spine events never fired. Chorus couldn't see them. Memory Architecture #1399 identified this as a capture pipeline gap.

tasks.title: Self ontology extension — Episode, Pattern, Connection, Value classes for experience graph
tasks.description:
## AC
- Ontology TTL defines: jb:Episode, jb:Pattern, jb:Connection, jb:Value, jb:Experience
- Episode links to: people (jb:involvedPerson), values (jb:expressesValue), other episodes (jb:relatedEpisode)
- Pattern links to: decisions (jb:informedDecision), episodes (jb:derivedFromEpisode)
- Connection links to: two memories with a predicate (jb:connectionType — e.g. shared_hardship, same_shape, led_to)
- Schema reviewed by Silas before merge
- Loaded into Fuseki as named graph

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399: Fuseki has 22.7M triples about things (photos, music, docs). No classes for experiences, values, or the connections between them. This is the schema layer that enables the aspiration: knowledge graph of lived experience.

tasks.title: Story harvester — parse stories.md into RDF with person, value, and episode edges
tasks.description:
## AC
- Harvester reads stories.md, parses each story entry
- Creates jb:Episode instance per story with: date, content, source session
- Extracts mentioned people → jb:involvedPerson edges
- Extracts expressed values → jb:expressesValue edges (loyalty, independence, honesty, etc.)
- Cross-links related stories → jb:relatedEpisode edges
- Writes TTL to pods, indexes to Fuseki
- Harvest manifest entry for stories source
- Incremental: only processes new/changed stories

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399: stories.md has 51 stories as flat text. No machine-readable structure, no graph edges. Stories are the richest memory type — they carry episodes, relationships, values, and craft patterns. Making them RDF unlocks the connection layer.

## Dependencies
- #1401 Self ontology extension must land first (need Episode, Value classes)

tasks.title: Edge persistence — write connections discovered in conversation back to Fuseki
tasks.description:
## AC
- When a role discovers a connection between memories (story↔person, episode↔value, pattern↔decision), emit a spine event with the edge data
- A listener writes the edge to Fuseki as an RDF triple
- Edges include: subject, predicate, object, discoveredBy, discoveredAt, sessionContext
- Retrievable via SPARQL: 'what connects Ginsburg to Julian?'
- Does NOT require manual annotation — edges are inferred from conversation context

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 + Kade's feedback: 'We find edges but don't write them.' Connections discovered in conversation evaporate when the session ends. This closes the loop: discover → persist → retrieve.

## Dependencies
- #1401 Self ontology extension (need Connection class)
- #1402 Story harvester (need story nodes to connect)

tasks.title: Memory pipeline completeness monitor — harvest-style freshness check for team memory capture
tasks.description:
## AC
- Daily check: did today's sessions produce story captures? Decision captures? Seed triages?
- Report gaps: 'Wren session had 3 stories shared, 1 captured, 2 missed'
- Alert if memory pipeline is stale (no captures for 48h during active sessions)
- Dashboard panel in Home Cloud Weather or Chorus dashboard

## Context
Silas's feedback on #1399: 'We monitor services but not did today's stories get captured?' A completeness check for memory — like harvest freshness but for the memory pipeline. Catches gaps like lost seeds before they compound.

tasks.title: Review transposition rate data after 24h — verify signal quality, check correlation with session patterns
Define garden + property ontology — zones, beds, plants, seasons nested under property/land/structure hierarchy
tasks.description:
## AC
- Ontology TTL defines: jb:Property, jb:Land, jb:Structure, jb:Garden, jb:Zone, jb:Bed, jb:Plant, jb:Season, jb:Planting
- Hierarchy: Property → Land → Garden → Zone → Bed → Planting (plant + season)
- Property also contains: Structure (house, garage, fence), Land aspects (sun exposure, drainage, soil type)
- Garden zones from Jeff's hand-drawn map: South Front, South Center, South Fence, East Front, East, House Edges, West Gutter, West Fence, North Front, Fence Edge, North Center
- Connects to existing ontologies: People (who gardens), Photos (garden photos), Documents (garden plans)
- PictureThis as future harvest source for plant inventory
- Schema reviewed by Silas before merge, loaded into Fuseki as named graph

## Context
Jeff's garden map cards (#1341, #1342, #1343) assume a garden ontology that doesn't exist. The garden is not standalone — it's a subgraph of property/land. The house has rooms, the property has structures, the land has sun paths and drainage. Jeff's front door faces east/south (warm in winter). These spatial relationships matter for garden planning and connect to the Self ontology (walking, sun exposure, embodied memory).

## Dependencies
- Inform #1341 (zone grouping), #1342 (photo overlay), #1343 (seasonal toggle)
- Parallel to #1401 (Self ontology extension) — different named graph, same pattern

tasks.title: Memory capture end-to-end demo — prove the full pipeline works live with a real story

## Context
Wren built a standalone prototype at product-manager/posture-strip.html — 574 frames across 5 days, working filters, film strip aesthetic. Jeff said 'this is cool and needs to go in analytics.' Wire it into the existing /jeff dashboard as a new section, or as a linked sub-page.

## Reference
- Prototype: product-manager/posture-strip.html
- Existing dashboard: views/jeff.ejs
- Posture data: scripts/posture-capture.sh → /tmp/posture-timelapse/

tasks.title: Story harvester should read app stories collection, not just stories.md — bridge the two stores
tasks.description:
## AC
- Story harvester reads from app stories collection (data/pods/jeff/stories/) as well as stories.md
- All stories in the app → Episodes in Fuseki with person/value edges
- Eliminates the 20-day gap: Mar 6-11 stories exist in the app but weren't harvested to Episodes
- Reconcile: stories.md entries that aren't in the app collection get created, app stories not in stories.md get harvested directly

## Context
Jeff noticed a 20-day gap in Episodes (Feb 24 → Mar 14). Stories exist in the app collection for that period (Shimmer, Chandra Bommas, dull saw, Celtics, Alexander Graham Bell, Athena/Staples, kaizen). The harvester only read stories.md. Two parallel stores — need one harvest path that covers both.

tasks.title: Review garden setup flow — team walkthrough of 7-step UX from property definition to seasonal planning
tasks.description:
## AC
- All three roles + Jeff review garden-setup-flow.html
- Each role provides feedback from their domain: Kade (app/UX feasibility), Silas (infra/data flow), Wren (product/sequencing)
- Identify which steps are ready to build vs need more design
- PictureThis integration feasibility assessed
- Weather API options evaluated
- Decision on first seasonal step to build (likely #4: Plant Your Garden)
- Permaculture principle alignment validated or adjusted

## Context
Product vision doc for the garden domain — 7-step UX flow from property setup to seasonal planning cycle. Steps 1-3 are one-time setup (mostly done: ontology #1406, zone map #1341). Steps 4-7 repeat each season and are the living system. Built as HTML artifact at product-manager/garden-setup-flow.html. Same pattern as memory architecture review (#1399) — conceptual doc reviewed by team, then cards flow from it.

## Reference
- Artifact: product-manager/garden-setup-flow.html
- Garden ontology: #1406 (accepted)
- Zone map: #1341 (accepted)
- Permaculture principles: Gaia's Garden (Dani's gift to Aubrey)

- Seasonal toggle: #1343 (carded, depends on #1341)

tasks.title: Garden bed editor — UI to add/edit beds within zones on the garden map
tasks.description:

- Beds persist to Fuseki via jb:hasBed property on zone

## Context
Garden setup flow step 3. ZONE_BEDS mapping exists but is hardcoded in the view. Need user-editable bed definitions. Garden ontology (#1406) has GardenBed class and hasBed property ready.

## Dependencies
- #1341 zone map (shipped)

tasks.title: Plant your garden — planting UI to record what's planted where and when
tasks.description:
## AC

- Plant species auto-suggest from existing plants in Fuseki or free-text entry
- Creates jb:Planting instances linked to bed, plant, season, and year
- Current plantings visible on the garden map (bed shows what's planted now)
- PictureThis integration as future enhancement for plant identification

## Context
Garden setup flow step 4. Ontology has Planting, Plant, Season classes (#1406). This is where the garden becomes a living record, not just a map.

## Dependencies
- #1412 bed editor (beds must exist to plant in them)

tasks.title: Garden observation tracker — photos, notes, health status per planting through the season
tasks.description:
## AC

- Observation types: photo, text note, health status (thriving/struggling/dead)
- Observations linked to specific plantings, timestamped
- Photo observations use existing photo upload pipeline
- Health status visible on garden map (color coding: green/yellow/red per bed)
- Ontology extension needed: jb:Observation class with date, type, content, health properties (Silas review)

## Context
Garden setup flow step 5. This is where the garden becomes a daily/weekly tool, not just seasonal setup. The richest data source for the garden domain — observations over time reveal patterns.

## Dependencies
- #1413 planting UI (must have plantings to observe)

tasks.title: Seasonal review and planning — year-over-year comparison and next season layout
tasks.description:
## AC
- End of season prompt: review each zone's performance
- Visual diff: this season's plantings vs last season (ghost/outline for previous year)
- Generate summary: what worked, what struggled, what to change
- Plan next season: copy current layout as starting point, modify plantings
- Drag plants between beds for planning
- Merges #1342 (photo overlay) and #1343 (seasonal toggle) concepts: each season can have its own hand-drawn annotation layer

## Context
Garden setup flow steps 6-7. The Reflecting spoke of the garden domain. Closes the annual cycle: Sowing → Growing → Harvesting → Reflecting → Sowing. Kade's idea: photo overlay (#1342) becomes seasonal — spring plan, summer plan, fall plan as separate annotation layers.

## Dependencies
- #1414 observation tracker (need season of data to review)
- Supersedes #1342 and #1343 conceptually (broader scope)

tasks.title: Decision-point memory trigger — surface relevant memories when cards are created, pulled, or direction changes
tasks.description:
## AC
- When a card is created or pulled, automatically query Chorus + Fuseki for related memories
- Related = matching people, values, domains, or similar episode shapes
- Surface as a brief context block: 'Related memories: Jeff shared X about Y, DEC-Z applies here'
- Triggers: card.pulled, card.created, interaction.pattern.detected (direction, ideation)
- Does NOT block the action — context appears alongside, not as a gate
- Uses DEC-074 search hierarchy: Chorus first, then graph, then filesystem

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 retrieval orchestration layer. Currently retrieval is ad-hoc — roles search when they think to. This makes it proactive: the system surfaces relevant context at decision points without being asked.

## Dependencies
- #1401 Self ontology (shipped — Episode, Value, Pattern classes exist)
- #1402 Story harvester (shipped — stories are in the graph)
- #1403 Edge persistence (shipped — connections are queryable)

tasks.title: Session-start memory retrieval — load relevant context from Chorus + graph at boot based on board state and recent activity
tasks.description:
## AC
- At session start (werk-init.sh), query Chorus for memories related to the role's current WIP card
- Query Fuseki for Episodes and Patterns connected to the card's domain/chunk
- Include in session-start context file: 'Memory context: 3 related stories, 2 prior decisions, 1 pattern'
- Links to specific memories, not summaries — role can read deeper if relevant
- Respects DEC-074: Chorus → graph → filesystem order
- Time-bounded: only memories from last 30 days + high-weight permanent memories

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 retrieval orchestration. Session start already loads board state, briefs, and health. This adds memory context — what the team has learned that's relevant to what's about to be worked on.

## Dependencies
- #1401-#1403 (shipped — graph has episodes, edges, connections)

tasks.title: Shape-matching retrieval — find memories with similar structure to the current situation using Chorus semantic search
tasks.description:
## AC
- Given a current context (card description, Jeff's direction, active discussion), find memories with similar shape
- 'Shape' = structural similarity, not keyword match: 'this retirement project feels like Staples' even if the word Staples isn't mentioned
- Uses Chorus semantic search across 96K+ messages for conversation-level matches
- Uses Fuseki SPARQL for pattern-level matches: episodes with same values, same connection types
- Returns ranked results with match reason: 'similar pattern: normalize→instrument→retire→rewrite'
- Exposed as an API endpoint: /api/memory/similar?context=<text>

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 retrieval synthesis layer. This is the 'your brain does this automatically' capability — connecting current work to past experience through structural similarity, not just keyword search. Jeff's Staples story informed the Docker migration because the SHAPE matched, not the words.

## Dependencies
- #1416 decision-point trigger (provides the trigger context)
- Chorus search API (exists at port 3340)
- #1401-#1403 (shipped — graph has queryable episodes and edges)

tasks.title: Ambient memory prompts — periodic context surfacing based on time, patterns, and Jeff's state
tasks.description:
## AC
- Morning practice check (already designed — feedback_morning_practice_gate.md)
- Weekly pattern reflection: 'This week you shared 3 stories, made 2 decisions, shipped N cards — here is what connects them'
- Stale memory check: 'You mentioned X a week ago — still relevant? Should it become a card?'
- Biometric correlation: when transposition rate spikes, surface stress-related memories/patterns
- Delivered conversationally, not as notifications — Wren weaves them into natural interaction
- Configurable frequency: daily, weekly, on-demand

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 retrieval delivery layer (ambient). The morning practice gate is the first example. This expands to periodic reflections that surface connections Jeff hasn't asked about but might benefit from seeing. Not a dashboard — a conversation partner that remembers.

## Dependencies
- #1417 session-start retrieval (infrastructure for memory loading)
- #1379 transposition rate (shipped — biometric signal available)

tasks.title: Cross-domain memory linking — connect photos to stories, music to episodes, documents to decisions
tasks.description:
## AC
- When a photo is harvested, check if it matches a story (by date, people, location)
- When music is played/harvested, check if it connects to an episode (Jeff's Songs I Love posts)
- When a document is routed, check if it relates to a decision
- Edges written to Fuseki: photo→episode, song→story, document→decision
- Uses existing edge persistence (#1403) infrastructure
- Visible on /experience page: 'This episode has 3 related photos, 1 song'

## Context
Memory Architecture #1399 gap: cross-domain edges. Each domain harvester writes nodes but not relationships between domains. This card creates the cross-domain linking layer. The garden photo overlay (#1342) is one example — photos connected to zones connected to seasons.

## Dependencies
- #1403 edge persistence (shipped)
- #1402 story harvester (shipped)
- Photo harvest pipeline (#1351, in progress)

tasks.title: Confidence-scored photo matching — fuzzy dedup across Drive, Takeout, and Apple Photos using date + dimensions + filename similarity
tasks.description:
## AC
- Match photos across sources using: capture date (±1s), image dimensions, filename similarity (Levenshtein or normalized)
- Each match gets a confidence score: exact (date+dims+name) = 1.0, date+dims only = 0.8, date only = 0.5
- Report: matched pairs with confidence scores, unmatched per source
- Reduces false 'truly new' count from 181K to actual unique photos
- No mutations — read-only analysis like #1364
- Output accessible via /api/drive-analysis/confidence or appended to existing drive-analysis page

## Context
#1364 census showed 0.2% overlap (397/283K) — suspiciously low because filename matching is exact-only. Drive filenames differ from Takeout and Apple Photos filenames for the same image. This card adds fuzzy matching to get accurate overlap numbers before routing.

## Dependencies
- #1364 Drive census (shipped — provides the file lists)
- #1351 Takeout harvest (in progress — provides Google Photos in Fuseki)

tasks.title: Route truly-new Drive photos to Photos domain — batch import with source tracking
tasks.description:
## AC
- Photos identified as truly new by #1421 (confidence < 0.5 match to any existing source) get routed to Photos domain
- Each photo gets jb:photoSource 'google-drive' to distinguish from Apple Photos and Takeout
- Metadata harvested: filename, date, dimensions, Drive folder path
- Resumable: manifest tracks which files have been routed
- Progress reporting: N/total routed, N skipped (already exists), N failed

## Context
After #1421 reduces the 181K 'truly new' count to actual uniques, this card routes them. Could still be tens of thousands of photos.

## Dependencies
- #1421 confidence-scored matching (must know which are truly new first)

tasks.title: Route truly-new Drive music to Music domain — batch import with source tracking
tasks.description:
## AC
- 6K music files from Drive routed to Music domain
- 0 overlap expected (music came from Apple Music, not Drive) — verify with #1421 fuzzy matching
- Each track gets jb:musicSource 'google-drive'
- Metadata: filename, file type, duration if available, Drive folder path
- Resumable with manifest

## Context
Drive census showed 6K music files with 0 overlap to Music domain. Expected — different sources. But verify with fuzzy matching before bulk import.

## Dependencies
- #1421 confidence-scored matching (verify 0 overlap is real, not a matching gap)

tasks.title: Video domain decision — extend Photos collection or create new Video collection for 28K Drive videos
tasks.description:
## AC
- Decision made: do videos live in Photos (as they do in Apple Photos and Google Photos) or get their own collection?
- If separate: define jb:Video class, jb:VideoCollection, video-specific properties (duration, resolution, codec)
- If within Photos: extend Photo class to handle video MIME types, update collection page to distinguish
- Consider: 28K videos + 6 large MOVs from Takeout (4-5GB each). Storage and streaming implications.
- Decision captured in decisions.md

## Context
Drive census: 28K videos. Takeout had 6 standalone MOVs (27GB total). Videos exist in Photos domain (Apple Photos includes video) but Drive has video-only files. The question is organizational: one collection or two? Bedroom Mac has Images API (gallery) and Video Server (streaming) as separate services — the infrastructure already treats them differently.

## Dependencies
- #1421 confidence matching (know how many videos are truly new)

tasks.title: Cross-source dedup reconciliation — same entity from multiple sources becomes one node with multiple source links
tasks.description:
## AC
- When the same photo/song exists in multiple sources (Apple Photos + Google Takeout + Drive), consolidate to one graph node
- Node gets multiple jb:photoSource / jb:musicSource triples (one per source)
- Preserve source-specific metadata as named sub-properties (Apple date vs Google date vs Drive date)
- Dedup key: confidence score from #1421 — matches above 0.8 are auto-merged, 0.5-0.8 flagged for review
- Report: N entities consolidated, N sources per entity, N flagged for manual review
- /photos page shows consolidated count, not source-duplicated count

## Context
The fundamental problem: Jeff's photos exist in Apple Photos, Google Photos Takeout, AND Google Drive. Without dedup, the knowledge graph triple-counts. This card ensures one photo = one node, with provenance tracking for which sources contain it.

## Dependencies
- #1421 confidence matching (provides the match pairs)
- #1422 photo routing (all photos in Fuseki before reconciliation)
- #1423 music routing (same pattern for music)

tasks.title: Pipeline manifest skill — auto-orchestrate multi-card sequences with dependency tracking and nudge-on-complete
tasks.description:
## AC
- Define a pipeline: ordered list of cards with dependencies and owners
- When a card is accepted (card.accepted spine event), check if next card's deps are met
- If met, auto-nudge the owner to pull
- Pipeline status queryable: 'Drive routing: 2/5 complete, #1422 ready to pull'
- Pipelines defined as JSON manifest files in a known directory
- /pipeline command to create, view status, and advance

## Context
Pattern emerging: multi-card pipelines with sequential dependencies (memory capture #1400-#1404, garden setup #1412-#1415, Drive routing #1421-#1425). Currently Wren manually orchestrates via nudges. Works but depends on Wren noticing card.accepted events and remembering the sequence. Automate when manual orchestration fails — not before.

## Decision
Deferred. Manual orchestration is working (2026-03-14 proved it). Card this so we remember the pattern when it breaks.

tasks.title: Retry failed photo thumbnails with ImageMagick — 11K GIFs and PNGs that sips couldn't convert
tasks.description:
## AC
- Install ImageMagick (or use Python Pillow) on Bedroom Mac
- Re-process the ~11,496 files that failed sips thumbnail generation during #1351
- Breakdown of failures: ~401 GIFs, ~4,839 PNGs, ~6,256 other (corrupt HEIC, truncated files)
- Generate 300px JPEG thumbnails for any that succeed
- Copy results to Library public/thumbnails/photos/ directory
- Report: N recovered, N genuinely unrecoverable (corrupt/zero-byte)
- Update google-index.json thumbnailPath entries for recovered files

## Context
During #1351 Google Takeout harvest, sips (macOS built-in) couldn't convert ~11.5K files to JPEG thumbnails. sips doesn't handle GIF→JPEG conversion and fails on some PNGs with alpha channels or unusual color profiles. ImageMagick or Pillow handles these formats reliably.

## Technical Notes
- Failed files are identifiable by: slug exists in google-index.json but no matching .jpg in public/thumbnails/photos/<bucket>/
- Media files on Bedroom at /Volumes/VideosNew/Gathering/Photos/GoogleTakeoutPhotos/extracted/Takeout/Google Photos/
- Lookup index already built at /tmp/google-media-lookup.tsv on Bedroom
- Run on Bedroom (local disk), rsync results to Library
- Parallel approach (8 workers) proven in #1351 — reuse same pattern

## Dependencies
- #1351 complete (provides the media files and index)

tasks.title: Retry failed thumbnails with ImageMagick/pillow — 11K GIFs and corrupt PNGs that sips can't convert
tasks.description:
sips fails on GIFs and corrupt PNGs during Google Takeout thumbnail generation. ~11K files affected out of 77K.

## AC
- [ ] ImageMagick or pillow used as fallback for files sips rejects
- [ ] Retry script processes the failure list from the sips run
- [ ] Coverage improves from 87% toward 95%+
- [ ] Failures logged with reason (corrupt, unsupported format, etc.)

tasks.title: Implement scheduled health subagents — Bedroom monitoring, Fuseki baseline, doc staleness, stale handoffs
tasks.description:
Wire up automated monitoring that runs without prompting. Five subagents on cron schedules.

Files: scripts/health-check-bedroom.sh, scripts/fuseki-baseline.sh, architect/scripts/launchagents/

## AC
1. Bedroom health check (every 30min) — SSH ping, node exporter scrape, card failures
2. Fuseki performance baseline (daily 9am) — SPARQL timing queries, log to Loki, alert on regression
3. Companion doc staleness scan (weekly) — diff numbers against live state, card updates needed
4. Stale handoff cleanup (every 4h) — scan briefs dirs, flag or delete stale handoffs
5. All implemented as CronCreate in session-start hook or LaunchAgent
6. Demo to Jeff showing scheduled runs and a sample alert

tasks.title: Automate Wren's routine work — free PM attention for value-add
tasks.description:
Shift Wren from clerk to Borg. Automate routine board/state/hygiene work so PM attention goes to pattern recognition, convergence, intellectual honesty, and connecting Jeff's experience to product decisions.

- [ ] Close-out checklist runs without prompting
- [ ] Memory freshness check is part of sweep cadence
- [ ] Wren's session time shifts measurably toward Borg observation, cadence analysis, and strategic work

## Subtasks
1. Board sweep on cron — DONE (skill built, 4h schedule)
2. State file auto-sync — trigger updates on card moves, not at close-out
3. Brief routing automation — brief written to role inbox triggers notification without manual nudge
4. Close-out as skill — /reboot handles all close-out steps, no manual checklist

tasks.title: Automate Kade routine checks as scheduled subagents — thumbnail rsync, sync manifest, test health, harvest staleness, post-deploy smoke
tasks.description:
## AC
- [ ] Thumbnail rsync subagent: every 30 min, copies new thumbnails from Bedroom NFS (/Volumes/Gathering/Photos/GoogleTakeoutThumbnails/) to Library (public/thumbnails/photos/). Reports count delta.
- [ ] Fuseki sync manifest check: every 2 hours, compares TTL file count on disk vs .sync-manifest.json entries. Flags drift >100 files. Auto-creates card if drift detected.
- [ ] Test suite nightly: runs full test suite at 2am, reports pass/fail count + any regressions vs last run. Logs to cost-log.md.
- [ ] Harvest staleness scan: daily at 9am, reads harvest manifests, flags domains >7 days since last run. Auto-nudges Kade with stale list.
- [ ] Post-deploy smoke: after every app-state.sh deploy, auto-runs smoke-check.sh --all. Reports failures to activity.md.
- [ ] All subagents log to ../messages/activity.md with [subagent] prefix
- [ ] Each subagent is a standalone script in scripts/subagents/ — schedulable via LaunchAgent or cron
- [ ] Subagents report only — never mutate app state or deploy

## Context
Jeff's direction: "automate routine, spend attention on value." Today's session revealed multiple manual checks (thumbnail copy, Fuseki state verification, smoke checks) that consumed Kade's attention during value work (#1351 pipeline). These are state-checking operations with no judgment required — pure automation candidates.

## Design
Each subagent is a shell script that:
1. Checks a specific condition
2. Reports to activity.md or nudges Kade
3. Exits cleanly — no long-running daemons

Silas owns the LaunchAgent scheduling (DEC-030). Kade writes the scripts, Silas wires them.

## Dependencies
- Silas for LaunchAgent creation after scripts are built

tasks.description:

tasks.title: Add domain view to /flow — vertical value pipelines alongside horizontal chunk view

## Example (Photos)
Phase 1: Smoke — 128 merges (Sept 2019) ✓
Phase 2: Batch — 10 months, ~30s →
Phase 3: Full — ~300 months, ~3 min ○
Phase 4: Fuseki sync — push TTL → graph ○
Phase 5: Browse page — separate card ○
Gaps: perceptual hashing, video domain, browse switch

tasks.title: Sync Drive routing TTL files to Fuseki — 281K photos + 6K music into graph

tasks.title: Add domain label support to board-ts — board-ts tag <id> <domain> --category domain

tasks.title: Add progress emission to all long-running pipeline operations — no more black boxes
tasks.description:
Every long-running operation (Fuseki sync, thumbnail gen, reconciliation, harvest) runs silent for minutes. Jeff can't tell progress from hung. 

Files: scripts/harvest-sync-fuseki.sh, scripts/drive-routing-cli.sh, scripts/gen-google-thumbnails.sh

## AC
- [ ] Fuseki sync emits progress every 1000 files: [HH:MM] N / total synced (X%)
- [ ] Reconciliation emits per-month progress: [HH:MM] month YYYY-MM, N matches, N merged
- [ ] Thumbnail generation emits per-batch: [HH:MM] N / total generated
- [ ] All progress goes to stdout (visible in session) AND to a progress file (tailable by gemba/navigator)
- [ ] Progress file at /tmp/pipeline-progress-<operation>.log — overwritten each run
- [ ] Jeff can tail progress from any terminal without asking a role

tasks.title: Add progress logging to harvest sync jobs — emit count/skip/error every 30s or N files
tasks.description:

- Format: synced=N skipped=N errors=N elapsed=Ns (single line, parseable)
- Same pattern for harvest-sync-fuseki.sh shell script
- No change to final JSON output — progress goes to stderr, result to stdout

tasks.title: Photo source lineage report — trace raw input to displayed count per source
tasks.description:

- Explain every drop (why 283K becomes 2,960 for Drive, why 118K becomes 53K for Takeout)
- Numbers verifiable: each stage cites the manifest, query, or function that produces it
- Jeff can read it and trust the pipeline without re-running anything

tasks.title: Fix Drive photo slug collision — 200K photos lost to same-slug overwrites in month buckets
tasks.description:
## AC
- photoSlug() for Drive photos includes Drive file ID suffix (e.g. img-0007-d8f3a2)
- Re-route all Drive photos with unique slugs
- Re-sync to Fuseki
- Verify: Drive photo count in Fuseki matches unique slug+month count from document index
- No data loss: every Drive photo with contentCategory=photo gets its own graph node

## Root Cause
photoSlug('IMG_0007.jpg') → 'img-0007' for 68 copies across different Drive folders. Same month bucket → same URI → last-write-wins. 283K docs → 180K unique slugs → 205K unique slug+month → only 4,420 survived in TTL.

## Fix
Append Drive file ID to slug: photoSlug(filename) + '-' + sourceId.slice(0, 8). Same pattern as google-drive-harvester.service.ts line 286.

## Blocks
- #1446 (full reconciliation) — needs correct data first

tasks.title: Apply reconciliation merges to Fuseki graph — SPARQL INSERT into correct named graphs
tasks.description:

- consolidatedFrom + photoSource triples on canonical nodes, queryable via SPARQL
- Duplicate nodes either linked or removed (decide with Silas)
- Browse page reflects consolidated count
- Depends on: reconciliation report (done), correct graph URI targeting (investigate localhost:3000 vs 3030 vs named graph paths)

## Root Cause
SPARQL INSERT DATA used full URIs but triples landed nowhere — graph targeting mismatch. Need to identify correct named graph pattern for INSERT.

## Files
- src/services/cross-source-reconciler.service.ts
- data/harvest/manifests/cross-source-reconciliation.json
- scripts/reconcile-photos.sh

tasks.title: Add dedup gate to auto-error card creation — check for existing card with same error hash before creating

tasks.title: Bedroom health probe + SSH discipline enforcement

tasks.title: Enforce API contract discipline — domain endpoints as validated access layer

3. Add domain tags to OpenAPI spec (photos, music, books, property, people, stories, documents) so endpoints are browsable by domain
4. Integration test coverage for each domain's core endpoints (list, get, create, delete) — tests hit the API, not the service layer
5. No new endpoint ships without swagger annotation and at least one integration test

## Context
Jeff wants validated, tested domain endpoints as the standard access pattern — not ad-hoc SPARQL. This is the access-layer complement to the harvest contract (#1456). Harvest standardized ingest; this standardizes reads.

## Files

4. Verify no regression in page rendering (existing integration tests pass)
5. Document the pattern so remaining domains follow the same approach

## Context
DEC-093 says consumers use endpoints, not ad-hoc queries. The next step: page handlers themselves should consume domain services instead of crafting bespoke SPARQL. One query path, tested once. Depends on #1459 (API contract audit) completing first.

4. Flag the highest-duplication and highest-coupling hotspots
5. Output feeds prioritization of #1460 (handler refactor)

## Context
Before refactoring handlers to consume services, we need to see the dependency map. Which pages touch which domains? Where is SPARQL duplicated vs. unique? This is the discovery step that makes #1460 surgical instead of boil-the-ocean.

## Context
Jeff wants to see the board as a funnel with domain circles showing the stack at each stage. Strategic view (funnel) vs execution view (cards) — same data, different projection. The funnel shape reinforces WIP discipline visually. The circles show where each domain's work concentrates.

tasks.title: Route Drive documents to correct domains — photos, music, video out of Documents into their domains
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Identify Drive documents that are actually photos (201K), music (6.3K), video (28.8K) by MIME type or file extension
2. Re-run routing logic (#1422/#1423 pattern) to move these into Photos, Music, and Video domains in the graph
3. Source: Computers backups (Mac mini × 2, USB devices), Google Photos folder, and scattered media in My Drive
4. Documents collection count drops by the routed items — only true documents remain
5. Photos/Music/Video collection counts increase accordingly
6. No data loss — graph edges updated, source files untouched on Drive

## Context
Google Drive harvest dumped everything into Documents (495K items). ~236K of those are photos/video/music from Computers backups, not documents. Routing logic exists (#1422, #1423) but linkages were removed. Drive is at 91% (1.83TB/2TB). DEC-092 says Video is its own domain.

tasks.title: Reclaim Drive storage — deprecate Computers backups after harvest verification
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Verify all Computers backup content (My Mac mini × 2, USB Devices) is harvested into Gathering graph
2. Verify source files exist on Bedroom storage (not just graph metadata)
3. Produce a manifest: what's safe to delete from Drive, estimated GB reclaimed
4. Jeff reviews and approves the delete list before any removal
5. Drive storage drops meaningfully from 91% (1.83TB/2TB)

## Context
Drive at 91%. Computers backups contain photos, videos, music that are being harvested into Gathering domains. Once harvested and verified on Bedroom, the Drive copies are redundant. Depends on #1472 (route Drive docs to correct domains) completing first. Jeff approves deletions — no autonomous removal.

tasks.title: Harden pair/gemba outer loop — navigator stays in pair, don't break loop to report to Jeff
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Pair skill has a DND mode — nudges from outside the pair queue instead of injecting immediately
2. Navigator's audience is the driver, not Jeff. Only surface to Jeff on decisions or card completion.
3. If Jeff or Wren messages during active pair, auto-acknowledge: "pair active on #N, will respond at checkpoint"
4. Gemba scope-boxed mode continues until card ships or explicit /pair stop — no TTL prompts
5. When navigator shifts from gemba to active pair, kill observation cron automatically
6. Anti-pattern detection: if navigator sends a non-decision message to Jeff mid-pair, flag it
7. Document failure modes from 2026-03-16 session in pair skill

## Context
Three loop breaks in one session. Deeper insight: ANY intrusion breaks the pair — nudge from Wren, message from Jeff, the navigator turning to report. Same as human pairing — tap on the shoulder kills the flow. Agents treat every incoming message as equal priority and can't say "hang on." The pair needs DND mode where external messages queue until a checkpoint. Only the driver and explicit /pair stop break through.

tasks.title: Harvest Google Photos videos into Video domain — 28K+ items from Drive

- Namespace must be registered in canonical-namespaces.md

## Files
src/ontology/icd-instance-video.ttl (new), scripts/reclassify-videos.sh (new), architect/canonical-namespaces.md

tasks.title: Fix Attention Cost counters on /werk — re-prompts, approval overhead, corrections all showing 0
