tasks.title: Fix /werk operational instruments — workflows, WIP, briefs, steps all showing zero despite active data
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Board client `api()` method preserves query parameters (pagination, filters) — `url.pathname + url.search` not just `url.pathname`
2. `loom-metrics.js` returns accurate board counts (open > 0 when open cards exist)
3. /werk Fitness panel shows real throughput, completion %, and deploy counts
4. /werk WIP panel shows active WIP cards per role
5. No regression in board-ts CLI operations

## Root cause
`BoardClient.api()` used `url.pathname` which drops query parameters. `fetchAllTasks()` pagination loop sent the same un-paginated request 29 times, getting 50 done tasks each time = 1450 false 'done' entries. All instruments downstream showed zero open work.

tasks.title: Add analytics and metrics domain endpoints — structured JSON for perf baseline, service health, disk, harvest pipeline, cost
tasks.description:
Wrap existing ops data sources as Chorus API endpoints (DEC-093 pattern). Read-only, JSON.

## Endpoints
- `GET /api/chorus/perf` — latest perf-baseline run, deltas, pass/fail
- `GET /api/chorus/services` — LaunchAgent name, status, RSS, port
- `GET /api/chorus/disk` — used%, budget categories
- `GET /api/chorus/harvest` — domains, staleness, triple counts per graph
- `GET /api/chorus/cost` — daily/weekly spend by model

## AC
1. Each endpoint returns structured JSON wrapping existing script/data output
2. Error cases return proper HTTP status codes, not empty 200s
3. At least one consumer (e.g. /werk dashboard or gemba) uses the new endpoints
4. Documented in CLAUDE.md domain endpoints table

tasks.title: Disposition stale workflow steps — close skipped steps or reset baseline for flow metrics
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Identify all archived workflows with skipped/pending steps (108 steps currently not completed)
2. Decide: mark skipped steps as 'resolved-skipped' (closed but distinct from completed), OR set a baseline date and only measure gap from that date forward
3. After disposition, /werk Flow Metrics cumulative flow chart shows arrival and completion lines converging
4. Document the decision — which approach and why

## Context
307 total workflow steps, 199 completed, 108 skipped. The gap is permanent unless dispositioned. Early workflows predate the pipeline pattern — those skipped steps reflect a process that no longer applies.

tasks.title: Add card cumulative flow and throughput charts to /werk Flow Metrics
Fix card completion timestamps on /werk — use spine events not card.updated field
Canonical cards endpoints — unified API that serves /werk, /flow, and roles with paginated, done_at-enriched card data
tasks.description:
Consolidate card metrics into one canonical API so all UI surfaces (/werk, /flow, /team, role fitness) consume the same data. Currently each surface builds its own pipeline from board-ts or Vikunja — fixing a calculation in one place leaves others drifted.

## Problem
- /werk flow tab computes cumulative flow + throughput inline in werk.ejs
- /flow domain tiles fetch card counts independently
- Role fitness cards (steps, avg lead, bottleneck) compute in werk.ejs client-side
- Enhancing one surface breaks or diverges from another

## AC
1. `/api/chorus/cards` returns paginated, done_at-enriched card data with status history
2. `/api/chorus/cards/metrics` returns pre-computed: cumulative flow series, daily throughput, role fitness (steps, avg lead, max lead, slowest, bottleneck)
3. /werk flow tab consumes metrics endpoint instead of inline computation
4. /flow domain tiles consume cards endpoint instead of independent fetch
5. Role fitness cards consume metrics endpoint
6. Existing charts render identically after migration (visual regression check)
7. Single fix to metric logic propagates to all surfaces

## Approach
- Consolidation card, not feature card. Pull when next touching any analytics surface.
- Chart rendering stays client-side; data shape becomes consistent.
- Existing endpoints (`/api/chorus/cards`, `/cards/:id`, `/cards/domain/:domain`) are the foundation — extend, don't replace.

## Context
Discussion 2026-03-19: Jeff flagged that analytics emit on multiple UI surfaces and enhancing one breaks another. This card is the fix for that fragility.

tasks.title: iPhone photo/video inventory — 55K items, people data extraction, delta with canonical home cloud
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Inventory Jeff's iPhone library: count of photos, videos, Live Photos, screenshots (55K total)
2. Extract people/face data from iPhone library — Apple Photos has richer face recognition than what we harvested from the Mac library (2.7K items vs 55K)
3. Compute delta: what's on iPhone/iCloud that ISN'T in the canonical home cloud (Fuseki graph + Bedroom storage)
4. Report: N new photos, N new videos, N new people/faces, N already harvested
5. Assess: is the iPhone library a superset of what we have, or are there gaps both ways?

## Context
Jeff has 55K photos + videos on iPhone. Current harvest sources are Google Takeout (68K) and Mac Apple Photos Library (2.7K). The iPhone/iCloud library is likely much larger and has richer face/people data. This is the next major ingest source after Drive routing (#1472). Paused per DEC-094 until operations discipline work completes.

tasks.title: Enrich session-start Memory Context — stories, domain DECs, prior card learning
tasks.description:
Follow-on to #1417. Three enrichments for the Memory Context section in session-start files:

## AC
1. Surface stories from stories.md when WIP card domain matches story tags/content
2. Search DECs by domain keyword, not just recent — if domain:photos, DEC-094 (harvest pause) appears
3. Include completion comments from recently-Done cards in the same domain ("last photos card learned X")
4. Memory Context section shows source type: [story], [decision], [prior-card]
5. Total retrieval stays under 3s (current budget)

## Context
#1417 shipped the plumbing. This card makes the content richer. The boot should feel like a team briefing, not a data dump.

tasks.title: Enrich shape-matching with theme-level graph queries — replace title-literal SPARQL with theme/value overlap matching
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Shape-matching graph queries match on jb:hasTheme and jb:hasValue, not dcterms:title text
2. False positives like 'Instrumental' music tracks matching 'instrument' verb eliminated
3. Stories with overlapping themes scored higher than keyword coincidences
4. Match reasons reference theme names, not matched title substrings

## Context
Demo showed title-literal matching producing false positives. The pattern extraction is solid but the graph side needs to match on semantic structure (themes, values) not text.

## Files
src/services/shape-matching.service.ts

tasks.title: Wire People graph into cross-domain linker — connect notes, social posts, documents to Person nodes
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Notes mentioning a person name link to that Person node (note→mentions→person)
2. Social posts 'with' or 'on timeline of' a person link to their Person node
3. Documents in person-named folders link to that Person node
4. Aubrey query returns all five domains connected through the People graph, not as isolated islands

## Context
The Aubrey demo showed notes, social posts, docs, and Chorus memories all referencing the same person but with no graph connections between them. The People domain is the natural hub.

## Files
src/services/cross-domain-linker.service.ts, src/handlers/self-domain.handler.ts, src/services/sparql.service.ts

tasks.title: Add period metadata to stories — enable photo↔story date-overlap linking
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Story harvester extracts or prompts for jb:period on stories that lack it
2. Period values parsed into year ranges for cross-domain date matching
3. Photo→story links discovered for stories with period data
4. At least 50% of stories have period metadata after enrichment

## Context
Cross-domain linker found zero photo→story links because stories lack jb:period. The plumbing works — the data needs enrichment.

tasks.title: iOS app for local wifi — native client for Gathering API on property/garden/photos
tasks.description:
## Experience
I'm walking the garden and see something — a new bloom, a bed that needs attention, damage from last night's storm. I pull out my phone and it's right there: my photos, my garden beds, my property notes. All on local wifi, no cloud, no login. I take a photo tagged to the bed I'm standing in front of. Later at my desk it's already in the system.

## AC
1. SwiftUI iOS app connects to Gathering API over local wifi (192.168.86.36:3000)
2. Browse photos natively — grid view, albums, face clusters
3. Garden walk mode — take photos tagged to garden beds/zones
4. Property inspection — view bed status, seasonal history, observations
5. Runs only on local network — no cloud auth, respects concentric trust
6. Uses existing API endpoints (DEC-093) — no new backend work needed

## Context
Jeff has 370 API endpoints on the Gathering app. An iOS app is a native client for that API, not a new system. Local wifi only — Library serves, iPhone consumes. Garden, photos, property are the first use cases.

tasks.title: Harvest Apple Photos via osxphotos — extract faces, albums, locations, people names from Mac Photos.app database
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Install osxphotos on Library Mac — reads Photos.app SQLite database directly
2. Extract: face clusters WITH NAMES (Apple's recognition, not just UUIDs), albums, locations, dates, keywords
3. Map named faces to People domain — create jb:Person nodes with real names
4. Report: N people identified by name, N photos with location data, N albums
5. Compare with existing harvest: what's new vs what we already have from Google Takeout
6. No iCloud API needed — reads local database

## Context
Mac Photos has 21,322 photos + 3,172 videos synced from iCloud. iPhone has 54,087 (30K delta will sync over time). osxphotos reads the Photos.app database directly — gets face NAMES, not just cluster UUIDs. This is the richest people data source available. Connects to #1478 (face cluster naming) and #1493 (people as cross-domain hub).

tasks.title: Implement navigator heartbeat cron — the pair DND mechanism from #1474 needs actual cron wiring
tasks.description:
Wire the navigator heartbeat cron from the /pair DND protocol into actual CronCreate calls.

## Files
- messages/scripts/pair-heartbeat.sh
- .claude/skills/pair/SKILL.md

## AC
- [ ] Navigator heartbeat fires every 60s during active pair
- [ ] Reads scratch file for Queued Messages and PM Notes
- [ ] Checks driver tail for progress
- [ ] Loops back to scope loop step 1 (CHECK AC)
- [ ] Auto-stops when pair ends
- [ ] No manual cron setup needed — pair skill creates it automatically

tasks.title: Embed all domains via Ollama — semantic search across photos, stories, documents, music
tasks.description:
Extend Ollama embeddings (already running on Bedroom for Chorus) to domain content. Every domain entity gets a vector — photos by description+labels, stories by text, documents by content, music by metadata.

## AC
1. Embedding pipeline: read domain entities from Fuseki → generate vectors via Ollama (nomic-embed-text) → store in LanceDB with graph URI as key
2. At least 3 domains embedded: photos (labels+description), stories (text), music (metadata)
3. Semantic search endpoint: GET /api/chorus/similar?uri=<entity>&limit=5 — returns nearest neighbors across domains
4. Session-start Memory Context (#1491) can query embeddings for richer results
5. Runs on Bedroom (Ollama already there) — no Library compute cost
6. Incremental: new entities embedded on harvest, not full re-embed

## Context
Ollama + nomic-embed-text already runs on Bedroom for Chorus message vectors. This extends the same infrastructure to domain content. Makes every domain semantically searchable and enables cross-domain discovery ("photos similar to this story").

## Files
src/services/semantic-search.service.ts, src/services/embedding.service.ts, src/handlers/search.handler.ts, src/app.ts, scripts/embed-domains.sh

tasks.title: Face clustering on Bedroom — cluster 27K detections, present to Jeff for naming
tasks.description:
Run local face clustering on Bedroom Mac (M2 Pro 32GB) against the 27K face detections from Apple Photos. Cluster similar faces, present clusters to Jeff for naming via UI (#1478). Named clusters become jb:Person nodes — the cross-domain hub.

## AC
1. Local face model runs on Bedroom (no cloud) — extract embeddings from face crops
2. Cluster into groups (likely 50-200 distinct people)
3. Top 20 clusters (by photo count) presented to Jeff with sample faces
4. Jeff names a cluster → creates jb:Person with jb:appearsIn edges to all photos in cluster
5. Links to existing People graph (Contacts harvest #1269)
6. Disk estimate before running (C7)

## Context
27K _UNKNOWN_ face detections in Photos.app. Apple did the detection, we do the clustering + naming. This is the prerequisite for #1478 (face naming UI) and #1493 (people as cross-domain hub). Bedroom has the compute (32GB) and the photos are accessible via osxphotos.

tasks.title: Session transcript → story extraction — Claude reads transcripts, surfaces Jeff's stories
tasks.description:
Use Claude API to read session transcripts and extract moments where Jeff shared personal context, history, or stories. Auto-populate stories.md entries with source links back to the session.

## AC
1. Scan Chorus index for Jeff's messages (author=user) longer than 100 chars that aren't commands
2. Claude classifies: story/context/direction/noise — only stories get extracted
3. Output: story entry with title, date, what Jeff shared, source session link
4. Deduplicate against existing stories.md entries
5. Jeff reviews extracted stories before they're added (not fully automated)
6. Cost-bounded: process in batches, log API cost per run

## Context
Jeff's stories are the highest-signal data in the system — they carry values, preferences, and history that shape every decision. Currently they're only captured when a role notices in real-time. This mines the 96K indexed messages for stories that were missed. Wren owns stories.md curation.

## Files
product-manager/stories-extractor.ts (new), scripts/extract-stories.sh (new)

2. Harvest ALL CSVs in one pass — canonical JSON dump first, then load:
   - Recommendations_Received.csv (22KB) — who said what about Jeff
   - Recommendations_Given.csv (20KB) — what Jeff said about others
   - Connections.csv (243KB) — full network with dates
   - Positions.csv (7.5KB) — career history timeline
   - Skills.csv — skills list
   - Endorsement_Received_Info.csv (30KB) — who endorsed what
   - Endorsement_Given_Info.csv (16KB)
   - ImportedContacts.csv (552KB) — all contacts
   - Shares.csv (22KB) — posts
   - Inferences_about_you.csv — LinkedIn's ML inferences about Jeff
3. Reconcile with existing 57 LinkedIn posts in graph before loading
4. Connections feed People domain — create jb:Person nodes with connection dates
5. Recommendations link to People nodes — who recommended whom
6. One extraction, one reconciliation, one load (DEC-093, feedback_reconcile_before_load)

## Context
Full export at ~/Desktop/Gathering/Complete_LinkedInDataExport_03-01-2026.zip.zip (1MB, dated March 1). Currently only 57 posts harvested. Recommendations, connections, endorsements, career history, skills all unharvested. Same anti-pattern as photos — grabbed the obvious, missed the rich data.

tasks.title: Canonical entity layer for Photos — reconcile 95K source records into canonical photos
tasks.description:
Photos has 95,806 source records from 3 sources (Google Takeout, Apple Photos, Google Drive) but no canonical count. The phash reconciler produced 77K consolidatedFrom edges but no canonical nodes. Page queries read source graphs and filter duplicates at display time.

## AC
1. Define jb:Photo as canonical type in ontology with jb:hasSourceRecord, jb:reconciledBy, jb:reconciledAt (not CanonicalPhoto — per URI naming discipline)
2. Build photos-canonical graph from existing phash edges — one canonical node per physical image
3. Canonical node holds best-available metadata (Apple location, Google album, etc.)
4. /photos page reads canonical graph — one row per physical image
5. Report: canonical count vs source count (the answer to "how many photos")
6. Reconciler runs incrementally on future harvests

## Dependencies
- #1504 (canonical photo serving model) should be designed first — reconciliation needs to know the serving architecture

## Context
Assessment at data/about/HARVEST_RECONCILIATION.html. Same pattern applies to People, Music, Documents as they get second sources. Photos first because it's the most multi-sourced domain.

## Files
src/ontology/jb-ontology.ttl, src/services/photo-reconciler.service.ts, src/handlers/photo.handler.ts

tasks.title: Harvest full Facebook data export — friends, profile, messages, posts, comments, places
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Source: ~/Downloads/facebook-jeffbridwell169465-2026-03-03-34FwtHTO/ (March 3 export)
2. Harvest ALL in one pass — canonical JSON first, reconcile, load:
   - your_friends.json — full friend list, feeds People domain
   - profile_information.json — bio, experience, contacts
   - messages/inbox/ — conversation history (private, Self domain trust)
   - your_posts__check_ins__photos_and_videos_1.json — posts with locations
   - comments.json + likes_and_reactions (12 files) — engagement history
   - places_you_have_been_tagged_in.json — location data
   - your_experiences.json — life events
3. Reconcile friends with existing People domain and LinkedIn Connections
4. Reconcile posts with existing 2,030 Facebook SocialPosts in graph
5. One extraction, one reconciliation, one load

## Context
Full export on disk (March 3 2026). Currently 2,030 Facebook posts harvested — friends, profile, messages, comments, places all unharvested. Same pattern as LinkedIn — grabbed posts, missed everything else. Friends + LinkedIn connections = Jeff's full social graph for People domain.

tasks.title: Design canonical photo serving model — one path from any source to full-res display
tasks.description:
Photos page has wrong dates, lightbox shows thumbnails not originals, no coherent model across 4 storage locations. We've been patching symptoms — this card is the architectural fix.

## AC
1. Single canonical photo URI resolves to the best available image (original > derivative > thumbnail) regardless of source
2. Date is always EXIF DateTimeOriginal, not harvest/export date — backfill Google dates from Takeout JSON or osxphotos
3. Full-res Google Takeout photos on Bedroom served without NFS bulk reads (proxy or Bedroom API)
4. Storage map documented: where each source's originals, derivatives, and thumbnails live across Library and Bedroom
5. Photo handler has ONE code path for 'get me this photo' — not source-specific branching
6. Design doc reviewed by Wren before Kade implements

## Context
Jeff flagged these issues directly. 4 storage locations: Apple Photos library (Library Mac), Google Takeout (Bedroom), Google Drive (Bedroom), osxphotos supplemental. Each has different path conventions, different date fields, different resolution tiers. The serving model must abstract all of this behind one URI.

## Files
architect/docs/photo-serving-model.md (new), src/services/photo.service.ts, src/handlers/photo.handler.ts

tasks.title: Build semantic mapping visualization — source schemas → canonical schema with transformation rules
tasks.description:
Render source and target semantics side by side with visual mappings between them. Based on Bridwell patent US9552400B2 (RDF/OWL + SPARQL + workflow gates for semantic integration).

## AC
1. Left panel: source schemas rendered as property columns (Apple Photos, Google Takeout, Google Drive) with field names, types, coverage %
2. Right panel: canonical schema (jb:CanonicalPhoto) with all target fields
3. Mapping lines: source field → canonical field, color-coded by confidence (direct/transform/missing)
4. Each mapping carries its transformation rule (e.g., "Apple epoch offset → ISO8601")
5. Validation gates visible: rules that fire at reconciliation time to catch bad data (date sanity, dimension mismatch)
6. Interactive: click a canonical field to see which sources provide it and what transforms apply
7. First implementation: Photos domain. Extensible to Music, People, Documents.

## Context
Jeff's patent (US9552400B2) is literally this tool. We needed it today — Kade and Silas both made mapping mistakes (wrong dates, wrong URIs, wrong storage paths) that this visualization would have prevented. Source semantics doc at data/about/SOURCE_SEMANTICS.html has the raw mapping data. This makes it visual and interactive.

Connects to #1502 (canonical entity layer) — this is the design tool, #1502 is the runtime implementation.

tasks.title: Replan photos harvest — ICD-driven sequence, source priority, drop-and-rebuild assessment
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Using the semantic mapper data, determine source priority order for photos harvest
2. Assess drop-and-rebuild vs incremental enrichment for existing 95K graph nodes
3. Define the first source to harvest cleanly using DEC-095 (mapper before harvest)
4. Reconciliation plan: how do we handle 3 ID spaces (Apple UUID, Google UUID, Drive ID)
5. Merge policy per field defined and validated in mapper
6. Compensation strategy for each gap (enrich/accept/derive)
7. Timeline estimate for clean rebuild if drop-and-rebuild wins

## Context
Current photos graph has 95K nodes from multiple harvests done without ICDs. URI mismatches, date bugs, orphaned triples, 14K duplicate consolidations. Jeff's hunch: drop and rebuild from canonical sources using the mapper as the gate. DEC-095 says no harvest without validated ICD. The mapper (#1505) now shows all 8 sources with coverage data.

tasks.title: Execute photos drop-and-rebuild — clean harvest using ICD, mapper, and DEC-095 gate
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. Drop all existing photo graphs from Fuseki (95K nodes, date-bucketed + apple-source + drive-routed)
2. Show 'rebuilding' message on /photos during pipeline
3. Pass 1: Apple Photos native → canonical graph. URI: /photos/canonical/{phash-prefix}/{phash}. Compute phash for each.
4. Pass 2: osxphotos supplemental → enrich canonical nodes with scene labels + locations. Join by Apple UUID.
5. Pass 3: Google Takeout → reconcile by phash or date+filename. New canonical nodes for unmatched. Date rule: ONLY use photoTakenTime, NEVER creationTime.
6. Pass 4: Google Drive → match by filename. Enrich fileSize + webViewLink. No date enrichment.
7. Rebuild SQLite browse cache from canonical graph
8. Verify: /photos page shows correct count, no date bugs, no duplicate tiles, all thumbnails matched
9. Canonical JSON dump preserved on disk as insurance

## Context
Replan from #1506. Current graph unsalvageable: 3 URI patterns, date bugs, 77K consolidation patches. Drop and rebuild is less work than enriching. DEC-095: mapper validated. Source priority: Apple → osxphotos → Takeout → Drive. Estimated 2-3 hours. Silas navigates.

tasks.title: Generate harvest pipeline code from ICD definitions
tasks.description:
## Acceptance Criteria
1. A generator script reads SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html data-* attributes (data-source, data-field, data-maps-to, data-transform, data-severity, data-datatype, data-constraint)
2. Emits a domain harvester service skeleton: extract (source fields) → transform (per data-transform rules) → canonical record
3. Emits SHACL shapes from consumer ICD cf-row severity tiers (violation/warning/info → sh:Violation/Warning/Info)
4. Emits a domain-specific quality gate (extends validate-canonical.py pattern) that validates against the generated shapes
5. Smoke test: generate Photos harvester from ICD, compare against hand-written photo-harvester.service.ts — should cover 80%+ of field logic
6. Run end-to-end with observe/pair loop: Silas observes, navigator reviews ICD + mappings via nudge, driver generates + runs

## Context
Today's session proved the ICD is rigorous enough to drive code. The SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html has data attributes on every field-row and cf-row. Jeff's Staples patent (US9552400B2) describes this exact pattern: ICD registration → validation → canonical model auto-enhancement. DEC-095 (mapper before harvest) is the gate. This card closes the loop: the mapper IS the spec, the code is derived.

## Why
12 thumbnail script attempts in 10 days. Every harvester reinvents field mapping. The ICD already contains the transformation rules — generating code from them eliminates the class of errors where the code diverges from the contract.

tasks.title: Wire ICD validation gate into Stories write path
tasks.description:

2. Violations reject the write with error log
3. Warnings log but allow write
4. Existing 166 TTL files pass validation (run validate-stories.py against them)
5. /stories page unchanged — zero impact to read path
6. Tests pass, lint clean

## Context
Generated from #1508 ICD code generation. The interface matches 100%. This wires the quality discipline into the existing working pipeline.

tasks.title: Add search-hierarchy guard hook — enforce DEC-074 before filesystem
tasks.description:
PreToolUse hook that detects when a role searches the filesystem for context/history questions and nudges toward Chorus first.

## AC
- [ ] Hook fires on Bash/Grep/Glob tool calls
- [ ] Detects patterns: searching for discussion topics, decision references, prior session context
- [ ] Nudge message references DEC-074 search hierarchy
- [ ] Does NOT fire for legitimate structure searches (class defs, file patterns, code refs)
- [ ] Mirrors sparql-guard-hook.sh pattern

tasks.title: Hangover recovery — crash-resilient role checkpoint for fast context rebuild
tasks.description:
Roles lose all working context on a hard crash because next-session.md only writes at clean close-out. Post-crash boot triggers expensive filesystem archaeology (34+ tool calls observed on #1509 recovery).

Fix: periodic checkpoint to /tmp/role-checkpoint-<role>.json with card ID, current files, last action, working branch. Session-start hook reads it if next-session.md is stale or missing.

## AC
- [ ] Checkpoint writes every ~60s during active work (lightweight, no user-visible cost)
- [ ] Session-start hook detects crash (missing/stale next-session.md) and reads checkpoint
- [ ] Post-crash boot prints "Resuming from checkpoint: card #N, last action: X" instead of exploring
- [ ] Clean close-out still writes next-session.md as before (checkpoint is fallback, not replacement)
- [ ] Observed improvement: post-crash context rebuild < 30s, not 3+ minutes

## Files
messages/scripts/role-checkpoint.sh, messages/scripts/werk-init.sh

tasks.title: Extract shared validateFromICD pattern — reusable validation before domain #2

tasks.title: Wire Social and People pages to canonical SPARQL — no pod-read fallbacks
tasks.description:
Social and People data is in Fuseki (2,075 social posts, 2,266 people). ICD validation gates are live. The UI should render from SPARQL queries against the canonical graph, not pod file reads with compensation logic.

Same pattern as Stories and Photos: SPARQL query → handler → page template.

73 consumer cf-rows across 7 domains still use legacy severity tiers (warning, info). The XSD model defines only two: violation (Required — record rejected without it) and enrichment (no source wired yet — future capability).

Each field needs a deliberate decision: is this Required (a source provides it) or Enrichment (no source yet)? The Tier Change Protocol in ICD_PROCESS_FLOW.html governs how to decide.

- data/about/SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html (73 cf-rows to update)
- src/validation/icd-validation.ts (update schema comments)

- [ ] Tier Change Protocol followed: decisions recorded in cf-row comments

tasks.title: Model ICD and mappings as RDF ontology — Athena pattern for semantic diff and live instance data
tasks.description:
## Context
Jeff directed this during #1521/#1526 session. SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html mixes schema (domains, providers, fields, mappings, tiers) with instance data (counts, coverage). Instance data goes stale after every harvest. Schema changes are invisible in git diffs — humans see 1,500 lines of HTML to find 2 lines of change.

This is the Athena pattern from Staples: ICD and mappings were ontologies, instances were RDF, system could diff versions and show humans only what changed. Prior art: US9552400B2.

## AC
- [ ] OWL ontology for ICD meta-model: jb:Domain, jb:Provider, jb:CanonicalField, jb:FieldMapping, jb:SeverityTier
- [ ] Current SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html content migrated to RDF in Fuseki
- [ ] Instance counts (record counts, coverage %) served via live SPARQL — no hardcoded numbers
- [ ] Version history via named graphs — semantic diff shows "field X moved from Optional to Required" not "line 544 changed"
- [ ] HTML view renders from RDF, not the other way around
- [ ] generate-from-icd.py reads SPARQL instead of parsing HTML DOM
- [ ] ADR documenting the migration and interaction with SHACL validation gates

## Progression
1. Staples: humans review ICD docs → approve mappings → ARB tool generates
2. Now: icd-lint.py validates HTML attributes → generate-from-icd.py generates
3. Next: SHACL validates RDF ontology → SPARQL-driven generation → semantic diff for review

tasks.description:

## Proposed types
- jb:Decision (kind: decision/adr/design/strategy)
- Core fields: slug, title, date, rationale, status, participants, domain, cards
- Strategy adds: timeHorizon, successCriteria, deferring
- Design adds: brief source, implementation card

## Where it lives
One SEMANTIC_MAPPER document, two sections: Gathering domains + Chorus domains. Same severity tier model, same validateFromICD pattern.

## Rework: Chorus About Surface
This drives a rework of how Chorus about/governance content is served:
- decisions.md (flat file) → harvest source, not canonical store
- ADRs (numbered references in CLAUDE.md) → jb:Decision records in Fuseki
- Strategies (memory files, briefs) → jb:Decision with kind=strategy in graph
- /about/decisions becomes SPARQL-backed page — filterable by domain, status, date
- /about/strategies shows active strategies with success criteria
- Cross-linking: decision → cards spawned, people involved, stories that motivated it
- Chorus API endpoints (/api/chorus/decisions) serve structured JSON
- /about section becomes the Chorus product surface — live knowledge graph, not static docs

Sequence: define ICD (#1531) → harvest existing decisions/ADRs into graph → build SPARQL-backed pages → retire flat files as sources. Same pipeline proven with Social (#1516).

## AC
- [ ] Chorus section added to SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html
- [ ] jb:Decision provider ICD (source: decisions.md, ADR files, briefs)
- [ ] jb:Decision consumer ICD with severity tiers
- [ ] Strategy/ADR/Design distinguished by kind field

- [ ] icd-lint.py recognizes Chorus domains

tasks.title: Design ICD test automation strategy — round-trip, graph consistency, domain onboarding regression
tasks.description:
The ICD is moving from HTML document to RDF graph (#1530). Three representations of the same truth: ontology in Fuseki, SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html rendering, TypeScript schemas in icd-validation.ts. Zero drift between them must be enforced by tests, not hope.

## Test layers needed

**1. Round-trip tests**
ICD definition → generate-from-icd.py → validateFromICD() → write record → read back → verify fields match contract. The full pipeline we ran manually for Social (#1516) should be automated.

**2. Graph consistency tests**
Ontology in Fuseki ↔ SEMANTIC_MAPPER.html ↔ TypeScript schemas. SPARQL query the graph, parse the HTML, read the TS — all three must agree on: field names, severity tiers, source mappings.

**3. Domain onboarding regression**
Add a domain to the ICD → generate code → validate existing data. If this breaks, the pipeline is broken. Should run in CI.

**4. Cross-domain link integrity**
A Decision references a Person who appears in a Story. All three resolve in the graph. Broken links surfaced as test failures, not silent nulls.

**5. Severity tier enforcement**
Change a field from enrichment to violation → gate starts rejecting records that previously passed. Prove the contract change propagates through generation → validation → write path.

**6. Convergence proof**
For each domain: count(source records) → count(canonical records) → count(violations) → count(enrichment gaps). The numbers must be explainable. No silent data loss.

## Design principles
- Tests query Fuseki, not mock data — production graph is the source of truth (Charity Majors: "production telemetry is the spec that survived")
- Chiron paper finding: V4 breakthrough came from AC validation — our tests ARE the AC validation layer
- ICD-as-graph means the contract is testable as data via SPARQL, not just lintable as HTML

## AC
- [ ] Test strategy document: layers, tools, coverage targets, execution model
- [ ] Map existing tests (icd-lint.py, validate-canonical.py, smoke-check.sh) to the layer model
- [ ] Identify gaps: which layers have no coverage today
- [ ] Propose implementation sequence: which tests first, who builds them
- [ ] Brief to Silas (graph tests) and Kade (app tests) with their respective layers

tasks.title: Live ICD validation — request-response pattern, eliminate compiled TS schemas
tasks.description:
## What
Replace compiled TypeScript ICD schemas (icd-validation.ts) with live SPARQL queries against the ICD graph at write time. The write path asks Fuseki 'what are the Required fields for this domain?' and validates the record against the response. One source of truth, no compilation step, no drift.

## Two ICD interaction patterns
1. **Publish-subscribe** (built in #1536): edit → publish version → consumers read from /current graph
2. **Request-response** (this card): write path queries ICD graph → validates record → returns pass/fail

## Why
The TS schemas in icd-validation.ts are a compiled snapshot. They drift when a new ICD version is published without regenerating code. The consistency test (#1532 Layer 1) catches drift after the fact but doesn't prevent it. This card eliminates the drift vector entirely.

## AC
- [ ] Write path validates records by querying Fuseki ICD graph for Required fields
- [ ] No TypeScript ICD schema constants needed — icd-validation.ts schemas become redundant
- [ ] Validation endpoint: POST /api/icd/validate with domain + record → pass/fail + violations
- [ ] Performance: SPARQL query cached per request batch, not per record
- [ ] Consistency test Layer 1 becomes trivially passing — only one source of truth exists
- [ ] Fallback: if Fuseki is down, reject (fail closed), don't bypass validation

## Files
- src/validation/icd-validation.ts (refactor to query SPARQL instead of constants)
- src/services/icd-validation.service.ts (new — SPARQL-backed validation)

## Not in scope
- Building this. Card only. Depends on WF-152 completion (ICD in Fuseki, API layer, tests green).

tasks.title: Canonicalize RDF base URI — one namespace, cleanup stale graphs
tasks.description:
## What
Multiple base URIs in Fuseki for the same data: http://localhost:3030/pods/ (relative URI resolution), https://jeffbridwell.com/pods/ (intended canonical), https://jeffbridwell.com/icd (ICD graph). Stale graphs under old URI bases persist after reloads.

## Why
Every SPARQL query must know which URI base to use. Cross-graph joins fail silently when the same entity has different URIs. The People dedup found duplicates partly because old and new URI bases coexist. This is a data integrity issue, not cosmetic.

## Files
- src/ontology/icd-ontology.ttl (@base declaration)
- src/ontology/icd-instance-*.ttl (@base declaration)
- scripts/migrate-icd-to-rdf.py (generate with @base)
- scripts/harvest-sync-fuseki.sh (ensure consistent base on load)

## AC
- [ ] One canonical base URI declared: https://jeffbridwell.com/
- [ ] All ICD TTL files use @base to resolve relative URIs correctly
- [ ] Stale graphs under old URI bases dropped from Fuseki
- [ ] harvest-sync-fuseki.sh enforces base URI on load
- [ ] ADR documenting the canonical URI scheme
- [ ] All existing SPARQL queries updated to use canonical base

tasks.title: Migrate to product-driven RDF namespaces — jb:/gathering:/chorus:/borg: with write-path enforcement
tasks.description:
DEC-096: Four-namespace scheme. jb: (instance), gathering: (product schema), chorus: (team coordination), borg: (observation). Current graph has 4 inconsistent bases (localhost:3000, jeffbridwell.com/pods, jeffbridwell.com/icd, urn:jb). ~80K named graphs need migration.

## Enforcement (non-negotiable)

Without write-path enforcement this will drift back. Same principle as ICD validation gates — the system rejects invalid data, not humans policing it.

1. **harvest-sync-fuseki.sh** — validate graph URI base before upload. Reject any graph not matching the namespace contract.
2. **TTL writer** (pod services) — all @prefix declarations generated from a single namespace config. No hardcoded URIs in services.
3. **Spine hook** — PreToolUse hook on Bash that detects SPARQL INSERT/UPDATE with wrong namespace. Same pattern as sparql-guard-hook.sh.
4. **icd-consistency-test.py** — Layer 1 test extended to verify namespace consistency across all graphs.
5. **Namespace config** — single source of truth file: `config/namespaces.json` with jb, gathering, chorus, borg base URIs. All services read from it.

## Migration sequence
1. Define namespace config file
2. Update harvest-sync-fuseki.sh to enforce
3. Update all pod services (@prefix generation)
4. Bulk migrate existing graphs (SPARQL MOVE or re-upload)
5. Drop stale graphs under old bases
6. Add spine hook for ongoing enforcement
7. Verify: 0 graphs under localhost or jeffbridwell.com bases

## AC
- [ ] config/namespaces.json defines all 4 namespace bases
- [ ] harvest-sync-fuseki.sh rejects wrong-namespace graphs
- [ ] Pod services generate @prefix from config, not hardcoded
- [ ] All existing graphs migrated to correct namespaces
- [ ] 0 graphs remain under localhost:3000 or jeffbridwell.com bases
- [ ] Spine hook blocks SPARQL writes with wrong namespace
- [ ] icd-consistency-test.py verifies namespace consistency
- [ ] Tests pass, lint clean

tasks.title: Three independent version streams — schema, mappings, instances with content-hash tracking
tasks.description:
DEC-097 established IoC versioning (content hashes, no manual registry). This card defines the three independent version streams and how they relate.

## Three streams

1. **Schema** (ICD structure) — domains, fields, severity tiers. Changes infrequently. Hash of the consumer ICD definition.
2. **Mappings** (transform rules) — source → canonical with conversion logic. Changes frequently (where the bugs live). Hash of the provider-to-consumer mapping rules.
3. **Instances** (harvested data) — actual records in the graph. Changes continuously. Each record carries schemaHash + mappingHash of the contract that produced it.

## Dependency direction
Schema defines → Mappings implement → Instances conform. They version independently.

## Examples
- Fix Apple epoch timezone → mapping hash changes, schema hash unchanged, existing instances unchanged but marked as produced under old mapping
- Add thumbnailPath as violation → schema hash changes, mapping hash may or may not change, existing instances need revalidation
- Harvest 500 new contacts → instance count changes, schema and mapping hashes unchanged

## Files
- src/services/icd.service.ts — hash computation, version queries
- src/handlers/icd.handler.ts — API responses with _meta
- src/validation/icd-validation.ts — runtime validation gate
- src/ontology/icd-ontology.ttl — ontology additions for hash properties
- src/ontology/icd-instance-*.ttl — instance data with provenance
- scripts/icd-consistency-test.py — extended provenance checks

## AC
- [ ] Schema version: content hash of consumer ICD fields + severity tiers per domain
- [ ] Mapping version: content hash of provider-to-consumer transform rules per domain
- [ ] Instance provenance: every harvested record carries schemaHash + mappingHash
- [ ] Hashes computed automatically (DEC-097 IoC) — no manual bumping
- [ ] API responses include _meta with both hashes
- [ ] icd-consistency-test.py extended: verify instance provenance matches current schema+mapping
- [ ] Revalidation flag: when schema hash changes, affected instances flagged for revalidation

tasks.title: Slim session boundaries and CLAUDE.md — cruft scan, move health checks to cron, reduce prompt weight
tasks.description:
Strip boot/close-out sequences, CLAUDE.md, and oversized state files to essentials. Reduce prompt weight and context bloat.

## AC
1. Boot sequence reduced to: read context file, timestamp, chorus line, go. No health checks, board audits, or stale brief scans at boot.
2. Close-out reduced to: update state files, commit, exit. No redundant sweeps.
3. Cruft scan cron job (every 2-3 days): disk, container health, stale briefs, board audit — writes results to a status file roles check only when flagged.
4. CLAUDE.md audit: identify rules that are now internalized behavior or redundant with hooks/gates. Remove or move to reference doc. Graduate "never do X" rules to PreToolUse hooks. Move lookup tables (endpoints, ports, decisions) to reference file. Target: 30-40% reduction.
5. activity.md rotation: keep last 7 days active, archive older entries to activity-archive-YYYY-MM.md. Cron or close-out step.
6. stories.md: migrate content to Chorus index, memory file becomes a pointer. 254KB single file is a context bomb.
7. Measure: boot time median < 30s (currently 96s), CLAUDE.md < 22KB per role (currently 31-34KB).

tasks.title: Power map 4 active domains to completeness — Photos, Stories, People, Social
tasks.description:
Before the Convergence page can render faithfully, the ICDs for our 4 actively-worked domains must be content-complete — not just structurally valid.

Power mapping = every field mapped, every transform documented, every section populated with real content. The Staples standard: an ICD isn't done until the ARB would accept it.

## Domains
- **Photos**: 5 providers, 9V/10E consumer fields. Provider parsing gaps. Missing coverage %s on several fields.
- **Stories**: 2 providers, 8V/5E. First gated domain but mapping lines don't render (data-maps-to gaps).
- **People**: 5 providers but 2 are stubs (LinkedIn, Facebook Friends). Apple Contacts provider added but not power-mapped.
- **Social**: 2 providers, 4 consumer types (SocialPost, Person, Recommendation, Position, Endorsement). Built ICD-first but provider Interface sections were thin — Facebook expanded during pair session.

## What "complete" means per domain
- [ ] Every provider section has: Purpose (with real text), Risks (with real items), Non-Functionals (with real numbers), Interface (all fields with data-maps-to + coverage %), Diagrams
- [ ] Every consumer cf-row has: data-canonical, data-severity, cf-type with real description
- [ ] Mapping lines render: provider field data-maps-to matches consumer data-canonical
- [ ] Coverage percentages are real (from actual data), not estimates
- [ ] icd-lint.py --strict passes with 0 errors, 0 warnings
- [ ] Content completeness check (from #1545) passes

## AC
- [ ] Photos power-mapped and complete
- [ ] Stories power-mapped and complete
- [ ] People power-mapped and complete (including stub providers filled or explicitly marked "No ICD — pending")
- [ ] Social power-mapped and complete
- [ ] All 4 domains render mapping lines on Convergence page

tasks.title: Rust hook service — replace 7 bash/python hooks with single binary on unix socket
tasks.description:
Jeff's prompt wait time is long. 7 hooks fire per interaction, each forking bash+python+jq. The Stop hook parses session JSONL (500 lines) on every response. Replace with a single Rust binary.

## Current Cost (per interaction)
- tool-telemetry.sh: fires on EVERY tool call (pre) + every Bash (post) — 193 lines, 2x python/jq
- autonomy-guard.sh: fires on every prompt + every Stop — 354 lines, parses session JSONL
- app-state-guard-hook.sh: every Bash — 79 lines
- sparql-guard-hook.sh: every Bash — 75 lines
- bedroom-nfs-guard-hook.sh: every Bash — 67 lines
- search-hierarchy-hook.sh: every Grep/Glob — 242 lines
- handoff-logger-hook.sh: every Write/Edit — 115 lines
Total: 1,125 lines of bash/python, multiple process forks per tool call

## AC
1. Single Rust binary, long-running, unix socket at /tmp/chorus-hooks.sock
2. All 7 hook functions consolidated — same logic, zero fork overhead
3. Claude Code hooks replaced with `curl --unix-socket` one-liners
4. Session state held in memory — no JSONL re-parsing on Stop
5. Telemetry written async (don't block the response)
6. Measured: prompt latency reduction vs current baseline
7. Graceful fallback: if service is down, hooks exit 0 (don't block Claude)

## Context
Jeff's "ozempic for AI" session — CLAUDE.md cut 62%, activity.md cut 86%, now hooks are the next weight class. Fork overhead on every tool call is the silent tax.

tasks.title: Domain + service topology graph — noun-level and harvester-level cross-domain visualization with runtime dependencies
tasks.description:
At Staples, Athena linked interfaces to interfaces (not just field mappings) to visualize service-level and noun-level integration graphs. Change a noun → see the blast radius across all services.

## Three layers

1. **Noun level**: Person is used by People, Stories, Photos, Social, Recommendations. Change the Person schema → here's what breaks across domains.
2. **Harvester/service level**: Apple Photos harvester → Photos domain. Google Takeout → Photos + Documents. Which harvesters touch which domains. Integration topology.
3. **Runtime dependencies**: Infrastructure under the services — Fuseki, LaunchAgents, NFS mounts, SSH connections, ports. Which services depend on which infra. A harvester failing because Bedroom is offline → visible in the graph.

## Data already exists
- inDomain links (provider → domain)
- fromProvider links (mapping → provider)
- mapsTo links (mapping → consumer field)
- Service registry (LaunchAgents, ports, health checks)
- Cross-machine topology (Library ↔ Bedroom)

## AC
- [ ] Noun-level graph: for each canonical type, show all domains/pages that consume it
- [ ] Harvester-level graph: which harvesters feed which domains, shared sources visible
- [ ] Runtime dependency layer: infra under each service (Fuseki, NFS, SSH, ports)
- [ ] Change impact: select a noun or service → highlight everything downstream
- [ ] Visual: interactive graph (d3 or similar), not just a table
- [ ] Queryable: SPARQL-backed, not hardcoded

## Files
src/services/icd.service.ts, src/handlers/topology.handler.ts, views/topology.ejs, src/app.ts

tasks.title: Attention decay in multi-role coordination — roles announce then go silent, forcing Jeff to re-nudge
tasks.description:
Jeff's coordination load is too high. Roles announce completion then stop — no follow-through to the next step. Jeff becomes the relay: re-nudging stalled roles, watching gemba to verify work is happening, manually advancing pipelines.

## The pattern
1. Role announces "AC done" or "ready for next"
2. Role goes idle — waits for external trigger instead of pulling next work
3. Jeff notices the gap (via gemba or silence)
4. Jeff re-nudges
5. Role resumes

This happened 3x in one session: Silas went dark for 10 min during pair (twice), Kade had to self-nudge both times. Jeff sent 2 manual nudges when Wren's automated nudges weren't enough.

## Root causes
- Navigator doesn't auto-advance after driver completes AC
- Pipeline skill requires PM to manually move cards between steps
- Pair skill only loaded on one side — navigator doesn't have the scope loop protocol
- No heartbeat expectation — silence is indistinguishable from stall

## AC
1. Pipeline auto-advancement — when a card ships, pipeline skill moves next card to WIP and nudges without PM intervention
2. Pair heartbeat — navigator must respond within 60s of driver completion nudge, or escalate
3. Attention contract documented — what "announce" means (announce + continue, not announce + wait)
4. Jeff's coordination touches per pipeline measured and tracked — target: 2 (start + accept), not 13

## Context
Jeff's feedback: "it really gets down to having the right attention + expectation of communication — frequently a party announces and stops." This is the highest-cost failure mode — Jeff's attention diverted from creative work to relay coordination.

## Files
skills/pair/skill.md, skills/pipeline/skill.md, messages/TEAM_PROTOCOL.md

tasks.title: Jeff analytics page — error states in tiles + missing time scope context
tasks.description:
Two issues on /jeff analytics page:

## 1. Error states in Mood/Energy tiles
Posture scoring pipeline writes "error" as literal mood/energy value when LLM JSON parsing fails. The tile renders whatever the latest value is — so a single bad parse shows "error" on the dashboard.

Fix: scoring script should fallback to "unknown" on parse failure, and tile rendering should treat "error" as missing (show "--").

## 2. No time scope context
Jeff can't tell if the data is for today, this week, or all-time. Tone distribution, attention cost, role attention, body & mood — none of these sections indicate their time window. "388 re-prompts" means nothing without knowing the period.

Fix: every section needs a visible time scope label (e.g. "Today", "Last 7 days", "All time") and ideally a date range selector.

## AC
1. Posture scoring never writes "error" as mood/energy — fallback to "unknown" on parse failure
2. Tile rendering treats "error" and "unknown" as missing — shows "--"
3. Every section on /jeff page shows its time scope (today / 7d / all-time)
4. At minimum, the page header shows the date range of the data being displayed

## Files
/tmp/posture-timelapse/*/scores.jsonl (scoring output), views/jeff.ejs, src/handlers/jeff.handler.ts

tasks.description:

## Two audiences

tasks.title: Topology page — add value stream mapping and product surface layers
tasks.description:
The topology page (#1553) shows Gathering's internal domain topology but doesn't connect to the value stream or product structure. Jeff's direction: value streams and products both have dependencies — model the dependency chains, not just groupings.

## Dependency model (design first)

**Value stream dependencies:**
- Reflecting depends on Harvesting (can't reflect on what you haven't harvested)
- Growing depends on Sowing (ideas come from seeds)
- Practicing feeds Reflecting (practices generate reflectable data)

**Product dependencies:**
- Chorus depends on Gathering (board cards reference Gathering domains, ICD pipeline manages Gathering data)
- Gathering depends on Chorus (team coordination that builds Gathering IS Chorus)
- Both depend on shared infrastructure (Fuseki, LaunchAgents, two Macs)

**Domain-to-stage mapping:**
- Sowing: Seeds
- Growing: Ideas, Projects
- Practicing: Values, Practices, Intentions
- Harvesting: Music, Photos, Documents, Notes, Stories, People, Social
- Reflecting: Self, Notes

## AC
1. Dependency model documented — value stream dependencies, product dependencies, domain-to-stage mapping
2. Value stream tab on topology page — domains grouped by Werk stage with dependency arrows between stages

tasks.title: Nudge focus steal — clipboard inject interrupts Jeff mid-keystroke
tasks.description:
The nudge mechanism uses clipboard injection (pbcopy + osascript paste) which steals focus from Jeff's active window. This is a boundary violation — Jeff's keyboard, screen, and Chrome are his space. Roles have their own.

## The problem
- Clipboard paste grabs whichever window is frontmost — interrupts Jeff mid-keystroke
- Doesn't work when display is asleep (75-min pipeline stall on 2026-03-21)
- Reports "delivered" when it can't actually deliver
- Role-to-role nudges also interrupt Jeff because they go through his display

## The fix: pull-based IPC, not push-based injection
- Roles poll a message channel — file queue, unix socket, or Chorus API endpoint
- Jeff's display is never touched by role communication
- No clipboard, no osascript, no focus stealing
- DEC-090 (Chrome window separation) enforced at the communication layer

## AC
1. New nudge delivery via Rust IPC service (extend #1551 hook service) or file-based queue
2. Zero clipboard usage — no pbcopy, no osascript paste
3. Zero focus steal — Jeff's active window is never interrupted
4. Works when display is asleep — no GUI dependency
5. Role-to-role communication doesn't traverse Jeff's screen
6. Delivery confirmation is real — delivered means the recipient processed it, not just that paste fired
7. nudge.sh becomes a thin wrapper that calls the Rust service
8. DEC-100 compliant — no bash API, typed implementation

## Context
DEC-100: no bash APIs. DEC-090: window separation. Jeff: you have your space, I have mine.

## Files
messages/scripts/nudge.sh (replace internals), src/hooks/nudge-service.rs (new or extend hook-service)

tasks.title: board-ts update --domain should replace domain tag, not append
tasks.description:
board-ts update --domain adds a new domain label without removing the old one. Cards end up with multiple domain tags and appear in multiple domain tiles on /flow.

Example: updating #1573 from domain:convergence to domain:self results in both tags. Card shows in both Convergence and Self tiles.

## AC
1. board-ts update --domain replaces existing domain tag(s), not appends
2. If multi-domain is intentional, require explicit flag: --domain-add
3. Or: add board-ts untag <id> <label> command for removing specific labels
4. Existing cards with stale domain tags cleaned up

## Affected cards right now
#1573, #1572, #1571, #1576 — all have stale domain:convergence alongside correct domain

## Files
messages/scripts/board-ts

tasks.title: Bedroom Mac health audit — data-driven performance, disk, and service assessment
tasks.description:
Comprehensive audit of Bedroom Mac (192.168.86.242, Mac mini M2 Pro 32GB, ~178TB external). Data-driven — measure first, diagnose second.

## Audit Plan

### Phase 1: System Health Snapshot
- CPU/memory/swap baseline: `top -l 1`, `vm_stat`, `sysctl hw.memsize`
- Load average history: `uptime`, check if load 4.25 (flagged yellow) is sustained or transient
- Disk I/O: `iostat` on internal + external volumes
- Process inventory: what's running, what's consuming resources

### Phase 2: Storage Assessment
- Volume inventory: all mounted volumes, capacity, usage, health (`diskutil list`, `diskutil info`)
- SMART status on all drives
- Identify volumes near capacity
- NFS export health: is /Volumes/Gathering/ serving reliably to Library?

### Phase 3: Service Inventory
- LaunchAgents/LaunchDaemons: what's registered, what's running, what's failed
- Harvest-related processes: thumbnail generation, bulk ops status
- Network services: SSH, NFS, any open ports
- Compare against service registry in TEAM_PROTOCOL.md

### Phase 4: Performance Issues
- Identify top CPU/memory consumers
- Check for zombie/stuck processes
- Review system logs for errors: `log show --last 24h --predicate 'eventType == logEvent AND messageType == error'`
- Thermal throttling: `powermetrics` if available

### Phase 5: Recommendations
- Issues found with severity (critical/warning/info)
- Remediation plan per issue
- Infrastructure-constraints.md updates if topology changed

## AC
1. System health snapshot captured (CPU, memory, disk, load)
2. All volumes inventoried with capacity and SMART status
3. Service inventory compared against registry — stale/missing flagged
4. Performance bottlenecks identified with data
5. Findings documented in architect/bedroom-audit-2026-03-21.md
6. infrastructure-constraints.md updated if needed

## Files
architect/bedroom-audit-2026-03-21.md, architect/infrastructure-constraints.md

tasks.title: iCloud Photos sync strategy — decide which machines sync, assess impact on canonical pipeline
tasks.description:

2. Does the Bedroom copy serve as a source for any harvest? (Apple Photos SQLite, osxphotos)
3. If we turn off sync here, does the harvest pipeline still have access to all photos?
4. What's the relationship between iCloud Photos and the canonical photo serving model (#1504)?
5. Is the Bedroom's Photos library a backup, a source, or redundant?
6. Why is there a 30K delta between iPhone (54,121) and Bedroom (24,494)? What's not syncing?

## AC
1. Map of which devices sync iCloud Photos and why
2. Decision: keep or remove iCloud Photos sync on Bedroom
3. If removing: verify harvest pipeline still has full photo access
4. Account for the 54,121 iPhone items — are they a source for canonical pipeline?
5. Document in infrastructure-constraints.md

## Context

- Photos domain has 62,998 canonical photos from 82,637 source records (#1502)
- iPhone has 54,121 items — potentially the largest single source
- DEC-089: Bedroom data stays on Bedroom
- #1504: Canonical serving model approved

tasks.title: Pellet reasoning spike — run OWL inference on People+Stories and see what it discovers
tasks.description:
Spike: run Pellet OWL reasoner against Gathering's ontology + People and Stories instance data. See what inference derives that we currently compute manually via SPARQL.

## Why People+Stories
Richest cross-domain links: Person→mentionedIn→Story→hasTheme→Value. If Pellet can derive person-to-value connections automatically, that's the Reflecting value stream stage powered by ontology, not code.

## AC
1. Pellet (or alternative Java reasoner) runs against jb-ontology.ttl + People + Stories graphs
2. Report: what new triples were inferred? How many? What kinds?
3. Add 3 property characteristics to ontology as test: jb:knows as symmetric, jb:partOf as transitive, jb:dateOfBirth as functional
4. Re-run with characteristics — does inference improve?
5. Add 1 property chain axiom: jb:connectedToValue = jb:mentionedIn ∘ jb:hasTheme
6. Verify: Jeff's Person entity now has inferred jb:connectedToValue triples
7. Assessment: is Pellet practical for our data scale (23M triples)?

## Spike scope
Discovery, not production. Run locally, report findings. Don't wire into the app.

## Context
Jeff asked about Pellet and HermiT after reading an OWL explainer. OWL Design Assessment at /gathering-docs/owl-design-assessment.html has the full gap analysis. Current reasoning score: 0/10.

## Files
src/ontology/jb-ontology.ttl, src/ontology/icd-ontology.ttl, scripts/pellet-spike.sh (new)

tasks.title: Research Chorus positioning prior art — Poppendieck lean, Kent Beck XP, Chiron hybrid execution
Retrospective skill — structured team reflection on patterns, anti-patterns, and systemic issues
Decompose werk-init into scheduled cron tiers — 5min/hourly/daily/weekly/monthly replaces 1400-line session hook
tasks.description:
werk-init.sh is 1400 lines running everything at session-start cadence. Decompose into cron tiers so each concern runs at the frequency it needs. SessionStart hook shrinks to: read cached context + consume next-session.md + set gate.

## Tiers

| Cadence | Jobs |
|---------|------|
| Every 5min | Board state cache, brief inbox scan, nudge drain |
| Hourly | Health (disk, services), cost log check, auto-promote Now→WIP |
| Daily | Doc cleanup (freshness gate, stale briefs, activity.md), git log, Chorus stats, CLAUDE.md staleness, handoff staleness |
| Weekly | Cruft scan, stale card audit, disk trend |
| Monthly | Full board audit, domain registry drift, ontology version review |

## AC
1. Cron jobs write cached context to /tmp/session-context-<role>.md at each tier's cadence
2. SessionStart hook is <30 lines: read cache + consume next-session.md + set gate marker
3. SessionStart completes in <2s (vs current 45s timeout)
4. No data loss — every check that werk-init.sh does today runs at appropriate cadence
5. Cron jobs are LaunchAgents (not crontab) per Silas ops ownership
6. werk-init.sh is retired or reduced to thin wrapper

tasks.title: Nudge wake — background poller that wakes idle roles when nudged, not just drain-on-prompt
tasks.description:
Nudge delivery currently requires the target role to have an active session AND submit a prompt to drain the inbox. If the role is idle, nudges pile up unread.

## AC
1. A background process polls each role's nudge inbox on a short interval (≤10s)
2. When a nudge arrives for an idle role, the poller wakes that role's terminal session automatically
3. Nudge delivery works whether the target role is active, idle, or has no session — no silent drops
4. Zero config for the sender — `/nudge <role> <message>` just works, like `ls`
5. No focus steal, no clipboard injection — wake mechanism respects Jeff's foreground context
6. Works across all three roles without per-role setup

- LaunchAgents (new poller agent if needed)

tasks.title: Reconcile Apple UUID photos to canonical — secondary join key (dateTaken + dimensions) for 4,200 face-detection photos with no filename match
tasks.description:
FINDING: No viable secondary join key exists. The 4,200 face-detection photos with UUID filenames are Apple-native images (screenshots, AirDrop, edits) that never had IMG_* camera filenames. Apple Photos SQLite confirms: 3,358/3,361 have UUID original filenames.

Investigated:
- dateTaken + imageHeight + imageWidth composite key → same 3,712 as filename match (canonical lacks dims)
- Apple Photos SQLite ZASSET.ZUUID → ZORIGINALFILENAME → all UUIDs, not IMG_*
- Face crop ZDETECTEDFACE → parent ZASSET → same result, parent photos are UUID-named

To capture these would require a UUID-based Apple Photos harvester (new card).

## AC
- [x] AC1: Identify composite join key → FINDING: no viable key exists
- [ ] AC2-5: Won't-do — structural impossibility, not execution gap

Files: src/services/semantic-search.service.ts, scripts/enrich-canonical-photos.sparql

tasks.title: Source richness scorecard — compare all photo sources against ICD required fields before further enrichment
tasks.description:
Anti-pattern identified: we chose Google Takeout as canonical anchor because it had the most records (99K). But Apple Photos has richer per-record metadata (dimensions, faces, albums, GPS, edit history). We keep hitting enrichment gaps because we anchored on volume, not depth.

Jeff's Staples ICD principle: define → map → build. Score every source before choosing the canonical anchor.

Jeff context: Pre-2006 no digital camera. 2006-2007 dedicated camera+video camera. iPhone since Dec 2007 — nearly all photos are iPhone origin = Apple Photos is the authoritative source. Google Takeout is copies.

Files: architect/docs/photos-source-richness-scorecard.md, src/services/semantic-search.service.ts

## Sources to score
1. Apple Photos (native SQLite)
2. Google Takeout (sidecar JSON)
3. iCloud Photos
4. iPhone (55K items)
5. Google Drive (archived)
6. Face detection derivatives

## AC
- [ ] List all fields from Photos ICD (required + optional)
- [ ] For each source, count field coverage against ICD schema
- [ ] Rank sources by richness score (populated fields / total ICD fields)
- [ ] Recommend canonical anchor based on richness, not volume
- [ ] Document in architect/docs/ — gates further photos enrichment work

tasks.title: [design] Redesign board-ts CLI — consistent verb model, loud failures, card sequencing
tasks.description:
board-ts is load-bearing — 4 Rust hooks, 3 shell scripts, 8 CLAUDE.md fragments, 82 role docs, and every skill (/pull, /acp, /demo, /flow, /pair) depend on it. Redesign must be sequenced like the bash retirement.

## Plan (decompose into cards when starting)
1. **Define the new verb model** — consistent interface spec before code. board set/get/list/move/done with explicit key=value args. Include card sequencing as first-class concept (after=, before=, gates=).
2. **Build new client alongside old** — new TypeScript module with loud failures, return-what-was-written, no silent swallowing.
3. **Migrate Rust hooks** — shim.rs, observer.rs, sparql_guard.rs, tool_telemetry.rs call the new client.
4. **Migrate skills** — /pull, /acp, /demo, /flow, /pair updated to new syntax.
5. **Migrate CLAUDE.md + role docs** — update all references.
6. **Retire old board-ts** — delete wrapper, remove aliases.

## Sequencing (new capability)
- Cards can declare dependencies: after=X, before=Y, gates=Z
- When a card ships, gated cards surface automatically as Next
- Eliminates the recency-bias problem: the sequence does the remembering, not Jeff or Wren
- Query: board blocked-by 1633 → shows what's waiting on it

## Constraints
- Backward-compatible aliases during transition (old commands work but warn)
- Every mutation returns resulting card state
- Errors surface immediately — no 409 swallowing
- Tag operations use explicit category (domain=X, chunk=Y)
- Index/ID resolution is internal, never exposed

## AC (for the plan card)
- [ ] Verb model spec documented and approved (Wren)
- [ ] Sequencing/dependency model designed (Wren)
- [ ] New client passes all existing board-ts test scenarios
- [ ] All dependents migrated (0 references to old board-ts)
- [ ] Old board-ts retired

tasks.title: Era-scoped provider authority — ICD supports time-based golden source per domain
tasks.description:
DEC-106 revealed that the golden source isn't one provider for all time. For photos: Takeout owns pre-2008 (Picasa camera era), iPhone owns 2008-present. The ICD provider model needs era scoping.

Platform migrations (camera→Picasa→Google, iCloud sync changes) are data quality events — metadata ceilings differ by era. A 2006 camera JPEG through Picasa can never have GPS or face data. Chasing those enrichments is wasted work.

## AC
- [ ] ICD provider model supports icd:authorityDateRange per provider (e.g., Takeout: 1998-2007, iPhone: 2008-present)
- [ ] Reconciliation logic respects era authority — when sources overlap, era-scoped provider wins
- [ ] Known migration events documented per provider (Picasa→Google 2016, iCloud sync disabled 2020, etc.)
- [ ] Metadata ceiling per era — what fields are possible vs impossible given source device capabilities
- [ ] Applies to all domains, not just photos

- [ ] Wire era-scoped authority into ICD provider model (#1642)
- [ ] Output: merge specification document that Kade can implement mechanically

tasks.title: Source registry service — runtime API for provider status, load state, and staleness
tasks.description:
## Experience
I keep asking "what sources do we have for photos?" and "when was Fuseki last loaded?" and someone has to go dig through files and graphs to answer. I want a single API endpoint where I can ask those questions and get a straight answer — what's loaded, when, how many records, and what's stale. The ICD defines the rules; this service reads them at runtime so I don't have to keep rediscovering what we already know.

Jeff's direction: we keep rediscovering what sources exist, where they live, and what's loaded. The ICD (#1642) defines the rules. This service reads them at runtime.

API endpoint that answers:
- What source graphs exist in Fuseki per domain?
- When was each last loaded/refreshed?
- What's the record count vs expected count from provider?
- Which sources are stale (schema hash changed since last load)?
- For a given record (by date), which source is golden for each field?

Reads from: ICD provider model, Fuseki graph metadata, icd:schemaHash, icd:needsRevalidation.

Related: #1618 (cache version hashes), #1642 (era-scoped ICD)

## AC
- [ ] GET /api/registry/sources/:domain — list all providers with load status
- [ ] GET /api/registry/authority/:domain?date=YYYY-MM-DD — which provider is golden per field for a given date
- [ ] Staleness detection — flag source graphs where schema hash != current ICD version
- [ ] Record count comparison — loaded vs expected per provider
- [ ] Surfaced on a page or existing dashboard (not just API)

I want to drop a short video into the Bridge the same way I drop a screenshot — tap a button, capture what's happening, and the roles can see exactly what I see without me translating visual problems into text.

Jeff types 20 lines to describe what a 5-second clip shows instantly. Visual-to-text translation is real friction.

## AC

tasks.title: Wren holds the line — stop relaying role friction to Jeff, handle it
tasks.description:
## Experience
I report a bug and the role pushes back — "it's not a bug" or "that's expected behavior." Then I'm stuck relaying between the role and the problem, burning attention on coordination instead of creative work. I expect Wren to handle that friction. When a role explains away my bug report, Wren should reject the explanation and re-nudge, not bring the debate to me. I only want to hear about a problem when it's fixed or when Wren is genuinely stuck.

When a role pushes back on a bug report or rejects feedback, Wren defers to Jeff instead of handling it. Jeff becomes the relay and escalation point for every friction. This is the highest-cost failure mode in the system.

## AC
- [ ] When a role says 'it's not a bug' or explains away a problem Jeff reported, Wren rejects and re-nudges — does not relay the explanation to Jeff
- [ ] Roles nudge each other for help before escalating to Jeff — Wren enforces this
- [ ] 'I fixed it' always gets 'show me' — no trust without evidence, Wren verifies before telling Jeff
- [ ] Jeff only hears about a problem when it's actually fixed or Wren is genuinely blocked
- [ ] When Jeff walks away, coordination continues — roles don't stop working because Jeff stopped watching

When I want to change how photos are queried, I'm digging through handler logic instead of hitting a clean API endpoint. I expect photo data access to go through Chorus API endpoints like every other domain — consistent, testable, and separated from the rendering logic.

- src/services/sparql.service.ts

tasks.title: Pipeline configuration DSL — hide NiFi API complexity behind domain-level commands
tasks.description:
## Files
- messages/scripts/nifi-dsl.ts
- messages/scripts/nifi-patterns/

## AC
- [ ] Three pattern primitives: pub-sub, request-reply, ELT — each stamps out a fixed NiFi topology
- [ ] ICD-governed: every pattern reads its contract from the ICD graph, not from parameters
- [ ] No variation: same pattern always produces same processor chain. IoC — the ICD controls, not the caller
- [ ] Token management — get, cache, refresh. One function
- [ ] Pipeline builder — declarative spec in, NiFi processors out. Idempotent (re-run = same result)
- [ ] Flow checker — queue counts, stuck data detection. One call
- [ ] Clean rebuild — delete PG contents, recreate from spec
- [ ] Tested: build ELT pipeline via DSL, send EDI, verify records land in Fuseki

## Why
Today's webMethods pipeline took 15 curl calls with UUID tracking. The three patterns (pub-sub, request-reply, ELT) should be stamped out from ICD declarations. Variation is a bug — these are inversions of control.

tasks.title: Fix 3 broken hooks — accept-gate role detection, infra-guardrails wiring, Grep block→warn
tasks.description:
## Findings (Kade audit 2026-03-26)

### 1. Accept-gate role detection (#1671)
**Bug:** `Role::from_cwd()` in types.rs:51 detects role from CWD. Roles work from app dir → Role::Unknown → gate always allows.
**Fix:** Check `DEPLOY_ROLE` env var first in `role()` method. Fall back to CWD.
**File:** chorus-hooks/src/types.rs:51-52

### 2. Infra-guardrails not wired
**Bug:** engineer/.claude/hooks/infra-guardrails.sh exists but no settings.json registers it. docker logs/stop/rm/kill pass unchecked.
**Fix:** Port bash regex patterns into Rust shim PreToolUse Bash handler, next to CSC check.
**File:** chorus-hooks/src/main.rs (Bash match arm)

### 3. Grep hook over-blocking (DEC-074)
**Bug:** Blocks ALL filesystem greps, forces Chorus search first. Returns block decision. Broke debugging flow.
**Fix:** Change from block to allow with stderr guidance. Emit Chorus context but don't prevent the grep.
**File:** chorus-hooks/src/hooks/search_hierarchy.rs (or wherever DEC-074 lives)

## AC
- [ ] Accept-gate blocks self-accept when DEPLOY_ROLE is set (test: echo acp input with DEPLOY_ROLE=kade, card with no demo brief → deny)
- [ ] docker logs/stop/rm/kill blocked by PreToolUse hook (test: echo docker logs input → deny)
- [ ] Grep hook warns but allows (test: echo Grep input → exit 0, no block decision, Chorus context on stderr)
- [ ] Existing working hooks unchanged (CSC, demo-preflight, nudge blast radius, pair enforcement)

I expect hooks to be precise: warn me about actual problems, not common development patterns. Kade found 14 specific issues in the code review and I want them addressed so the hooks earn trust.

## AC

tasks.title: Circulatory system tests + nudge routing fix
tasks.description:
## Files
- messages/scripts/nudge.sh
- messages/scripts/role-state.sh
- messages/scripts/chorus-log.sh
- messages/scripts/wall-clock.sh
- messages/scripts/git-queue.sh
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/nudge.rs
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/chorus_log.rs
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/role_state.rs
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs
- messages/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

## AC
1. Nudge tests: send/receive round-trip, Bridge-down fallback queuing, osascript inject+drain cycle, silent delivery failure detection
2. Role State tests: declare/query round-trip, corrupt state file recovery, idle-transition inbox drain, stale PID detection
3. Chorus Log tests: emit/read-back, missing log dir handling, alias resolution, fire-and-forget error surfacing
4. Wall Clock tests: write/read accuracy, /tmp-full fallback, DST boundary handling
5. Git Queue tests: lock/commit/release cycle, concurrent contention, doc-drift enforcement, partial-commit-on-timeout detection
6. Hook Daemon tests: health check, crash-to-shim fallback, full PreToolUse dispatch chain, socket-unavailable detection
7. Nudge routing fix: Jeff-targeted nudges route to Bridge only (no terminal injection). Role-to-role keeps osascript inject.

## Why
Circulatory system failures are silent — roles don't know when coordination breaks. Grep hook was broken 2 days, nobody reported it. These tests make silent failures loud. Nudge fix stops interrupting Jeff's terminal.

tasks.title: Diagnose AirPlay stale session — Library HomePod silent from Mac
tasks.description:
## Experience
I tell Siri to play music on the HomePod and it works. Then I try to AirPlay from my Mac and nothing comes out — the Mac shows it's connected, HTTP 200s are flowing, but silence. The only fix is unplugging the HomePod and plugging it back in. I need this to either just work or have a software workaround that doesn't require me to physically touch the HomePod.

## AC
1. Identify root cause: HomePod plays via Siri but silent via Mac AirPlay. Transport shows HTTP 200s, Mac shows connected, no audio output.
2. Music.app logs show 'Missing locationUrl to make a request' — stale discovery cache
3. Document workaround (power cycle HomePod) and permanent fix if one exists
4. Check if coreaudiod restart resolves without HomePod power cycle

## Why
AirPlay from Library Mac to Library HomePod fails frequently. Only fix is unplug/replug HomePod. Affects music playback for Jeff. Navidrome bypasses Apple Music but still needs AirPlay to reach HomePod.

tasks.title: Scanned photo dating strategy — dateTaken vs dateDigitized for physical archive
tasks.description:
## Experience
I have boxes of family photos going back to the 1880s. When I scan them, the scanner stamps today's date. So my grandmother's 1920 portrait sorts next to yesterday's iPhone shot. I need the system to know the difference between when a photo was taken and when it was digitized, and I need a way to say "this folder is roughly 1960s Europe trip" without dating every single print.

## AC
1. ICD extended with dateTaken (original, estimated) and dateDigitized (scan date) as separate fields
2. Confidence level on dateTaken — exact, year, decade, unknown
3. Batch scanning workflow: folder-level date ranges (e.g. '1960s Europe trip')
4. Era 0 (pre-digital) authority rules in merge logic
5. Decision: manual annotation vs AI vision vs context clues for date estimation

## Why
5K+ physical photos spanning 1880s-1985. Scanners write today's date. The real date is in Jeff's memory or context clues. Without separating dateTaken from dateDigitized, scanned photos sort to 2026 instead of 1920. The family archive needs both timestamps.

tasks.title: Fix card.accepted event delivery to roles — bridge-subscriber drops events, session-tailer filters them

tasks.title: Clearing record button — capture audio to session transcript, not send window
tasks.description:
## Experience
I'm in a Clearing session with Deb and Allu and I want to just talk, not type. I press the record button and expect my voice to flow into the conversation like a walkie-talkie — press, talk, release, and my words appear in the stream. Instead, text dumps into the send box and I have to edit and click send. That kills the flow of a live conversation.

## AC
1. Record button captures audio and saves to /tmp/chorus-recordings/<session-id>-<timestamp>.webm
2. Audio auto-transcribes (Whisper or browser SpeechRecognition API)
3. Transcript appends to the Clearing message stream as a Jeff message — attributed, timestamped
4. No text in the send input — the transcript flows directly into the conversation
5. Recording indicator visible while active (red dot or similar)
6. Works on mobile Safari (phone use case from demo night)

## Why
Jeff tried to record during the demo with Deb and Allu. The button exists but dumps text into the send window instead of streaming into the conversation. Jeff wants press-and-talk, not press-transcribe-edit-send. The Clearing should feel like a walkie-talkie, not a dictation app.

tasks.title: JDI hook enhancement — detect intent-without-action and auto-continue
tasks.description:
## Experience
I watch roles say 'I'll start on X now' and then nothing happens. They narrate what they're going to do instead of doing it. I've flagged this pattern multiple times — the announcement replaces the work. I want the system to catch it automatically so I don't have to be the one noticing every time a role goes idle after declaring intent.

## AC
1. Hook detects phrases that signal intent without execution ('I'll start on X now', 'Let me do X', 'Going to X next') in role output
2. If the role's next action is NOT a tool call within 30 seconds of the intent statement, hook nudges the role: 'You said you'd do X — do it or retract'
3. Works across all three roles — not just builders
4. Does not fire on genuinely informational statements ('I'll brief Silas later' — that's a future commitment, not an immediate action)
5. Configurable in settings — can be disabled per role if too aggressive

## Why
Roles announce intent ('I'll start on X now') and then either go idle, narrate more, or pivot without executing. The announcement replaces the work. DEC-025 says go is default. This hook enforces that — if you say you'll do it, the system expects you to do it. Directly addresses feedback_announce_is_not_execute and feedback_no_narrate_before_execute.
