tasks.title: Add Haiku classification layer to The Clearing — tone, intent, routing
tasks.description:
## Experience
When I'm in a Clearing session, the routing is dumb — it's regex matching on @mentions. So half the time messages go to the wrong role, or the tone chart on /jeff says 61% coordination when that's not what's actually happening. I want the system to understand what I'm saying — is this a question, a directive, a story — and route it to the right place without me tagging anyone. The tone data should actually mean something when I look at it.

## AC
1. Haiku classifies incoming messages: tone (directive, collaborative, question, corrective, narrative, etc.), intent (action request, status update, acknowledgment, routing)
2. Classification drives message routing — replaces regex @mention parsing with intent-aware routing
3. Feeds the tone analytics on /jeff page with richer signal than keyword waterfalls
4. Latency budget: <500ms per message (Haiku is fast enough)
5. Fallback to current regex if API unavailable — no hard dependency

## Why
The Clearing currently uses regex for everything — routing, tailer filtering, message classification. The /jeff tone chart showed 61% coordination because keyword matching can't distinguish narrative from routing from acknowledgment. Haiku is $0.25/1M input tokens — cheap enough to classify every message. Richer classification improves the /jeff analytics, enables smarter routing, and powers the JDI intent detection (#1746).

tasks.title: Werk Flow Metrics tab — validate data completeness, fix understated card counts
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the Werk Flow Metrics tab and the numbers don't match what I know. I've shipped 24 cards in a session and the dashboard says 98 total completed — that's way off. I need to trust these graphs the same way I trust the board itself, and right now the undercount makes the whole metrics tab feel unreliable.

## AC
1. Card count matches Vikunja reality — all cards, all statuses, not a subset
2. Completed count includes Won't Do + Done
3. Per-role lead times exclude parked/Later cards that skew averages
4. Cumulative flow slopes match actual velocity (24 cards/session, not flat)
5. Trace and fix data source — Vikunja pagination, workflow engine subset, or query filter causing undercount
6. Extend existing /api/analytics/pipeline endpoint

## Why
212 cards / 98 completed massively understates reality. All graphs show lower throughput than actual. Jeff can't trust the dashboard if the numbers don't match what he sees.

## AC

2. Shows: WIP limits (DEC-051), attention contract (DEC-1571), proving gates (DEC-048), role ownership (DEC-058)
3. Live contract status — which rules are being followed vs violated right now
4. Data from existing decisions + board state, no new API needed
5. Extend /api/analytics endpoints for contract compliance metrics

## Why

I need instruments I can trust — accurate numbers, clean layout, data that matches what actually happened.

## AC
1. Brief Latency shows actual time-to-read, not just "unread" status
2. Card Velocity shows all recent cards, not just 5
3. Fitness Functions (weekly throughput, deploys) match actual numbers — 32/week is wrong when 24 shipped in one session
4. Attention Cost signal rate validated against real message classification
5. Visual hierarchy — sections organized by importance, not crammed two-column
6. Extend existing /api/analytics endpoints

## Why
Instruments tab has the most useful data but numbers don't match reality and layout is dense. Brief latency shows "unread" not time. Fitness throughput at 32/week contradicts observed velocity. Jeff needs trustworthy instruments.

tasks.title: Document LaunchAgent namespace convention — com.chorus vs com.gathering

- [ ] Audit all 51 LaunchAgents against convention
- [ ] List any misplaced agents (e.g., com.gathering.messaging should be com.chorus.*)
- [ ] Add to infrastructure-constraints.md

## Context

tasks.title: Bidirectional Clearing — role narration syncs both ways between Claude sessions and the Clearing

- [ ] No duplicate messages — dedup by content hash + timestamp

tasks.title: Product taxonomy sketch — Jeff's hand-drawn architecture mapping Phase/Werk/Loom to domain structure

Root cause: Clearing had just restarted, new Node process lost System Events accessibility grant. osascript lies — returns 'ok' without actually delivering.

I have Grafana dashboards but no actual HTTP metrics from the app. I'm relying on blackbox probes to tell me if requests are slow or failing, which means I only know something is wrong after it's already down. I want real request counts and latency histograms so I can see degradation before it becomes an outage.

## AC

From Silas logging strategy. Relying on blackbox probes instead of native HTTP metrics.

src/app.ts
package.json

tasks.title: Memory-and-research gate hook — PreToolUse block on writes without prior memory/git check
tasks.description:
## AC
1. PreToolUse hook fires before Edit/Write to code files
2. Hook checks session history for memory search (MEMORY.md, decisions, briefs) AND research (git log/show, Read of current state)
3. If neither found, hook blocks with message: check memory and research first
4. Hook passes silently when both checks detected
5. Tests cover: block when no prior check, pass when memory checked, pass when both checked

tasks.title: Prove-it gate hook — block 'cant be done' assertions without evidence
tasks.description:
## Experience

A role says "that can't be done" and the system says: prove it. Show the constraint, the error, the code path. I stop hearing "impossible" without evidence — every limitation comes with a receipt.

## AC
1. PreToolUse hook detects when a role asserts something is impossible or cant be done
2. Hook blocks with message: prove it — show the constraint, the error, or the code path
3. Assertion without evidence is not accepted — role must demonstrate the limitation
4. Hook passes when evidence (error output, code reference, platform constraint) is provided
5. Tests cover: block bare assertion, pass with evidence attached

tasks.title: Clearing session tailer whitelist — invert filter logic to show only Jeff-facing content
tasks.description:
## AC
1. Session tailer uses a whitelist (what Jeff should see) not a blacklist (what to hide)
2. Role-to-role nudges, /chat messages, DELIVERED confirmations, and nudge.sh refs never appear in Clearing
3. Only Jeff-authored messages and role responses addressed to Jeff flow through
4. Tests cover: role-to-role chat filtered, nudge filtered, Jeff message passes, role response to Jeff passes
5. No new filter needed when a new role-to-role message type is added

tasks.title: Verification gate hook — block Done declaration without proof of fix
tasks.description:
## AC
1. TDD gate — block demo/done without tests covering every AC item
2. Pair gate — block building without pair on code cards
3. Demo gate — block done without demo evidence
4. All PreToolUse hooks that block, not suggest
5. Tests cover each gate

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/tdd_gate.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/pair_gate.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/demo_gate.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/mod.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

tasks.title: Root cause gate hook — require cross-role consultation with data before coding a fix
tasks.description:
## Experience

I see a role jump straight to coding a fix without talking to anyone. The system catches it and says: "discuss root cause with another role first — bring data, not guesses." No more solo debugging spirals that waste hours chasing the wrong cause.

## AC
1. PreToolUse hook detects when a role begins editing code to fix a defect
2. Hook checks session history for a /chat or brief exchange with at least one other role about the problem
3. The exchange must include data — logs, error output, code references — not just description of symptoms
4. If no cross-role consultation with data found, hook blocks with message: discuss root cause with another role first, bring data not guesses
5. Tests cover: block solo fix attempt, block consultation without data, pass with data-backed cross-role discussion

tasks.title: Development framework — gate suite for agent software development lifecycle
tasks.description:
## Experience
Roles follow a structured development lifecycle — like the integration framework (ICD, mapper, validation) but for code changes. No guessing, no solo debugging, no declaring done without proof.

## AC
1. Memory-and-research gate (#1811) — check decisions, memory, git history before any code change
2. Root cause gate (#1815) — cross-role consultation with data (logs, errors, code refs) before coding a fix
3. Prove-it gate (#1812) — "cant be done" requires evidence, not assertion
4. Definition-of-done gate (#1814) — verified working against the symptom, not just code written and built
5. TDD enforced — unit, integration, and page-level tests on all code cards (DEC-1674)
6. Service design updated — every shipped card updates its service design page if touched
7. ICD updated — data flow changes update ICD
8. Blast radius and domain radius checks pass
9. Structured logging — product, value stream, domain, card, code object, datetime, version on every log line
10. Use lc/lw/ls/lk/nudge/chat when stuck — tools before guesses
11. All gates implemented as PreToolUse hooks that block, not reminders that suggest
12. Framework documented as a single reference page roles can read at session start

tasks.title: Normalize log metadata — enrich spine event emitter with product, stream, card, file, version
tasks.description:
## AC
1. emitSpineEvent injects product, stream, card, file, version from context at construction — not per call site
2. Field names normalized: vertebra→stream, appName→product (chorus-events→Chorus, jeff-bridwell-personal-site→Gathering)
3. card populated from role-state.sh card context
4. version populated from git rev-parse --short HEAD at app startup
5. file populated by caller (code object path)
6. Missing keys emit as null, not error
7. chorus-log.sh accepts the same keys for bash callers
8. Tests verify: all 7 fields present in emitted events, null for missing, field name normalization correct
9. After: #1805

## AC

3. Tests cover: role-to-role /chat messages do NOT appear in Clearing send box
4. Tests cover: no feedback loop — Clearing does not re-inject its own messages
5. Tests cover: guest identity displays correctly (role names, not Guest)
6. Tests cover: reconnect after disconnect — no duplicate messages, no lost messages
7. Tests cover: background image renders, OG tags correct
8. Tests cover: session tailer only shows Jeff-facing content (whitelist, not blacklist)
9. All tests run in CI — no manual verification needed

tasks.title: Board state validation tests — card numbers, state cards, stream/domain accuracy
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at the board and the numbers shift between refreshes. I move a card to Won't Do and it stays visible. I ask for WIP count and get cards from Done. I can't trust what I see, so I can't plan. The board is how I manage — if it's wrong, I'm managing blind.

## Files
- platform/board-client/src/client.ts
- platform/board-client/src/sdk.ts
- platform/board-client/tests/

## AC
1. Tests verify card numbers returned by board-ts match Vikunja source
2. Tests verify state cards (WIP per role) show correct current card for each role
3. Tests verify stream and domain tags display correctly on card views
4. Tests verify card move operations update state immediately — no stale cache
5. Tests verify board-ts list output matches actual Vikunja state
6. Tests verify view() shows correct bucket for all cards (not just first 50)
7. Tests verify move reports success only when Vikunja confirms persistence
8. All tests run in CI

## What we learned (2026-04-05 session)
- fetchBuckets() caps at 50 per bucket, no pagination
- fetchAllTasks() had page<30 cap hiding 232 cards
- list() and view() use different status paths — list was fixed, view wasn't
- CLI reports 'Moved' without verifying API response
- Test suite was creating production cards via cards add

Jeff directive: automate the WHOLE board page, not just state cards.

tasks.title: Fix chorus folder structure — production code in gitignored directory
tasks.description:
## Experience

I expect any role to be able to check out the repo and run the tests. Right now Clearing code lives in a gitignored directory — Silas sees 52 passing tests, Wren sees 41. That's not a test problem, that's a structure problem. Production code should never be invisible to the team.

## AC
- [ ] Clearing/Bridge code (chorus/bridge/) is committable and shared across roles
- [ ] Tests run from committed code, not working tree only
- [ ] Folder structure documented — what lives where, why
- [ ] No production code in gitignored paths

## Context
chorus/ is gitignored from the team repo. The Clearing server, session tailer, test files — all trapped in one role's working tree. Roles can't verify each other's changes. #1818 tests pass for Silas (52/52) and fail for Wren (41/52) because the test file isn't committed. This is a structural problem, not a test problem.

3. Role directories (architect, engineer, product-manager) moved into chorus/roles/
4. All path references updated: CLAUDE.md fragments, skills, hooks, LaunchAgents, shell scripts
5. 3 runtime app path.resolve() calls updated or symlinked (spine-events.json, loom-metrics.js, workflows)
6. All 3 CLAUDE.md files regenerated via claudemd-gen.sh
7. All 3 roles boot clean on new paths
8. Full test suite passes from the new repo structure
9. One coordinated commit — all 3 roles idle during migration
10. Old messages/ path either removed or symlinked for backward compat

## Experience
I check disk usage and Fuseki is eating 710GB — over a third of Library's disk. That can't be right for the amount of data we have. I need to know if it's bloat, duplication, or legitimate, and I need it shrunk.

## AC
- [ ] Count actual triples vs expected — determine if data is duplicated across graphs
- [ ] Check for large literal values stored inline (base64, HTML blobs)
- [ ] Test TDB2 compact operation and measure space reclaimed
- [ ] If compact insufficient, plan clean rebuild from TTL sources
- [ ] Disk usage under 200GB after fix (or documented justification for current size)

## Files
- architect/infrastructure-constraints.md

tasks.title: Reports from the Blockverse — blog about what it's actually like to coordinate AI agents
tasks.description:
## Experience
Nobody is writing honestly about what it's actually like to coordinate AI agents day-to-day. The seasick Clearing, the nudge that said nothing, the 710GB nobody asked for — these are real stories that would resonate with anyone trying to build with AI teams. I want to publish this because it's the authentic version of "AI team coordination" and it positions Chorus as a real product, not vaporware.

## AC
- [ ] First post drafted — pick from today's session stories (seasick Clearing, invisible elephant, 710GB nobody asked for, the nudge that said nothing)
- [ ] Published on wordpress-blog or lightlifeurbangardens.com/blog (Jeff's call)
- [ ] Connects to Chorus product positioning — this is the honest version of "AI team coordination"

tasks.title: Experience-driven card format — every card starts with Jeff's experience in his voice

## AC

2. Experience section is 2-5 sentences describing what Jeff sees/feels/gets

I edit a skill during a card and can't commit the change. Git can't see it, can't diff it, can't track it. 30+ skills live outside the repo in ~/.claude/skills/ with no version control. Edits vanish between sessions.

## AC
1. All skill SKILL.md files have canonical source inside the chorus repo
2. ~/.claude/skills/ contains symlinks pointing to repo source, not standalone files

tasks.title: Stop-on-error gate — PostToolUse hook blocks next action when previous tool errors
tasks.description:
## Experience
I watch a role hit 3-4 tool errors per turn and keep going like nothing happened. Spine events fail silently, paths break, scripts 404 — and the role just moves to the next tool call. I shouldn't have to be the one who notices.

## AC
1. PostToolUse hook detects non-zero exit code or error in tool output
2. Hook blocks the next tool call with a clear message: stop, diagnose, fix
3. Role must explicitly acknowledge or fix the error before continuing
4. Exemptions only for known-benign patterns (e.g. grep finding no matches)
5. Spine event emitted on every suppressed error so we can measure the problem

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/stop_on_error.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

I run team-scan and it tells me a role is idle when it's actually working. The timestamps are stale, so the system reports silence that isn't real. This is the worst kind of false signal — it makes me intervene when nothing is wrong, which is exactly the relay coordination tax I'm trying to eliminate.

I need to see a clear event that says "Kade booted without board data" so I can catch it before it cascades into bad work.

## AC

- [ ] Emit session.context.error when context cache (session-start file) fails to generate

I send a nudge and it gets drained from the queue, but I have no idea if the role actually did anything with it. "Drained" and "acted on" are two completely different things. Right now nudge delivery looks successful even when the recipient ignored it, which means I can't tell if my coordination is working or just disappearing into a void.

## AC

tasks.description:
## Experience
When a role's session ends, I have no idea it happened until I notice silence. There's no event, no duration, no signal that says "Silas worked for 47 minutes and closed." I'm flying blind on session boundaries — I can't tell if a role crashed or cleanly exited, and I can't see how long sessions actually run.

## AC
- [ ] Emit session.role.ended spine event when session-close runs
- [ ] Event includes: role, duration (seconds since session.role.started), clean/crash indicator

- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs

tasks.title: Enforce chorus prompt timestamp — hook that injects wall clock into every response, roles can't drift
tasks.description:
## Experience
I see stale timestamps on role responses — Silas says 17:10 when it's 18:30. That cascades into wrong escalation decisions. The timestamp should be real every time, not cached from session start.

## AC
1. PostToolUse or PreToolUse hook injects current wall clock into every response cycle
2. Roles cannot emit a cached/stale timestamp — each response gets a fresh date call
3. Hook fires on all tool types, not just Bash

- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

## Experience
I look at spine events and see UTC timestamps. I look at gemba and see Boston time. Daily reviews use yet another format. When I'm trying to correlate what happened when, I have to do timezone math in my head.

## AC
1. All spine events emit timestamps in Eastern time (Boston)
2. Gemba output uses the same format as spine events
3. Daily review scripts use the same format
4. One canonical format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM Eastern

tasks.description:
## Experience
I see team-scan say a role was active '1m ago' or '2m ago' and assume they're idle. But they're mid-thought on a complex response — Opus takes time. The staleness threshold doesn't account for thinking time.

## AC
1. Team-scan threshold raised from 120s to a value that accounts for model thinking time
2. Active roles mid-response not reported as stale
3. Threshold is configurable, not hardcoded

- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs
- chorus/platform/scripts/werk-init.sh

tasks.title: Re-run photo harvest pipelines — restore 20K records lost in Fuseki rebuild
tasks.description:
## Experience
I had 100K photos. Now I have 82K. The missing 20K were loaded by harvest pipelines (takeout, iphone, apple) via SPARQL INSERT — not stored as TTL. Lost in Fuseki rebuild.

## AC
1. Re-run all photo harvest pipelines from original source data
2. Photo count restored to 100K+ in Fuseki
3. Photos page renders with full data

tasks.title: Externalize configuration from code — ports, paths, endpoints hardcoded everywhere
tasks.description:
## Experience
I change a port number and have to grep through 40 files to find every place it's hardcoded. Paths to scripts, API endpoints, database ports, machine IPs — all baked into the code. When Fuseki moves from Docker to native and the port stays the same, it works by accident. When it doesn't, everything breaks silently.

## AC
1. Inventory all hardcoded ports, paths, endpoints, and machine IPs across scripts and services
2. Define a single config source (env file, config.json, or similar) that all scripts and services read
3. Migrate top 10 most-referenced values (Fuseki port, Vikunja port, Chorus API port, Library/Bedroom IPs, key paths) to the config source
4. Scripts and services read from config, not inline constants
5. Document the config contract — what lives where, how to override

tasks.title: Add tracing to all hooks — every intercept logged with input, decision, output
tasks.description:
## Experience
I can't tell what the hooks are doing. They block commands, inject content, and alter behavior — but there's no log of what they intercepted or why. When something goes wrong I have no way to trace it.

## AC
1. Every PreToolUse hook logs: tool name, decision (allow/block/ask), reason, to a single file
2. Every PostToolUse hook logs: tool name, decision, reason
3. Every UserPromptSubmit hook logs: what was injected into stderr
4. Log file is human-readable, one line per intercept
5. Log file path: ~/Library/Logs/Gathering/hooks.log

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

tasks.title: Hook pulse logging — every invocation emits structured telemetry
tasks.description:
## Experience
I watch a role hit a hook error and have no idea what just happened. Was it PreToolUse? Which module? Did it block or warn? I can't see into the hooks — they're a black box. When they break, the whole team operates on fiction and nobody knows until I notice something wrong.

## AC
1. Every hook invocation emits one structured log line — no silent passes
2. Fields: timestamp, role, hook_type, tool, module, decision (allow/block/warn), reason, duration_ms, source line
3. Log destination is readable by all roles (Loki or shared log file)
4. Blocked hooks include the reason in the log line
5. Hook duration tracked — catches silent hangs
6. Wren can tail the log and commentate on what's firing in real time

## Context
Lobotomy pattern: grep hook broke all 3 roles for 2 days, nobody reported it. Docker fiction: hooks returned success output but Docker was off. Jeff's Fund That Flip operational excellence framework — Auditability pillar.

tasks.title: Move Fuseki DB and pod data to CSC-scoped persistence — out of app repo
tasks.description:
## Experience
All my data lives inside the app repo. If someone git cleans or deletes the repo, Fuseki and 151K TTL source files go with it. App logs write to /tmp and get wiped on every reboot. Data and logs should survive code changes and reboots.

## AC
1. Fuseki TDB2 moved to ~/.gathering/data/fuseki/
2. Pod TTL source files moved to ~/.gathering/data/pods/
3. Harvest inputs moved to ~/.gathering/data/harvest/
4. App config updated to reference new paths
5. Fuseki LaunchAgent plist updated for new DB path
6. App LaunchAgent plist logs to ~/Library/Logs/Gathering/ not /tmp
7. Repo deletable without data loss
8. App logs survive reboot

## Files
- data/fuseki/configuration/pods-simple.ttl
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gathering.fuseki.plist
- ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gathering.app.plist
- architect/scripts/launchagents/app-wrapper.sh

tasks.title: Normalize photo predicates and graph queries — photoFileName vs photoFilename, dateTaken vs dcterms:created, multi-graph query scope
tasks.description:
## Experience
I go to the photos page and only see a fraction of my photos. I know I have over 100K photos across Apple Photos, Google Takeout, and iPhone — but the page shows maybe 14K. The data is in Fuseki, it just can't find it because the field names don't match and the queries only look in one graph.

## AC
- [ ] Normalize photoFileName/photoFilename to single predicate across all photo graphs
- [ ] Align dateTaken vs dcterms:created — handler queries dateTaken, 68K records use dcterms:created
- [ ] Update photo handler to query all photo graphs (urn:gathering:photos, urn:jb:photos/canonical/, urn:jb:photos/restored) or consolidate into one
- [ ] Photos page renders full corpus after fix

## Files
- src/handlers/photo.handler.ts
- src/config/sparql-constants.ts
- config/namespaces.json

tasks.title: Hooks circuit breaker — auto-disable broken hooks after 3 failures
tasks.description:
## Experience
A hook panics or hangs and all three roles can't do anything. Two days pass before anyone notices. I need hooks that protect themselves — if one breaks, it steps aside.

## AC
1. Each hook module wrapped with 2-second tokio timeout
2. Per-module failure counter: 3 failures in 5 min triggers auto-disable
3. Auto-disabled hooks log CIRCUIT_BREAK to hooks.log
4. Auto-disabled hooks notify Bridge so Jeff sees it
5. Hooks re-enable on service restart
6. Test: mock panicking hook, verify circuit-break after 3 failures
7. All subprocess-calling hooks wrapped with 3-second timeout
8. Subprocess timeout logs SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/state.rs

tasks.title: Pulse log enrichment — module name, duration, full reasons on every hook decision
tasks.description:
## Experience
I tail hooks.log and see allow/block decisions but can't tell which hook module made the call. When something blocks wrong, I need the module name, duration, and full reason — not just 'DENY'.

## AC
1. Every log line includes the hook module name
2. Every log line includes duration_ms
3. BLOCK/DENY lines include full reason (not truncated)
4. Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm
5. Session_id (8 chars) in log lines
6. Log rotation: max 10MB, rotate to hooks.log.1
7. Consistent response conventions: blocks use exit_code 2 + stderr + deny JSON
8. Single log_decision() function all hooks call

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

tasks.title: Hook test coverage floor — every module gets allow + block tests
tasks.description:
## Experience
21 of 33 hook modules have zero tests. When I change a hook, I can't tell if an untested module started misclassifying.

## AC
1. Every hook module has at least 2 tests: one allow, one block/warn
2. Priority: session_init_gate, csc_guard, demo_preflight, accept_gate, sensitive_paths
3. One integration test through full pre_tool_use pipeline
4. tool_telemetry gets 2 tests
5. Test count 221 → 280+

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/

tasks.title: Session JSONL cache — 5 hooks re-reading the same file independently
tasks.description:
## Experience
Five hooks scan my entire session JSONL looking for evidence — did I write tests? Am I in a pair? Did I do a demo? Each one re-reads the whole file independently, every tool call. As sessions get longer, this gets slower. I shouldn't feel the hooks getting heavier.

## AC
1. Shared session JSONL reader in AppState: read once per prompt cycle, cache results
2. tdd_gate, demo_gate, pair_gate, memory_gate, autonomy_guard all use the cached reader
3. Cache invalidates on new tool_response (not on every check)
4. Benchmark: measure per-hook JSONL read time before and after, log in pulse

## Context
From hooks-pulse-audit.html. 5 hooks independently scan session JSONL with no caching or shared reader.

tasks.title: Guard consolidation — merge overlapping hooks, standardize response format
tasks.description:
## Experience
Two hooks block the same docker commands with slightly different patterns. When one gets updated and the other doesn't, the rules drift. I get confused about which guard is which. The hooks should have clean boundaries — one concern per module, no overlaps.

## AC
1. Merge app_state_guard into infra_guardrails (they overlap on docker, kill, terraform patterns)
2. Audit all remaining hooks for pattern overlap — flag and consolidate
3. Standardize response conventions: all blocks use exit_code 2 + stderr message + stdout deny JSON
4. Single log_decision() function that all hooks call

## Context
From hooks-pulse-audit.html. app_state_guard and infra_guardrails overlap on docker, kill, and terraform. Inconsistent response conventions between hooks.

tasks.title: Context-aware hooks — interaction pattern feeds into hook urgency
tasks.description:
## Experience
I am in SWAT mode fixing a broken service and the hooks block me the same way they do during a normal session. I am doing a demo and demo_preflight hangs for 10 seconds on subprocess calls. The hooks do not know what I am doing — they fire identically regardless of context.

## AC
1. Interaction pattern detection (SWAT, demo, pair, reflection) feeds into hook dispatch
2. SWAT mode: loosen safety guards for debugging, skip blast radius warnings
3. Demo mode: tighten quality, demo_preflight must complete in under 2 seconds
4. Pair mode: navigator blocks visible to driver via pulse log
5. Reflection mode: hooks invisible, no interruptions
6. Pattern to hook urgency mapping documented and testable

## Context
From hooks-pulse-audit.html section 5c. Wren emits interaction.pattern.detected spine events but hooks ignore them. Wiring pattern to hook behavior eliminates false-positive attention cost.

tasks.title: Fix Promtail JSON parsing — structured logs reaching Loki as plain text
tasks.description:
## Experience
We spent all day adding structured logging. App writes perfect JSON. Loki gets plain text.

## AC
1. Promtail config parses JSON log lines preserving all fields
2. Loki receives full structured JSON
3. Loki queries by appName, level, component work
4. Verify last 100 lines in Loki have JSON fields

## Files
- src/config/promtail-native.yaml
- chorus/platform/scripts/log-rotate

tasks.title: Photo detail shows thumbnail instead of full image
tasks.description:
## Experience
I click on a photo in the grid and get a blurry thumbnail instead of the full-size image. I want to see my photos — the detail view should show the real thing, not a tiny render scaled up.

## AC
1. Photo detail view serves full-resolution image, not thumbnail
2. Verify with 5+ photos across different sources (Apple, Takeout, iPhone)
3. If full image unavailable, show thumbnail with a clear indicator that full-size is missing

tasks.title: Remove stale Docker references — 275 refs across codebase for retired infrastructure
tasks.description:
## Experience
I ask where Fuseki is and three roles say Docker. The code says Docker. The CLAUDE.md says Docker. The permission profiles allow Docker compose. But Docker has been retired for weeks — Fuseki runs natively. Every stale reference is a lie waiting to confuse the next role that reads it.

## AC
1. app-state.sh: remove all Docker Fuseki lifecycle commands, keep only native LaunchAgent management
2. CLAUDE.md (jeff-bridwell-personal-site): remove Docker network, Docker log driver, Docker DNS references
3. README.md: update from Terraform+Docker to LaunchAgent-based
4. TEAM_PROTOCOL.md: remove Docker network references, update Fuseki entry
5. Permission profiles (base.json, silas.json): remove Docker compose allows
6. settings.local.json: remove Docker compose allow
7. server.js line 1: remove Docker development message
8. validate-ttl.sh: remove container name reference
9. infra_guardrails.rs: evaluate which Docker guards are still needed (content matching vs command matching)
10. Delete dead files: docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, .dockerignore, docker/ directory, terraform/Dockerfile, terraform/app-state.sh
11. Leave alone: archived workflows, ontology ServiceRuntimeType, the test that asserts Docker absence

## Files
app-state.sh, scripts/app-state.sh, CLAUDE.md, README.md, server.js, scripts/validate-ttl.sh, docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile, .dockerignore, terraform/Dockerfile (app repo)
TEAM_PROTOCOL.md, proving/config/profiles/base.json, proving/config/profiles/silas.json, platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/infra_guardrails.rs, .claude/settings.local.json (chorus repo)

## Context
Docker scan found 275 refs. Fuseki moved to native LaunchAgent today (#1833, #1856). Three remaining Docker containers (Vikunja, Navidrome, webvowl) — check if those still need Docker before deleting compose file.

tasks.title: Doc-catalog consolidation — 232 HTML files scattered, catalog only reads one folder
tasks.description:
## Experience
I create an HTML artifact and it ends up in one of 5 different directories. When I want to find it later, I go to doc-catalog and it is not there. 63 HTML files across chorus and the app repo — service designs, research, borg assessments, architecture docs, manuals — and doc-catalog only reads from one folder. The catalog should be where I find everything.

## AC
1. doc-catalog.html in public/ scans multiple source directories (docs/, data/about/, public/gathering-docs/, chorus product-manager/artifacts/, chorus product-manager/data/borg/)
2. NOT a handler — standalone static page with client-side or build-time directory listing
3. NOT in the navbar — standalone page at /doc-catalog.html
4. Search box — filter by title or content
5. Deduplicate: product-manager/ and platform/roles/wren/ have ~60 identical files — single canonical location
6. Section map covers all authored HTML (not generated reports or screenshots)
7. Every authored HTML artifact accessible from doc-catalog page
8. Delete duplicate copies after consolidation

## Files
- public/doc-catalog.html (existing static page — extend this, don't create a handler)
- docs/ (current source)
- data/about/ (30 files not in catalog)
- public/gathering-docs/ (50 files not in catalog)
- chorus product-manager/artifacts/ (23 files not in catalog)
- chorus product-manager/data/borg/ (9 files not in catalog)

tasks.title: Fix session-start disk check — uses df, reports false 96% on APFS
tasks.description:
## Experience
I glance at my session boot and see 96% disk. My stomach drops — then I remember it's wrong. df lies on APFS. I shouldn't have to remember that; the system should report truth.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session-start health check uses diskutil (not df/du) for disk reporting
- [ ] Purgeable space is excluded from "used" calculation
- [ ] Reported percentage matches what Finder shows

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs (context_cache_cmd disk check ~L973)

tasks.title: Seed read endpoint on Chorus API — roles can't see seeds without browser auth
tasks.description:
## Experience
I text a seed from my phone. I expect the team to see it. But no role can check seeds from their terminal. The whole point of seeds is I can send from anywhere and it lands in the workflow. Right now it lands in a black box.

## AC
- [ ] GET /seeds on Chorus API (localhost:3340) returns recent seeds
- [ ] No browser auth — open like other Chorus endpoints
- [ ] Roles can verify seed arrival from terminal
- [ ] Include status, timestamp, content preview

## Files
- chorus/platform/chorus-sdk/src/routes/seeds.ts
- chorus/platform/chorus-sdk/src/app.ts

tasks.title: Audit formal docs against doc-catalog — surface overlap and drift
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open /docs on the site and see pages I'm not sure are current. I open doc-catalog and see artifacts I know are fresh. I can't tell which source of truth to trust. I need to see them side by side so I know what's real.

## AC
- [ ] List all formal /docs pages with last-modified dates
- [ ] Map each to equivalent doc-catalog artifacts (if any)
- [ ] Flag drift: formal doc says X, catalog artifact says Y
- [ ] Produce a single comparison artifact (HTML)

tasks.title: Deprecate formal /docs into doc-catalog — single docs surface
tasks.description:
## Experience
I go to one place for docs — doc-catalog. Not two. The formal /docs section is retired. Everything lives in doc-catalog with search. I stop wondering which is current.

## AC
- [ ] All formal /docs content migrated or superseded by doc-catalog artifacts
- [ ] /docs route redirects to doc-catalog (or removed)
- [ ] No orphaned formal docs pages
- [ ] Decision logged (DEC-xxx)

tasks.title: Write product, architecture, and engineering manuals — Gathering and Chorus
tasks.description:
## Experience
I hand Silas the architecture manual and say "red pen this." He marks what's wrong. Same with Kade on engineering. The manuals are written from existing docs as-is — stale parts included. The gaps ARE the value. This is how I find out what we actually know vs what we think we know.

## AC
- [ ] Product manual — Gathering (vision, domains, value streams, user experience)
- [ ] Product manual — Chorus (vision, services, coordination model, team ops)
- [ ] Architecture manual — Gathering (topology, data stores, integrations, infra)
- [ ] Architecture manual — Chorus (hooks, scripts, messaging, observability)
- [ ] Engineering manual — Gathering (codebase, conventions, test strategy, deploy)
- [ ] Engineering manual — Chorus (codebase, hooks, gates, CLI tools)
- [ ] All manuals as HTML artifacts in doc-catalog
- [ ] Written from current docs state — no cleanup first
- [ ] Briefed to Silas and Kade for red-pen review

tasks.title: Remove chunk taxonomy — redundant with domain
tasks.description:
## Experience
I have two labels on every card that mean the same thing — chunk and domain both tell me "what area." Domain maps to real things I care about (photos, seeds, chorus). Chunk is abstract and has no consumer. Worse, the mandatory chunk gate on card creation is friction every time I add a card. I want domain only, and I want the gate gone.

## AC
- [ ] Remove mandatory chunk gate from cards CLI (add command no longer requires --chunk)
- [ ] cards CLI accepts cards without chunk tag — no error, no warning
- [ ] Existing chunk labels on cards left in place (no bulk removal — that's a separate sweep)
- [ ] Sequence tag remains unchanged — it's cross-cutting, not redundant
- [ ] Document the decision (DEC needed per Jeff's comment)

## Files
- platform/board-client/src/cli.ts
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/quality_gate.rs

tasks.title: Seed pipeline photo delivery broken — photos sent via SMS don't arrive
tasks.description:
## Experience

I send photos via SMS and they don't arrive. The #wren hashtag works, the photos don't. I shouldn't have to wonder if my photos landed — the seed pipeline should handle media attachments reliably.

## AC

- [ ] Photos sent via SMS arrive in the seed pipeline and are persisted
- [ ] Media attachments (image/jpeg, image/png) are extracted from Twilio webhook
- [ ] Photo URL is stored alongside the seed text content
- [ ] Existing text-only seed flow is not affected

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/handlers/seed.handler.ts

tasks.title: Link design artifacts to cards — sequence diagrams, service designs, borg assessments
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open a card and see AC and files. But I don't see the design that informed it. The pair-flow-sequence diagram drove the pair gate hook, but the card doesn't link to it. When a builder picks up the card, they implement from AC text, not the actual design. The design becomes invisible. Service designs, sequence diagrams, borg assessments — these are domain-level artifacts. They should travel with the card.

## AC
- [ ] Cards reference their design artifacts (service designs, sequence diagrams, assessments)
- [ ] Design artifacts findable from doc-catalog AND from the card
- [ ] Establish convention: ## Design section in card description linking to artifact

## Files
- product-manager/CLAUDE.md (convention documentation)
- product-manager/decisions.md (DEC for the convention)

tasks.title: Bridge subscriber LaunchAgent — managed lifecycle instead of session-spawned
tasks.description:
## Experience
Bridge subscribers die and nobody restarts them. They need managed lifecycle.

## AC
1. Each bridge-subscriber gets its own LaunchAgent plist
2. KeepAlive ensures respawn on crash
3. app-state.sh subscribers-reload command
4. Logs to ~/Library/Logs/Chorus/

## Files
- chorus/platform/scripts/bridge-subscriber.js

tasks.title: Doc-catalog as live service — scan directories at request time, no rebuild needed
tasks.description:
## Experience
I write a new HTML artifact and want it in doc-catalog immediately. Right now I have to ask Kade to rebuild a static page. That's a manual step that breaks flow. I should write the file and it shows up — no rebuild, no nudge, no waiting.

## AC
- [ ] GET /api/doc-catalog returns live listing of all HTML from 5 source directories
- [ ] doc-catalog.html becomes a client that calls the API instead of hardcoded entries
- [ ] New artifacts appear immediately — no rebuild step
- [ ] Search still works client-side from the API response
- [ ] Existing section grouping and badges preserved

## Files
src/handlers/doc-catalog.handler.ts src/app.ts public/doc-catalog.html public/js/doc-catalog-search.js

## Context
From #1867 doc-catalog consolidation. Currently static HTML with hardcoded entries. Convert to live API endpoint that scans 5 source directories at request time.

tasks.title: Observer reads andon state, not card events — card number stale when role skips /pull
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at team-scan and see 'kade: building #1866' but Kade actually pulled #1882 twenty minutes ago. The observer shows stale card numbers because it reads andon state, which only updates when a role calls role-state.sh. If a role moves a card via board-ts without /pull (which calls role-state.sh at step 5), the observer is blind.

## AC
- [ ] Observer reads card.pulled spine events from chorus.log instead of (or in addition to) andon state files
- [ ] When a card is moved to WIP via board-ts, the card number in team-scan updates within 60 seconds
- [ ] Fallback: if no recent card.pulled event, still read andon state (backwards compat)

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/observer.rs (read_role_card function ~L57)

tasks.title: Log-first gate — require log inspection before coding a fix
tasks.description:
## Experience
A role sees a bug and jumps straight to the code. They read 550 lines of Rust, form a theory, and code a fix — but the logs would have shown the real cause in 30 seconds. Today: Wren read observer.rs to understand stale card display, then checked logs and found the actual root cause was different (stale session state, not missing role-state call). Context synthesis gate catches missing search; this catches missing log inspection.

## AC
- [ ] PreToolUse gate: before editing code in a fix context, check for recent log/grep of chorus.log or hooks.log
- [ ] Gate message: "Check the logs before fixing. What do chorus.log and hooks.log say about this failure?"
- [ ] Ties into context synthesis gate — logs are part of the synthesis requirement
- [ ] Does NOT block new feature work — only fix/debug contexts

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/log_first_gate.rs (new)
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/mod.rs (register module)
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs (wire into pre_tool_use_inner)

tasks.title: Handler and hook templates with logging pre-wired — scaffold new code with observability built in
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every new handler or hook starts dark — no logging until someone adds it. We just spent 2 days retrofitting logging across 9 handlers and 33 hook modules. New code should start observable. I scaffold a handler from a template and logging is already there — request in, response out, errors caught, spine events emitted.

## AC
- [ ] TypeScript handler template: structured logger, request/response logging, error handling, spine event helper
- [ ] Rust hook module template: entry/exit logging, decision logging, duration_ms, module name in every line
- [ ] EJS view template: base layout with error boundary
- [ ] Templates live in chorus repo, referenced by /pull when scaffolding new code
- [ ] Kade's existing logging middleware (#1824) and handler loggers (#1806) are the pattern source

tasks.title: Demo skip for non-code cards — /demo skip or card tag exempts process cards from demo gate
tasks.description:
## Experience
I finish a chore card — cleanup, migration, doc update — and the demo gate blocks acceptance. There's nothing to demo. I shouldn't have to fake a demo for a card that has no user-visible change. Chore and docs cards should skip the demo gate.

## AC
- [ ] Demo gate checks card type — type:chore and type:swat skip demo requirement
- [ ] TDD gate checks card type — type:chore skips test requirement
- [ ] Cards with no code files in blast radius skip both gates
- [ ] Demo gate still enforces for type:new, type:enhance, type:fix

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/demo_gate.rs (card type check)
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/tdd_gate.rs (card type check)

tasks.title: Doc-catalog filters — value stream stage and domain chips
tasks.description:
## Experience

I open the doc catalog and see 147 documents across 13 groups. I want to narrow down to just the docs relevant to what I'm working on — filter by value stream stage (Capturing, Designing, Building, Proving, Reflecting) or by domain (chorus, photos, seeds, etc). Each doc shows small chips indicating its stage and domain so I can scan visually without clicking.

## AC

- [ ] Doc-catalog page shows value stream stage chip and domain chip on each document row
- [ ] Clickable filter bar with stage buttons (Capturing, Designing, Building, Proving, Reflecting) — click to toggle, multiple allowed
- [ ] Clickable filter bar with domain buttons populated from the data — click to toggle, multiple allowed
- [ ] Filters compose: selecting Building + domain:chorus shows only docs matching both
- [ ] Active filters visually highlighted; click again to deselect
- [ ] Filter state preserved in URL query params so links are shareable
- [ ] Existing search box works alongside filters (search within filtered results)
- [ ] No backend changes — classification logic runs client-side from existing API response data

## Files
- `public/doc-catalog.html` — filter bar UI
- `public/js/doc-catalog-search.js` — filter logic + chip rendering
- `src/handlers/doc-catalog.handler.ts` — may need stage/domain fields in response

tasks.title: Domain pages — every domain gets a page showing UI, API, integration, data, and child service designs
tasks.description:
## Experience
I click into the Photos domain and see everything: what pages render photos, what API endpoints serve them, what pipelines feed the data, what's in the graph, and the service designs for each service. Same for Seeds, Music, People, Chorus — every domain. I stop hunting across doc-catalog, the app, and the navbar. One page per domain, everything connected.

## AC
- [ ] Domain page template: UI section, API section, Integration section, Data section, Child Services section
- [ ] Sourced from ontology — domain references in OWL drive the page content
- [ ] Child service pages (service designs) linked from domain page
- [ ] Domain pages accessible from doc-catalog and from the mind map
- [ ] At least 3 domains populated: seeds, photos, chorus
- [ ] Page updates when ontology changes — no manual maintenance

## Design
- [Chorus Product Ontology](/docs/pm/artifacts/chorus-product-ontology.html)
- [Chorus Value Stream Deep Dive](/docs/pm/artifacts/chorus-value-stream-deep-dive.html)
- [Chorus Domain Model Assessment v3](/docs/pm/artifacts/chorus-domain-model-assessment-v3.html)

tasks.title: Chorus API disk endpoint uses df — same false positive as session-start
tasks.description:
## Experience
I check the API disk endpoint and it says 94% — same false alarm as session-start. Every surface that shows disk usage should agree with what Finder shows. Right now the API uses Container Free Space which ignores purgeable. Finder says ~48%.

## AC
- [ ] GET /api/chorus/disk uses Finder free space (osascript) instead of Container Free Space
- [ ] MonitoringService health check uses Finder free space instead of df
- [ ] Both report the same percentage as Finder
- [ ] API response includes finder_free_bytes field for transparency

## Files
- platform/api/src/server.ts (GET /api/chorus/disk ~L1808)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/services/MonitoringService.ts (~L313)

tasks.title: Clearing domain sort — most active domain at top, not alphabetical
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the Clearing and see domains listed alphabetically. The domain with 3 stale cards from two weeks ago is buried below one I just touched. The thing that needs attention is hidden by the thing that's fine. I want the Clearing to show me what matters — oldest and most stuck at the top, not alpha order.

## AC
- [ ] Domains sorted by most active first (most WIP/recent card moves)
- [ ] Within each domain, oldest cards at top — the card waiting longest surfaces first
- [ ] Stuck or forgotten cards are visually distinct from active ones
- [ ] Sort updates live as card state changes — no manual refresh
- [ ] Works across all domain views in the Clearing

## Design
- [Domain — Loom](/docs/pm/artifacts/domain-loom.html) — Cards sub-domain

## Files
- chorus/directing/clearing/public/index.html
- chorus/directing/clearing/src/ (if server-side sort)

tasks.title: Chorus domain taxonomy — decompose monolithic Chorus into sub-domains from Jeff's drawing
Session-tailer filters card lifecycle events — Clearing goes dark on pulls/accepts
tasks.description:
## Experience
I'm watching the Clearing and a role pulls a card — nothing appears. Another role accepts a card — silence. The ambient heartbeat of the team is gone. I have to switch to terminals to know what's happening. Yesterday these events showed up; today they don't.

## AC
- [ ] session-tailer.ts line 264 regex no longer filters card lifecycle events (Pulled #, Moved #, Accepted #, Demo ready:)
- [ ] Card events appear on the Clearing as ambient messages when roles pull, demo, or accept
- [ ] Spine event echoes (card.pulled, card.accepted) still filtered to avoid double-display

## Files
- directing/clearing/src/session-tailer.ts (line 264 filter regex)

tasks.title: Chorus product page — Shared Awareness with drill-down into domains and services
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open /chorus and see the Shared Awareness map — Time, Pulse, Memory, Knowledge, System Context. I click a node and drill into its detail: the service design, the API endpoints, the data, the known issues. I don't navigate away — the map is the product page, the nodes are the doors. Everything the team has built is reachable from this one surface.

## AC
- [ ] /chorus page nodes are clickable — drill into domain/service detail
- [ ] Detail view shows: UI surface, API endpoints, data stores, service designs, known issues
- [ ] Organized by Shared Awareness layers (Time, Pulse, Memory, Knowledge, System Context), not flat tables
- [ ] Each layer binds: Layer → Node → UI → API → Data
- [ ] Service design artifacts linked from each node (DEC-109)
- [ ] Chorus is a standalone product page — linked from Gathering, not nested inside it
- [ ] No separate domain page needed — /chorus IS the domain page with depth

## Design
- [Service Design — Pulse](/docs/pm/artifacts/service-design-pulse.html)
- [/nudge Sequence Diagram](/docs/pm/artifacts/sequence-nudge.html)
- [Domain — Chorus](/docs/pm/artifacts/domain-chorus.html) (current flat version — to be replaced)
- [Domain — Observability](/docs/pm/artifacts/domain-observability.html)
- [Domain — Loom](/docs/pm/artifacts/domain-loom.html)

## Context
Jeff's handwritten Shared Awareness model (2026-03-31): Time, Pulse, Memory, Knowledge, System Context. The /chorus page already has the visual architecture — nodes, clusters, connections. This card adds the drill-down depth and reorganizes by the five layers.

tasks.title: Convergence page permutation tests — comprehensive coverage for complex ICD UI
tasks.description:
## Experience
The convergence page has grown complex — domains, providers, fields, mappings, integration contracts, sections, risks, diagrams. Any graph change can silently break the render. I shouldn't discover a blank page after a Fuseki reload. The page needs comprehensive tests that exercise every permutation: domain with providers, domain without, provider with integration contract, without, fields with mappings, empty states.

## AC
- [ ] Page-level tests for convergence page covering: domain list, provider expansion, field table, mapping table, integration contract section
- [ ] Empty state tests: no domains, domain with no providers, provider with no fields
- [ ] Integration contract permutations: provider with all 4 fields, partial fields, no fields (hidden section)
- [ ] Tests run against real Fuseki graph data (not mocked) per DEC-1674
- [ ] All tests green before any future ICD TTL change ships

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/tests/ (new test file)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/handlers/icd.handler.ts
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/services/icd.service.ts

tasks.title: iPhone provider ICD is empty — built pipeline but ICD doesn't reflect it
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the convergence page, expand the iPhone provider, and it's a stub — just a status note saying 'Not yet synced.' But we built extraction scripts, inventory tooling, and a sync pipeline for 54K items. The map doesn't match the territory. If I'm making decisions about the photos domain, the ICD is lying by omission.

## AC
- [ ] iPhone provider ICD populated with actual schema from built pipeline
- [ ] Field mappings reflect what the extraction scripts produce
- [ ] Endpoints reference the actual scripts and paths used
- [ ] Integration contract matches the built pipeline (TriggeredExtraction)

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/ontology/icd-instance-photos.ttl (iPhone provider section)

tasks.title: ICD section structure must be invariant — Integration Contract as mandatory section for all providers
tasks.description:
## Experience
I scan the convergence page across providers and the sections are different — some have Integration Contract, some don't. The structure should be predictable so I can compare providers at a glance. Every provider follows the same sections in the same order. Missing data shows as empty, not hidden.

## AC
- [ ] Integration Contract is an icd:Section with fixed sectionOrder (position 1, before Purpose & Scope)
- [ ] Every provider across all 8 domain instance TTLs has the Integration Contract section
- [ ] Empty Integration Contract sections render with 'Not yet defined' placeholder, not hidden
- [ ] Section order is enforced by the ontology, not by render logic
- [ ] All existing providers migrated — ~20 providers across 8 domains

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/ontology/icd-ontology.ttl (section order constraint)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/ontology/icd-instance-*.ttl (all 8 domain instances)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/views/icd.ejs (render invariant sections)

tasks.title: Link NiFi flow diagrams to ICD provider instances — data pipeline visibility
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the convergence page and see providers with integration contracts, but I can't see the actual NiFi flow that moves data. The flow exists — I built it — but the ICD doesn't link to it. I should be able to click from a provider to its NiFi pipeline diagram.

## AC
- [ ] Providers with NiFi flows have icd:nifiFlowId linking to the flow
- [ ] Convergence page renders a 'Pipeline' link in the Integration Contract section when nifiFlowId exists
- [ ] Photos domain: Apple Photos and Google Photos Export providers linked to their NiFi flows

## Files
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/ontology/icd-ontology.ttl (nifiFlowId property)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/src/ontology/icd-instance-photos.ttl (flow links)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/views/icd.ejs (render link)

tasks.title: Convergence page filters — product and value stream step chips for ICD domains
tasks.description:
## Experience

I open the convergence page and see 8 domains listed vertically. I want to quickly filter to just the Gathering domains or just the Chorus domains. I want to see which value stream step each domain belongs to — Sowing, Growing, Practicing, Harvesting, Reflecting — so I can reason about data flow across the life loop. Small chips on each domain tile and a filter bar at the top, same pattern as doc-catalog.

## AC

- [ ] Filter bar with product chips (Gathering, Chorus) — click to toggle, composable
- [ ] Filter bar with value stream step chips (Sowing, Growing, Practicing, Harvesting, Reflecting) — from domain page metadata
- [ ] Each domain tile shows product and step chips
- [ ] Filters compose: selecting Gathering + Harvesting shows only matching domains
- [ ] Filter state preserved in URL query params
- [ ] Client-side only — classification from existing API data or domain page mappings

## Files
- views/icd.ejs — filter bar + chip rendering
- public/js/ — if filter logic extracted (optional)

tasks.title: Instrument ICD process — spine events for TTL loads, provider validation, Fuseki reload
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at the Clearing and see cards being pulled and accepted, but the ICD loads are invisible. Silas loaded TTLs into Fuseki five times today and there's no trace — no spine event, no log entry, no way to know what changed or when. If something breaks after a reload, I can't correlate.

## AC
- [ ] chorus-log.sh emits icd.loaded event when TTLs are loaded into Fuseki
- [ ] Event includes: domain, graph URI, triple count, loader role
- [ ] ICD linter runs automatically after Fuseki reload and emits icd.lint.passed or icd.lint.failed
- [ ] Events visible on Clearing as ambient messages

## Files
- platform/scripts/chorus-log.sh (new event types)
- jeff-bridwell-personal-site/scripts/icd-reload.sh (new — wraps curl + emit)

tasks.title: chorus-log.sh --level flag — classify pulse events at emit time (info/warn/critical)
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at the Clearing and see events flowing but I can't tell what's urgent. A card.pulled and a circuit_break look the same. I want critical signals to punch through and informational ones to stay ambient. The classification has to happen at the source — when the event is emitted — not at the consumer.

## AC
- [ ] chorus-log.sh accepts --level flag (info|warn|critical)
- [ ] Default level is info if not specified
- [ ] Level field included in every structured JSON event in Loki
- [ ] team-scan filters by level — critical shown prominently, info shown passively
- [ ] Clearing filters by level — critical gets highlight treatment
- [ ] Nudge delivery respects level — critical justifies osascript inject, info is persist-only

## Design
- [Service Design — Pulse](/docs/pm/artifacts/service-design-pulse.html)
- [/nudge Sequence Diagram](/docs/pm/artifacts/sequence-nudge.html)

## Context
From /chat pulse review (2026-03-31): Q2 resolved — classify at emit time, not query time. Kade: "Build the classification, not the plumbing." Silas: "chorus-log.sh --level is the right primitive."

tasks.title: team-scan shows card events filtered by pulse level
tasks.description:
## Experience
I glance at team-scan and see card events mixed in with tool calls — pulled, accepted, demo started. But they all look the same. A critical alert should punch through. A routine card.pulled should be ambient. The --level flag is on the events; team-scan just needs to read it.

## AC
- [ ] team-scan output includes recent card events from chorus.log (card.pulled, card.accepted, card.demo.started)
- [ ] Critical events shown prominently (prefixed or highlighted)
- [ ] Info events shown passively (one-line, after tool activity)
- [ ] Events read the level field from chorus.log JSON

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/shim.rs (team-scan generation)

tasks.title: Clearing highlights critical pulse events — visual distinction by level
tasks.description:
## Experience

I'm watching the Clearing stream and a critical alert fires — service down, circuit break — but it looks the same as a routine card move. I can't tell what's urgent without reading every message. Critical events should jump out visually so I know instantly when something needs attention.

## AC

- [ ] Messages with level=critical get a red left border and bold treatment in the Clearing stream
- [ ] Messages with level=warn get an orange left border
- [ ] Messages with level=info (default) render normally — no visual change
- [ ] Level parsed from chorus.log JSON events displayed in the stream
- [ ] Works in both the unified stream and role-specific fold views

## Files
- chorus/directing/clearing/public/index.html (message rendering)

tasks.title: nudge.sh respects pulse level — critical=osascript inject, info=persist-only
tasks.description:
## Experience
I send a nudge and it always does the same thing — osascript inject regardless of urgency. A routine 'card pulled' notification steals Jeff's focus the same way a 'service down' alert does. The nudge system should respect the pulse level: critical = osascript inject (justified focus steal), info = persist only (ambient, drain on next prompt).

## AC
- [ ] Rust nudge subcommand in chorus-hooks: chorus-hook-shim nudge <target> '<message>' --level <level> [--from <sender>]
- [ ] Level determines delivery: critical = osascript inject + persist, warn = persist + stderr hint, info = persist only
- [ ] Persist writes to messaging tier SQLite (direct, no curl)
- [ ] nudge.sh becomes a thin wrapper calling the Rust binary
- [ ] DEC-107 still holds: persist AND deliver on every nudge — level controls the 'deliver' method

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/nudge.rs (existing — enhance with level support)
- platform/scripts/nudge.sh (thin wrapper)

tasks.title: Two-layer heartbeat watchdog — launchd watches agents, Bedroom SSH watches Library
tasks.description:
## Experience
A LaunchAgent dies and nobody notices until the next session boot. The daily review agents were exit 127 for two days before Wren caught it. I need two layers: launchd auto-restarts dead agents, and Bedroom cross-checks Library health so a machine-level failure gets caught too.

## AC
- [ ] Critical LaunchAgents have KeepAlive=true (auto-restart on crash)
- [ ] Heartbeat agent emits a pulse event every 5 minutes via chorus-log.sh
- [ ] Bedroom SSH health probe checks Library services hourly, alerts on failure
- [ ] Alert emits as --level=critical nudge to Jeff when a monitored service is down

## Files
- Library/LaunchAgents/com.chorus.heartbeat.plist (new)
- platform/scripts/heartbeat.sh (new)
- platform/scripts/library-health-probe.sh (new — runs on Bedroom)

tasks.title: Pulse calibration — 1 week of level data, define normal cadence per signal type
ICD version contract — structural schema per version, validation gate
tasks.description:
## Experience

I look at the convergence page and see Photos at v3 and Documents at v2. What does that mean? Right now nothing — version is just a number someone bumped. I need version to be a contract: v3 means these sections, these required fields, this structure. If a domain claims v3 but is missing Integration Contract, that's a lint failure.

## AC

- [ ] Define what each ICD version level requires structurally (v1=minimal, v2=fields+mappings, v3=integration contract+full sections)
- [ ] ICD lint validates that a domain's claimed version matches its actual structure
- [ ] Domains that don't meet their claimed version level are flagged as violations
- [ ] Version definition lives in the ontology (icd:VersionSpec class), not in code
- [ ] Convergence page shows version badge as pass/fail, not just a number

tasks.title: ICD events through NiFi — spine events as integration bus for TTL loads, lint, reload
tasks.description:
## Experience
NiFi flows run silently on Bedroom. When a TTL load fails or a lint catches bad triples, nothing appears in Loki or the spine. Jeff finds out when data is wrong, not when the flow broke. Every NiFi flow should emit structured logs that land in the same observability stack as LaunchAgents and hooks.

## AC
- [ ] NiFi logging standard: every flow emits structured JSON on load, lint, and reload events
- [ ] Events include: flow name, domain, record count, duration, success/failure, error detail
- [ ] Logs land in Loki via the same path as LaunchAgent logs
- [ ] Pattern documented so new flows conform automatically

tasks.title: Context synthesis gate must check git history — require git log on edited file before fix-context writes
tasks.description:
## Experience
I fix seed.handler.ts for the 18th time and nobody reads the git history first. The same mistakes repeat because nobody checks what was tried before. The synthesis gate catches missing Chorus search, but doesn't check if you read the commit history of the file you're about to edit. Fix cards should require git log evidence.

## AC
- [ ] Synthesis gate checks for git log/blame on the target file in session history (fix cards only)
- [ ] Gate reads card type label (type:fix) — only enforces git history for fix/swat cards
- [ ] Gate message tells the role to run git log on the specific file
- [ ] Existing Chorus search + synthesis checks still apply (additive, not replacement)

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/memory_gate.rs (add git history check)

tasks.title: Auto-sync ICD TTLs to Fuseki — add instance TTLs to fuseki-sync watch list
tasks.description:
## Experience

Silas edits an ICD instance TTL and forgets to reload Fuseki. The convergence page shows stale data or missing domains. I shouldn't have to manually reload — the system should watch TTL files and sync them to Fuseki on change and on startup. And the convergence page shouldn't need 25 round trips and a client-side domain map — one API call should return everything.

## AC

- [ ] fuseki-sync service watches all src/ontology/icd-instance-*.ttl files
- [ ] Changes to any ICD TTL auto-reload into urn:gathering:icd/current graph
- [ ] On app startup, all ICD TTLs are loaded if graph is empty or stale
- [ ] GET /api/convergence returns full domain taxonomy with ICD status, product, step, providers, compliance — one call
- [ ] POST /api/icd/reload endpoint for manual trigger
- [ ] Convergence page consumes single API instead of 25 round trips
- [ ] No manual Fuseki reload needed after TTL edits
- [ ] Client-side DOMAIN_META map replaced by server-side classification

## Files
- src/services/fuseki-sync.service.ts
- src/services/icd.service.ts
- src/handlers/icd.handler.ts
- src/ontology/icd-instance-*.ttl
- views/icd.ejs
- tests/integration/convergence-page.test.ts

tasks.description:
## Experience
Wren creates a service design doc and I don't know about it until she nudges me hours later. The artifact exists but no event fires, no role gets briefed. Design docs should announce themselves — when a new artifact lands, emit a pulse event and brief the relevant roles automatically.

tasks.title: Domain service — GET /api/chorus/domain/:name returns full domain view
tasks.description:

I type domain/get photos and see everything: pages, endpoints, pipelines, data, service designs, ICD providers, health. One call. The domain pages exist as static HTML — this makes them live, queryable, and usable by every surface (Clearing, /chorus drill-down, doc-catalog, team-scan).

tasks.title: Card type field — new/enhance/fix/chore/swat drives gate behavior
tasks.description:
## Experience
I create a fix card and the system automatically requires git history and log inspection before anyone edits the files. I create a new feature card and the builder can go straight to building. The card type IS the gate trigger — not keyword matching on titles, not fragile heuristics.

## AC

tasks.title: Local backup reconciliation and strategy — TM disabled, need a real plan
tasks.description:
## Experience
Time Machine was backing up locally to the same SSD with no external drive — eating 880GB in snapshots during heavy sessions. We disabled it, but now Library has no backup at all. Git covers code. Fuseki has TTL sources. But system config, LaunchAgents, photos library, and user data have no protection against drive failure.

## AC
- [ ] Inventory what needs backup protection (system config, photos library, LaunchAgents, user data)
- [ ] Evaluate options: Bedroom NAS via TM network destination, rsync to Bedroom, or iCloud
- [ ] Implement chosen strategy
- [ ] Verify restore path works
- [ ] Document in infrastructure-constraints.md

## Context
TM disabled 2026-03-31. Exclusions set for CascadeProjects, .gathering, Library/Caches. Daily snapshot thinner (com.chorus.tm-thin) still runs as safety net if TM is re-enabled.

tasks.title: Card type drives interaction pattern — fix=SWAT, new=ideation, enhance=demo, chore=direction
tasks.description:
## Experience

I pull a fix card and the system shifts to SWAT mode — tighter gates, git history required, log inspection before code edits. I pull a new feature card and it shifts to ideation mode — more room to explore, lighter gates. The card type automatically sets the interaction pattern so I don't have to think about which mode I'm in.

## AC

- [ ] When a card is pulled to WIP, detect its type: label and set interaction pattern
- [ ] fix → SWAT pattern: require git log + Loki search before edits (feed to #1903 gate)
- [ ] new → ideation pattern: own-domain passthrough, lighter gates
- [ ] enhance → demo pattern: Chorus search required before edits
- [ ] chore → direction pattern: minimal gates
- [ ] swat → bypass: skip all non-critical gates
- [ ] Pattern stored in session state so hooks can read it

## Files
- chorus/platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/state.rs (read card type, set pattern)
- chorus/platform/scripts/cards (emit pattern on pull)

tasks.title: Fix acp push race — git pull --rebase before push, no stash improvisation
tasks.description:
## Experience
I commit and push from two roles within seconds of each other. One push fails, the role improvises a stash-rebase-push recovery, and my changes vanish. I have lost work twice in 12 hours. I need commits to never silently disappear.

## AC
1. acp skill Step 4 uses git pull --rebase and git push, single step, no stash
2. If working tree is dirty at push time, acp refuses and tells role to commit first
3. Remove old git-queue.sh role-name syntax from acp skill (currently broken)
4. Prove: two roles commit within 5 seconds, both land in history, no lost changes

## Files
- platform/scripts/git-queue.sh
- .claude/skills/acp/SKILL.md

tasks.title: Standards surface + pulse instrumentation — single page of team policies with pulse measurement
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open one page and see every team standard — definition of done, code standards, process rules, communication rules, ops rules. Each one shows whether it's documented only, instrumented in a gate, or measured in pulse. The gaps are visible. Pulse checks the ones that aren't gate-enforced.

## AC
1. Single HTML standards page at /chorus/standards — lists all team policies grouped by category
2. Each standard shows status: documented | instrumented (which hook) | measured (which metric)
3. Pulse checks added for: TDD compliance, WIP limit violations, re-nudge frequency, commit-before-accept, brief response latency, Jeff touches per card
4. Standards page links to the source (DEC, ADR, feedback memory, hook file) for each standard
5. Page updates automatically from decisions.md, CLAUDE.md fragments, and hook registry

tasks.title: Clearing synthetic monitor — 60s heartbeat proves message channel alive
tasks.description:
## Experience
I open the Clearing and type a message. Sometimes it sends, sometimes it vanishes silently. I need proof every 60 seconds that the channel is alive — and an alert when it isnt.

## AC
1. LaunchAgent runs every 60 seconds, POSTs a probe message to localhost:3470/api/message
2. Verifies probe appears in GET /api/messages within 5 seconds
3. Emits spine event on success, alerts on failure
4. Socket.IO emit gets ack callback — client knows if send succeeded or failed
5. Dashboard or log shows uptime percentage over rolling 1 hour

- platform/launchagents/com.chorus.clearing-probe.plist

## AC
1. socket.emit jeff-message uses ack callback — server confirms receipt
2. If no ack within 3 seconds, client retries once then shows error indicator

## Experience
I open the Clearing from my phone on wifi, my phone on 5G, and my Mac in Chrome. All three should just work — I type my name, send a message, and see it appear. When one path breaks I want the test to tell me which one and why.

3. Steps use curl or playwright to simulate each access path — real HTTP, not mocked
4. Can run as: npx cucumber-js features/clearing-access.feature

tasks.title: Seed pipeline trust — Jeff sends it, team picks it up, always arrives, never drops #tags as seeds
tasks.description:
## Experience
I send a seed and the team picks it up with urgency and attention. I never wonder if it arrived. I never discover days later that a seed was silently dropped. When I use #wren or #silas, it routes to that role — the hashtag is correlation, not the seed itself. The content before the tag is the seed. The system treats both parts together, never the tag alone.

## AC
1. Seed delivery is confirmed — Jeff gets visible proof the seed arrived and who picked it up
2. Seeds are processed with urgency — not batched, not queued for next session, not waiting for /cs
3. #tags are NEVER treated as seeds — they are routing/correlation markers attached to the preceding content
4. A seed is always two SMS messages: content first, #tag second. Both messages together form the seed. Never process them independently.
5. End-to-end test: Jeff sends SMS seed → team receives → Jeff sees confirmation — no unit test fakes
6. When seed arrives, the receiving role acts on it in the same prompt cycle — card it, route it, or discard with reason

## Files
- platform/roles/silas/scripts/seed-probe.sh
- platform/board-client/tests/seed-pipeline-flow.test.ts
- platform/api/src/server.ts
- product-manager/docs/seed-pipeline-criteria.md

tasks.title: Hook to block /tmp usage — enforce CSC-scoped persistence
tasks.description:
## Experience
A role writes state to /tmp and it works until reboot or launchd cleanup. Then state is gone silently. /tmp is not persistence — CSC-scoped paths are.

## AC
- [ ] PreToolUse hook on Write/Edit blocks file paths under /tmp/ except allowlisted patterns (session-start-*.md, pair-*.md, claude-team-scan/)
- [ ] Hook message tells the role where to write instead — maps common /tmp patterns to CSC equivalents
- [ ] Audit existing /tmp usage in scripts and migrate to CSC paths
- [ ] Reference CSC decision (DEC-095, card 1856)

tasks.title: chorus-hooks exclusive socket bind — detect and replace orphan processes on startup
tasks.description:
## Experience
An orphan chorus-hooks process from a prior session held the unix socket. The new process started by launchctl competed with it. Jeff got stale hook responses until the orphan was manually stopped. I need the server to own the socket exclusively — detect a dead holder and take over, or fail loudly if another live process holds it.

## AC
1. On startup, chorus-hooks checks if /tmp/chorus-hooks.sock is held by another process
2. If holder PID is dead, remove stale socket and bind
3. If holder PID is alive, log error and exit with non-zero (launchd will retry)
4. Write PID file at /tmp/chorus-hooks.pid for orphan detection

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs

When I read the Gherkin I know exactly what the builder will do — and if it looks like theater, we fix it before a line is written.

## AC
1. Every card entering Building must have a design artifact: actor flow (who touches what, from where, in what order)
2. BDD scenarios derived from the actor flow — written in Jeff's language, describing the user experience not the implementation
3. PreToolUse gate blocks Edit/Write on code files if no BDD feature file exists for the active card's domain

tasks.title: Activity-day decay for chorus search — relevance scoring inspired by MIRA-OSS
tasks.description:
## Experience
Jeff searches Chorus for a topic discussed weeks ago. Results from active working sessions rank higher than results from days with low or no activity. Sessions where nobody was building naturally decay in relevance. Going on vacation does not erase memory — decay counts activity days, not calendar days.

## AC
1. Relevance scoring formula that weights results by activity-day proximity — days with spine events count, idle days do not
2. Chorus search results incorporate the decay score alongside FTS5 and semantic ranking
3. Prior art documented: MIRA-OSS sigmoid formula (access count, momentum decay, hub score, entity links, mention count)

## Context
Seed from Michael Gruen (CTO, Fund That Flip/Upright) via github.com/taylorsatula/mira-OSS. Their memory decay formula uses 5 percent per day momentum with activity-day normalization. Our index grows monotonically — no decay means old noise ranks alongside recent signal.

tasks.title: role-state must emit spine event on every state transition — Clearing tiles go stale
tasks.description:
## Experience
I look at the Clearing and the tiles show stale card numbers. Silas finished #2003 ten minutes ago but the tile still says #2000. The state changed but the Clearing never heard about it — because role-state writes a file but doesn't emit to the spine.

## AC
- [ ] role-state binary emits role.state.changed to chorus.log on every state transition (building, idle, blocked, observing)
- [ ] Clearing tailer picks up the event and updates tile card number within 5s
- [ ] No silent state changes — if the state file updates, the spine event fires
- [ ] Integration test: set role-state, verify role.state.changed in chorus.log, verify Clearing tile updates via Socket.IO

Root cause: role-state updates /tmp/role-state-<role> but never writes to chorus.log, breaking the Clearing tile subscription chain.

## Files
- platform/scripts/role-state
- directing/clearing/src/tailer.ts

tasks.title: Permutation coverage gate — actor diagram states drive test scenario count
tasks.description:

## Context

tasks.title: Team awareness actor diagram + BDD — spine and pulse detect stalled roles before Jeff does
tasks.description:
## Experience
