## AC
1. Actor diagram for team awareness flow: role action → spine event → Pulse aggregation → team-scan → other roles aware
2. Actor diagram for stall detection: no spine events for N minutes → Pulse flags → observing role nudges → escalation if no response
3. BDD scenarios: role goes silent → system detects → nudge sent → escalation at 3 minutes
4. Domain page section added to Pulse or Spine (whichever owns detection)
5. Linked from domain-interactions.html

## Context
April 1 session: both Kade and Silas stalled for 20+ minutes on #1956. Wren nudged at 8 minutes but only because Jeff was watching. The system should catch this automatically. DEC-1571 attention contract says roles poll each other — this is the infrastructure that makes that possible.

3. Steps test real role-state files and real nudge delivery
4. Scenario: role resumes after nudge, watchdog resets

tasks.title: Memory gate missing search-order enforcement — only gates Edit/Write, not Grep/Bash searches
tasks.description:
## Experience
I grep the filesystem without checking Chorus first. The memory gate doesn't block me because it only fires on Edit/Write (line 217 of memory_gate.rs). Grep and Bash searches pass through unchecked.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PreToolUse gate intercepts Grep and Bash search commands (grep, rg, find on session/card files)
- [ ] Gate blocks if no prior chorus-query.sh call detected in the session
- [ ] Gate allows if Chorus was already queried this session
- [ ] Tests cover: grep without Chorus (blocked), grep after Chorus (allowed), non-search Bash (allowed)

tasks.title: Continuous awareness gate — surface unresolved state on every prompt cycle, not just boot
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every prompt cycle, the role sees active system state AND relevant context from prior sessions. Semantic + keyword search runs automatically against the Chorus index on every prompt. No role has to remember to check — the system brings the context to them. The compound loop: every interaction makes the system smarter.

## AC
- [ ] UserPromptSubmit hook extracts terms from Jeff's message, runs semantic + keyword search against Chorus index
- [ ] Results injected into role context automatically — role sees relevant prior discussions, decisions, and patterns
- [ ] PostToolUse hook checks active state on every tool call (ops alerts, pending seeds, pipeline health, unresolved flags)
- [ ] Surfaces warnings via stderr injection — not blocking, surfacing
- [ ] State checks are fast (sub-100ms) — cached, not live API calls on every prompt
- [ ] Tests: semantic search returns relevant results for known topics; keyword search returns exact matches; both inject on every prompt
- [ ] Replaces boot-only triage — this IS the compound loop closer

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/context_inject.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/main.rs
- platform/scripts/test-continuous-awareness.sh

tasks.title: Four-layer compound search — Chorus + logs + git + code on every search, all errors not warns
tasks.description:
## Experience
Every search I run gets context from all four layers automatically: (1) Chorus — what has the team said about this, (2) Logs — what is the system reporting right now, (3) Git — what changed and when, (4) Code — what currently exists. Each layer compounds on the others. All must run. Every time. No exceptions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search gate runs all four layers on every Grep/Glob: Chorus query, log check, git history, then allow the code search
- [ ] Each layer's results are injected into the deny message — the role sees all four before retrying
- [ ] All failures are errors, not warns — dropped seeds, missing context, stale state
- [ ] Retry after deny goes through clean (existing session-scoped dedupe)
- [ ] Sub-500ms total for all four layers (Chorus SQLite, log tail, git log are all local)
- [ ] Tests verify all four layers appear in deny message content

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/search_hierarchy.rs
- platform/tests/features/gates/memory_first.feature

tasks.title: LaunchAgent lifecycle tool — app-state.sh for native services
tasks.description:
LaunchAgent lifecycle is currently manual — roles kill PIDs, forget to use launchctl, leave orphans holding ports. Jeff has said repeatedly: no raw process killing. app-state.sh handles Docker containers but native LaunchAgents have no equivalent tool.

## Experience
When Jeff reboots a role or restarts a service, it should be one command that just works — like app-state.sh does for Docker. Today roles guess at launchctl syntax, leave orphan processes, and Jeff discovers hung ports. The experience: agent-state.sh restart chorus-hooks and it's done. No orphans, no port conflicts, no manual PID hunting.

## AC
- [ ] Script manages LaunchAgent start/stop/restart/status for all com.chorus.* and com.gathering.* agents
- [ ] Detects and kills orphan processes holding ports (ppid=1, not managed by launchd)
- [ ] Single command: agent-state.sh {start|stop|restart|status|orphans} [service-name]
- [ ] Hook updated to allow agent-state.sh as approved kill path

- platform/scripts/agent-state.sh

- platform/tests/features/gates/agent_lifecycle.feature

- [ ] Retire com.gathering.* duplicates (context-cache, cruft-scan, perf-baseline) — one namespace owns each job

tasks.title: Operational deploy governance — runbook, pre/post checks, rollback discipline
tasks.description:
Deploys are ungoverned. Roles run app-state.sh deploy without pre-checks, post-verification, or rollback plans. Today's seed failures came from deploys that broke write paths while reads passed. No deploy checklist, no smoke test after deploy, no ownership of deploy verification.

## Experience
When a role deploys, Jeff should never discover the breakage. The deploy process itself should catch it — pre-deploy smoke, post-deploy verification, automatic rollback if health degrades. One command, one runbook, zero surprises.

## AC
- [ ] Operational runbook document covering: deploy sequence, pre-checks, post-verification, rollback
- [ ] Pre-deploy gate: smoke tests must pass before app-state.sh deploy proceeds
- [ ] Post-deploy verification: health + functional checks run automatically after deploy
- [ ] Rollback procedure documented and tested
- [ ] All roles follow the same deploy process — no ad-hoc deploys

tasks.title: Cards CLI single-pass validation — all fields validated at once, infer defaults from context
tasks.description:
Cards CLI single-pass validation with inference — stop the 5-retry card creation loop.

AC:
1. `cards add` validates ALL required fields in one pass and reports every missing field in a single error message, not one at a time.
2. Inference: sequence inferred from domain when unambiguous (e.g. domain:chorus + context → sequence:framework). Chunk inferred from domain. Type inferred from title verb (fix→fix, add→new, update→enhance).
3. `--quick` bypasses desc/experience/blast-radius gates for unplanned work (already exists, verify it works).
4. Experience section warning remains a warning, not a blocker — it's a PM concern, not a creation gate.

tasks.title: role-state preserves stale card ID on non-building transitions — waiting/idle should clear card field
tasks.description:
## Problem
When `role-state` transitions to `waiting` or `idle`, the `card=` field from the previous `building` state persists. Clearing tiles show stale card IDs for roles that have finished their work.

## Root Cause
The `role-state` Rust binary only sets `card=` when explicitly passed. Transitions like `role-state silas waiting` preserve the previous card value instead of clearing it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `role-state <role> waiting` clears the card field (sets to null/empty)
- [ ] `role-state <role> idle` clears the card field
- [ ] `role-state <role> building card=<id>` still sets the card field correctly
- [ ] Clearing tiles show no card ID for waiting/idle roles

## Experience
Jeff sees the Clearing tiles and each role shows its actual state — building with a card number, or waiting/idle with no card. No stale card IDs lingering from previous work.

I rebuild chorus-hooks and nudges keep working — no manual step needed. The post-build script handles Accessibility re-registration automatically, or at minimum warns me that the permission needs re-toggling.

## AC
- [ ] After `cargo build --release`, chorus-hook-shim retains or re-acquires Accessibility permission
- [ ] Nudge --force inject works immediately after rebuild without manual intervention
- [ ] If auto-registration is not possible (SIP blocks TCC writes), emit a clear warning with the exact fix step

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/build.rs
- scripts/post-build-hook.sh

tasks.title: CLI test card
CLI test card seq
Daily signal integrity scan — codebase weather, trust verification, signal-to-noise
tasks.description:
Scheduled autonomous agent run (cron, 6am ET daily). Not a status dashboard — a developing judgment about system health.

## What it does
1. Signal integrity — smoke tests that exercise actual paths. Did nudge deliver? Did seed pipeline write to Fuseki? Does board CLI match Vikunja? Can chorus search find yesterdays index?
2. Codebase weather — git log analysis: what shifted overnight, which areas got touched by multiple roles, whats accumulating
3. Trust verification — are health checks lying? The lobotomy pattern: system says green but is broken. End-to-end probes, not process checks
4. Log signal-to-noise — new error fingerprints vs retry noise, gate blocks that are real vs false positives, pattern formation
5. Drift detection — stale handoffs, uncommitted files accumulating, WIP without commits in 24h
6. Reactive ratio check — are yesterdays completed cards still holding? Track origin:reactive vs origin:reflective trend. If reactive percentage is climbing, surface it as the top signal. Building features while reactive ratio rises = building on sand.

## Learning over time
The scan gets smarter daily. Builds a model of normal — known noise patterns get suppressed, new signals get surfaced. Not static checks — developing situational awareness.

## AC
- [ ] Cron fires at 6am ET autonomously, no Jeff interaction
- [ ] Exercises real paths (nudge delivery, Fuseki query, board CLI, chorus search) not just process status
- [ ] Produces weather report: what shifted, whats drifting, what needs attention
- [ ] Distinguishes signal from noise — known patterns suppressed, new patterns surfaced
- [ ] Checks yesterdays completed cards still hold — no regressions post-demo
- [ ] Surfaces reactive ratio trend (daily delta from Wren origin analysis)
- [ ] Output to Bridge + /tmp/daily-signal-YYYY-MM-DD.md
- [ ] Composes existing checks (cruft scan, health-hourly) where useful, adds trust verification layer

tasks.title: Weekly climate report — reactive ratio, card durability, debt trajectory, coverage slopes
tasks.description:
Scheduled autonomous agent run (cron, Sunday 5am ET weekly). The climate report — trends, not snapshots.

## What it does
1. Reactive ratio by layer — pull the full origin:reactive vs origin:reflective breakdown per dependency layer (Apps, Infrastructure, Protocol, Loom, etc). Is each layer getting more or less stable? Compare to prior week.
2. Test coverage direction — not a number, a slope. Are we adding tests or losing them? Which modules have coverage gaps growing?
3. Tech debt trajectory — dead code accumulating, TODOs aging, file sizes growing (another 1776-line shim forming?). Debt growing or shrinking.
4. Card durability — cards shipped this week that generated fix cards. The "shipped and held" vs "shipped and broke" ratio. Direct measure of proving gate quality.
5. Error rate slopes — are error fingerprints trending up or down across the week? New recurring patterns?
6. Codebase hotspots — files touched by 3+ commits this week. Churn = instability signal.
7. Role work distribution — is one role carrying disproportionate reactive load? Early warning for burnout patterns.

## The weekly question
Is the system getting better or worse? Not today — over the arc of a week. The daily is weather. This is climate.

## AC
- [ ] Cron fires Sunday 5am ET autonomously
- [ ] Reactive ratio by layer with week-over-week delta (from Wren origin analysis data)
- [ ] Card durability metric — shipped cards that held vs generated follow-up fixes
- [ ] Test coverage slope per module
- [ ] Tech debt trajectory with specific hotspots named
- [ ] Codebase churn map — files with highest commit frequency
- [ ] Produces HTML report (Jeff engages with HTML, not markdown logs)
- [ ] Output to Bridge + /tmp/weekly-climate-YYYY-MM-DD.html
- [ ] Includes week-over-week comparison — this week vs last, trend arrows

tasks.title: Watchdog fires on active roles — check team-scan activity, not just role-state
tasks.description:
## Experience
The watchdog tells me a role is stale when I can see them actively working. It checks role-state declarations but roles forget to declare. Team-scan shows live tool calls — that's the real signal. I shouldn't have to tell the system what I can see.

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/ops_awareness.rs
- platform/scripts/deep-health.sh

## AC
- [ ] Watchdog checks team-scan JSONL for recent tool calls, not just role-state file timestamp
- [ ] Role with tool activity in last 5 minutes is never reported as stale
- [ ] Role with no tool activity AND no role-state update in 45 minutes triggers stale alert
- [ ] False positive rate drops to zero for actively building roles

tasks.title: Consolidate 5 search hooks into 1 — one Chorus search per prompt, shared state, delete redundant paths
tasks.description:
## Experience
When I hit enter, I expect Chorus to surface what the team already knows. Instead, roles ignore the injected context and fall back to git log. I had to demand Wren search Chorus. The system makes the wrong thing easy. One search, one path.

## Files
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/context_inject.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/hooks/search_hierarchy.rs
- platform/services/chorus-hooks/src/state.rs

## AC
- [ ] context_inject stores search results in AppState after running on UserPromptSubmit
- [ ] search_hierarchy reads from AppState instead of querying Chorus DB independently
- [ ] memory_gate, memory_first, log_first_gate recognize when context_inject already ran (check AppState, not session JSONL)
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] No new hooks added

## What we learned (after reading all 5 files)
- Only 2 hooks actually search Chorus: context_inject and search_hierarchy
- memory_gate, memory_first, log_first_gate are behavior gates — they check session JSONL for evidence, not Chorus
- The real redundancy is 2 independent Chorus queries, not 5
- The behavior gates have a separate problem: they scan JSONL for markers but don't know context_inject already provided results

tasks.title: Fix daily review and alert routing — one summary to Bridge, alerts to roles, no overnight spam
tasks.description:
## Experience
I wake up, check the Bridge on my phone, and see 7 red tunnel-down alerts that fired overnight while nobody could act. Then two quality messages with different test counts. Then ops saying all healthy — while the tunnel is down. I can't tell what's real. The morning signal should be one clear message that tells me the truth.

## Context
Jeff wakes up to: 7 duplicate tunnel-down alerts on Bridge (nobody can act overnight), two separate quality review messages with different test counts, and ops review saying healthy while tunnel is down.

## AC
1. Daily review posts ONE consolidated message to Bridge — ops health + test results in a single message
2. Ops review checks alert state — if alerts fired since last review, the summary includes them (no all healthy while tunnel is down)
3. Quality review aggregates all test suites into one result line (total failed / total passed / total), not separate messages per suite
4. Infrastructure alerts (tunnel-down, API crash) nudge the owning role directly — not the Bridge
5. Alerts suppress after first fire until the condition resolves — no repeat alerts to the Bridge overnight
6. Verify: run the review, confirm one Bridge message, confirm alert routing goes to role

tasks.title: Async migration — eliminate event loop blocking in hot-path handlers
tasks.description:
## Problem

300+ synchronous blocking calls across 95 files in the Gathering app. This card is the parent tracker. Work split into focused cards below.

## Split Cards

- #TBD-A: photo.handler async migration (35 sync calls, includes execSync sips — worst offender, 10s blocks)
- #TBD-B: pod-storage middleware async migration (9 sync calls — runs on EVERY pod request)
- #TBD-C: knowledge-graph + document handler async migration (31 sync calls — complex directory traversal)
- #TBD-D: team.handler + service-layer async migration (5+3 sync calls — execSync node scripts)
- #TBD-E: event loop lag detection sensor (proves the fixes work)

## Reference

Silas's Chorus API fix (commit 9afc12bf, card #2241): const execAsync = promisify(exec)

## Long Tail

70+ additional files with sync calls in background harvesters and lower-traffic handlers. Separate P2 after hot path is clean.

tasks.title: Async: photo.handler — replace 35 sync calls including execSync sips (10s event loop blocks)
tasks.description:
## Problem

photo.handler.ts has 35 synchronous calls in request handlers. The worst: execSync('sips -s format jpeg -Z 800 ...') at line 573 blocks the entire Node event loop for up to 10 seconds during HEIC-to-JPEG conversion. Every other request waits.

## Files
- src/handlers/photo.handler.ts

## Experience
When I'm browsing photos — especially recent ones from my iPhone in HEIC format — the whole app freezes. Not just the photo page. Everything. Other tabs, other people browsing, all waiting for one image conversion to finish. It's the worst choppiness I feel in the app.

## AC

- [ ] AC1: Replace execSync('sips ...') in serveFullres() with async child process (execFileAsync or worker thread)
- [ ] AC2: Replace all readFileSync calls (index, manifests, photo data) with fs.promises.readFile
- [ ] AC3: Replace all existsSync calls with fs.promises.access wrapped in try/catch
- [ ] AC4: Replace writeFileSync, mkdirSync, readdirSync with fs.promises equivalents
- [ ] AC5: Zero sync calls remaining in photo.handler.ts request paths
- [ ] AC6: All existing photo tests green. No regressions.

## Reference

Pattern: const execAsync = promisify(exec) — see Silas commit 9afc12bf (#2241)
Proven in #2250 (pod-storage middleware) — same session.

tasks.title: Async: pod-storage middleware — replace 9 sync calls on every pod request
tasks.description:
## Problem

pod-storage.middleware.ts has 9 synchronous file operations (existsSync, mkdirSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, unlinkSync) that execute on EVERY pod storage request. This middleware is in the critical path for all pod reads and writes.

## Files
- src/middleware/pod-storage.middleware.ts

## Experience
When I'm browsing my pod data — books, photos, notes — the app hesitates. Page loads stutter. It's small but cumulative. Every pod read and write waits for synchronous file I/O to complete before anything else can happen.

## AC

- [ ] AC1: Convert middleware to async — all fs operations use fs.promises
- [ ] AC2: Zero sync calls in pod-storage.middleware.ts
- [ ] AC3: All existing pod/storage tests green. No regressions.

## Reference

Pattern: const execAsync = promisify(exec), fs.promises.* — see Silas commit 9afc12bf (#2241)

tasks.title: Async: knowledge-graph + document handlers — replace 31 sync calls in directory traversal
tasks.description:
## Problem

knowledge-graph.handler.ts (17 sync calls) and document.handler.ts (14 sync calls) both do complex directory traversal and cache I/O synchronously per request. Graph construction and doc catalog requests freeze the event loop.

## AC

- [ ] AC1: knowledge-graph.handler — replace readdirSync, readFileSync, statSync with fs.promises. Zero sync calls in request path.
- [ ] AC2: document.handler — replace all sync cache reads/writes with fs.promises. Zero sync calls in request path.
- [ ] AC3: All existing knowledge-graph and document tests green. No regressions.

tasks.title: Async: team.handler + cards.service — replace execSync node script calls
tasks.description:
## Problem

team.handler.ts has execSync('node script.js') at lines 177 and 198 that block the event loop for entire script execution. cards.service.ts has 3 additional execSync calls for board operations.

## AC

- [ ] AC1: team.handler — replace execSync with async child process for metric script calls
- [ ] AC2: cards.service — replace execSync with promisify(exec) for board CLI calls
- [ ] AC3: Zero sync calls in team.handler.ts and cards.service.ts request paths
- [ ] AC4: All existing team/cards tests green. No regressions.

tasks.title: Event loop lag detection — log warnings when blocked >100ms
tasks.description:
## Problem

No sensor exists to detect event loop blocking. The async migration cards fix the cause — this card adds the sensor that proves they worked and catches future regressions.

## AC

- [ ] AC1: Add event loop lag monitor (monitorEventLoopDelay or setInterval drift detection)
- [ ] AC2: Log structured warning when event loop blocked >100ms with stack context
- [ ] AC3: Emit spine event on sustained lag (>500ms) for Loki alerting
- [ ] AC4: Verify sensor fires against a synthetic blocking call in tests, stays silent after async migration

tasks.title: Fix /pull skill — role-state.sh path broken, state never declared on pull
tasks.description:

I can't see what any role is doing. Team-scan shows stale state from yesterday for every role. When I ask what's happening, the system has no answer because state declarations silently fail on every pull, pair, and gemba.

## Files

- platform/skills/pair/SKILL.md

- platform/skills/gemba-tick/SKILL.md

2. /pull declares building state after moving card to WIP
3. /pair declares building state on pair start and waiting on pair end
4. /gemba declares observing state on start and waiting on exit
5. Verified: zero remaining role-state.sh references in any skill

tasks.title: Bridge message mutation — Jeff's question delivered as acceptance directive to role
tasks.description:
## Experience
I typed 'did we do 2246?' on the Bridge. Silas received something that looked like an acceptance directive and ran /acp. My question got turned into an action. I can't trust the Bridge to deliver what I actually said.

## AC
1. Bridge delivers Jeff's exact text to role terminals — no translation, no reformatting
2. Reproduce: send a question via Bridge, verify the role receives the identical text
3. Root cause identified and fixed

tasks.title: Async: MonitoringService — cache disk checks, eliminate execSync from /health path
tasks.description:
## Experience
The app freezes 2-22 seconds multiple times per day. I see pages hang, requests timeout, and event loop lag alerts fire. The monitoring system that checks app health is itself causing the freezes — every 15 seconds, Prometheus hits /health, which spawns two synchronous subprocesses (diskutil + osascript) and does 2 synchronous filesystem checks. That's 136K blocking operations per day from the health check alone.

## Files
- src/services/MonitoringService.ts (primary — 4 hot-path sync calls)
- tests/unit/services/monitoring.service.test.ts (add /health endpoint tests)

## AC
1. execSync('diskutil info /') replaced with cached async — disk usage cached 60 seconds, not checked every request
2. execSync('osascript') for Finder free space replaced with cached async — same 60-second cache
3. fs.existsSync(podsDir) cached on startup — pod directory doesn't move
4. fs.existsSync(photosDb) cached on startup — same
5. /health endpoint responds in <50ms under load (currently 150-3000ms due to subprocess spawns)
6. Event loop lag events in Loki drop measurably after deploy (query last 24h before vs after)
7. Tests cover /health response time and cache behavior — currently zero /health tests exist
8. Git version resolution already cached (no change needed, verify preserved)
